abstract: This paper introduces V2A-DPO, a novel Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) framework tailored for flow-based video-to-audio generation (V2A) models, incorporating key adaptations to effectively align generated audio with human preferences. Our approach incorporates three core innovations: (1) AudioScore—a comprehensive human preference-aligned scoring system for assessing semantic consistency, temporal alignment, and perceptual quality of synthesized audio; (2) an automated AudioScore-driven pipeline for generating large-scale preference pair data for DPO optimization; (3) a curriculum learning-empowered DPO optimization strategy specifically tailored for flow-based generative models. Experiments on benchmark VGGSound dataset demonstrate that human-preference aligned Frieren and MMAudio using V2A-DPO outperform their counterparts optimized using Denoising Diffusion Policy Optimization (DDPO) as well as pre-trained baselines. Furthermore, our DPO-optimized MMAudio achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple metrics, surpassing published V2A models. Our demos and code are available at https://nolanchan23.github.io/V2A-DPO/.

title: LLM4RIM: Leveraging Large Language Model for Radar-Based in-Vehicle Monitoring
authors: Chong Rao, Jiahang Guo, Xikang Jiang, Lei Li, Lin Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The growing need for non-invasive in-vehicle monitoring in the intelligent cabin underscores the potential of radar sensing. However, existing radar-based solutions often rely on task-specific approaches, lacking a unified framework across multiple downstream tasks. In this work, LLM4RIM, a large language model (LLM) multi-task fine-tuning framework is proposed for radar-based in-vehicle monitoring (RIM) tasks, including occupancy detection, drowsiness detection, gesture recognition, and vital sign monitoring. By utilizing the Mixture of Experts with Low-Rank Adaptation (MoE-LoRA) approach for fine-tuning, the framework leverages the cross-modal learning capabilities of LLMs to enable efficient multitask learning. Experiments conducted on four task-specific datasets demonstrate an average accuracy of 93.20%, outperforming the state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods by 13.48%. Our code is released at https://github.com/bupt-uwb/LLM4RIM.

title: ActiveParam: Selective Parameterization for Efficient and Robust Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Siqiang Li, Xifan Liu, Peng Peng, Shouqiang Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances Large Language Models (LLMs) by incorporating external knowledge. However, parametric RAG (PRAG) methods often suffer from redundancy and noise sensitivity, leading to inefficiency and degraded robustness. We propose ActiveParam, a framework for selective knowledge parameterization. ActiveParam introduces a Knowledge Probing stage, where the LLM computes an ignorance score to identify knowledge gaps for each document. Only documents with high scores, indicating novel information, are parameterized through lightweight adapters. Experiments on four question-answering benchmarks and three LLMs show that ActiveParam consistently improves F1 scores by 2-5 points over baselines like Naive Combine, while simultaneously reducing the number of required parameter adapters. This work thus establishes that selective parameterization, guided by our novel ignorance score, offers a more efficient and robust path for knowledge augmentation.

title: Open-Loop and Closed-Loop Low-Frequency Repetitive TMS Inhibits Motor Activation During Online BCI Control
authors: Akhil Surapaneni, Hussein Alawieh, Minsu Zhang, Michael Solomon, Satyam Kumar, Uneeb Sajjad, John S. Kuo, José del R. Millán
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Maladaptive plasticity is a counterproductive neural reorganization in response to injury that hinders functional recovery. This may be corrected by inducing further plasticity in nodes of the dysfunctional network. Repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) can focally alter cortical excitability. However, its effect on functional activation patterns is understudied, especially when activation patterns, such as the maladaptive ones, are reinforced. As a model case in healthy subjects, we investigate the effect of low-frequency rTMS on individualized, multivariate patterns of motor cortex activation decoded and reinforced by a brain-computer interface (BCI) with feedback. Low-frequency rTMS, delivered in open-loop, shows a trend towards inhibiting motor activation. But its effect is abrogated with continuous motor imagery BCI feedback. We developed a novel closed-loop BCI-triggered rTMS system that delivers low-frequency stimulation when maladaptive patterns are decoded. We demonstrate that closed-loop stimulation can inhibit motor activation during continuous motor imagery BCI control. Our results support the use of rTMS, open and closed-loop, for correcting maladaptive plastic changes underlying central nervous system disorders.

title: Variational Bayesian Filtering Using Gaussian Mixtures
authors: Felix Gustafsson, Roland Hostettler
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper introduces a variational Bayes filtering framework that employs Gaussian mixture models as the variational distribution to approximate complex and multimodal posterior distributions in state estimation problems. Unlike traditional Kalman-based filters or particle filters, the proposed method enables parametric estimation of non-Gaussian posteriors. The proposed approach optimizes the evidence lower bound using Monte Carlo approximations and the reparameterization trick. Experimental results on a nonlinear toy example and a stochastic oscillator demonstrate that the proposed filter significantly improves posterior approximation and reduces Kullback–Leibler divergence between the estimate and the true posterior compared to single-Gaussian variational Bayes and Unscented Kalman Filters, achieving performance comparable to dense grid approximations.

title: Self-Attention Decomposition for Training Free Diffusion Editing
authors: Tharun Anand, Mohammad Hassan Vali, Arno Solin, Green Rosh, B H Pawan Prasad
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Diffusion models achieve remarkable fidelity in image synthesis, yet precise control over their outputs for targeted editing remains challenging. A key step toward controllability is to identify interpretable directions in the model’s latent representations that correspond to semantic attributes. Existing approaches for finding interpretable directions typically rely on sampling large sets of images or training auxiliary networks, which limits efficiency. We propose an analytical method that derives semantic editing directions directly from the pretrained parameters of diffusion models, requiring neither additional data nor fine-tuning. Our insight is that self-attention weight matrices encode rich structural information about the data distribution learned during training. By computing the eigenvectors of these weight matrices, we obtain robust and interpretable editing directions. Experiments demonstrate that our method produces high-quality edits across multiple datasets while reducing editing time by 60% over current benchmarks.

title: Beat and Downbeat Detection: A Reformulated Approach
authors: James Bolt, Johan Pauwels, George Fazekas
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We propose a novel beat and downbeat detection model that departs from standard classification-based approaches, addressing their limitations and encouraging exploration of alternatives. Our approach leverages beat and downbeat phase information by computing phase differences between all time frames of a song, forming a Phase Difference Matrix (PDM) that couples frames globally and induces periodicity. We predict the PDM using a Kolmogorov-Arnold Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) with mean absolute error loss. We introduce this model as the Kolmogorov-Arnold Phase TCN with Regression Estimation, KAPTURE. Unlike previous approaches that modify the classification loss to address class imbalance, our method requires no such adaptation, simplifying training and improving generality. Experimental results show that KAPTURE achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art systems whilst reducing model parameters. We highlight the model’s potential through an oracle peak-picking experiment, indicating scope for refinement. This work aims to inspire new directions in beat and downbeat detection beyond classification frameworks.

title: Understanding Textual Capability Degradation in Speech LLMS via Parameter Importance Analysis
authors: Chao Wang, Rui-Chen Zheng, Yang Ai, Zhen-Hua Ling
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The integration of speech into Large Language Models (LLMs) has substantially expanded their capabilities, but often at the cost of weakening their core textual competence. This degradation limits the ability of speech-enabled LLMs to fully exploit their pre-trained text-based knowledge. In this work, we analyze the underlying mechanisms of this issue through a focused study of the widely used encoder-adaptor paradigm. We propose an analytical framework based on parameter importance estimation, which reveals that fine-tuning for speech introduces a textual importance distribution shift: the layer-wise allocation of parameters critical to textual reasoning is disrupted. Building on this insight, we investigate two mitigation strategies: layer-wise learning rate scheduling and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), both aim to preserve the original parameter distribution. Experimental results show that both approaches better maintain textual competence than full fine-tuning, while also improving downstream spoken question answering performance. Furthermore, our analysis offers a principled explanation for the effectiveness of the proposed mitigation strategies, linking their benefits to the structural properties of textual knowledge in LLMs.

title: METASPDB: A Shared-Personalized Dual-Branch Framework for Cross-Subject EEG Emotion Recognition
authors: Hexi Li, Ling Wu, Jiacai Zhang, Fangyi Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) is a powerful tool for emotion recognition(ER), but its practical application is severely hampered by inter-subject variability. To address this challenge, we introduce MetaSPDB, a lightweight yet powerful meta-learning framework featuring a Shared-Personalized Dual-Branch architecture. Our design decouples representation learning into a shared branch for capturing universal emotional features and a personalized branch for subject-specific traits. This dual-branch structure maintains high expressive power while ensuring each component remains computationally tractable for meta-optimization. Through training, MetaSPDB learns a globally optimal initialization that can rapidly adapt to unseen subjects. Cross-subject evaluations on the SEED and SEED-IV datasets validate its efficacy, achieving accuracies of 88.36% and 81.88% in 1-shot setting, respectively. Notably, this performance is achieved with a highly efficient model of only 0.96M parameters, successfully bridging the gap between model expressiveness and meta-learning feasibility.

title: SIREN: Spatially-Informed Reconstruction of Binaural Audio with Vision
authors: Mingyeong Song, Seoyeon Ko, Junhyug Noh
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Binaural audio delivers spatial cues essential for immersion, yet most consumer videos are monaural due to capture constraints. We introduce SIREN, a visually guided mono→binaural framework that explicitly predicts left and right channels. A ViT-based encoder learns dual-head self-attention to produce a shared scene map and end-to-end L/R attention – replacing hand-crafted masks. A soft, annealed spatial prior gently biases early L/R grounding, and a two-stage, confidence-weighted waveform-domain fusion (guided by mono reconstruction and interaural phase consistency) suppresses crosstalk when aggregating multi-crop and overlapping windows. Evaluated on FAIR-Play and MUSIC-Stereo, SIREN yields consistent gains on time–frequency and phase-sensitive metrics with competitive SNR. The design is modular and generic, requires no task-specific annotations, and integrates with standard audio-visual pipelines.

title: Leveraging Point Transformer for 3D Human Mesh Reconstruction with Incomplete Point Cloud
authors: Jingyi Wu, Han Yu, Juncen Guo, Jingyu Zhang, Lang Qian, Xiaoguang Zhu, Liang Song
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Reconstructing 3D human models from point cloud is a fundamental problem in computer vision, but it remains challenging in anomalous data conditions. Existing methods often produce distorted or incomplete models because they lack the ability to robustly infer the geometry of hidden body parts. To address this, we propose a novel network with two branches that leverages pre-trained foundation models based on point transformer for collaborative full-body reconstruction, accurately recovering geometry even from anomalous inputs. The dense geometry branch, built upon a foundation model, leverages the global pose from visible parts to predict a complete, dense compactness vector field. Simultaneously, the sparse semantic branch robustly regresses a set of sparse anatomical keypoints, providing a strong structural prior for the global pose, making it robust against local anomaly. Finally, a parameterized body model is fitted by jointly optimizing its alignment with the completed dense surface and the stabilized sparse keypoints. Comparison with current leading methods highlights the superiority of our model. Especially in challenging scenarios, our solution can successfully reconstruct the human body with high precision.

title: VISA: Virtual Identity for Secure Face Anonymization
authors: Xianhan Zeng, Xiaoxiao Hu, Sheng Li, Xinpeng Zhang, Zhenxing Qian
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In this paper, we propose VISA (Virtual Identity for Secure face Anonymization), a novel framework for generating anonymized yet identifiable faces. VISA binds each face to a virtual identity, enabling reliable verification in virtual spaces without revealing the real identity. To ensure strong privacy protection, VISA enforces that anonymized faces are not only visually and statistically distinct from their originals but also different from all identities in a specified reference database. Technically, VISA builds on a pretrained Style-GAN inversion encoder and StyleGAN generator, enhanced with a lightweight latent transformer for efficient identity transformation. We further design a training strategy based on a multi-queue feature bank and tailored loss functions to balance anonymity, identifiability and utility. Experiments demonstrate that VISA achieves effective face anonymization while preserving recognition and authentication utility.

title: Doppler-Based Pseudo-Reciprocity in FDD for LEO MU-MIMO
authors: Rei Richter, Itsik Bergel, Yair Noam, Ephi Zehavi
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Channel reciprocity is useful in wireless communication since it reduces the overhead of channel-state feedback. However, true reciprocity exists only in time division duplex (TDD) systems. In this paper, we propose a novel tracking method that exploits implicit reciprocity in frequency division duplex (FDD) line-of-sight (LOS) channels, as observed in low-earth-orbit (LEO) satellite communication (SatCom). This novel approach, which we term pseudo-reciprocity, is significant for applying multiple-user multiple-input multiple-output (MU-MIMO) techniques to satellite communication systems that require channel state information at the transmitter (CSIT). We consider a LEO satellite communication system where multiple satellites communicate with a multi-antenna land terminal. In this framework, the terminal tracks downlink channel variations to estimate the corresponding uplink channels, achieving precoding performance comparable to that obtained with full CSIT. The proposed scheme requires only an initial phase of CSIT feedback when the satellite rises.

title: Few-Shot Bearing Fault Diagnosis Using Multi-Scale Feature Extraction and Attention-Based Prototype Matching
authors: Dingli Zhang, Wei He, Rong Cao, Tiantian Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Modern industrial systems rely on motors, where rolling bearings are critical yet prone to faults under harsh conditions. Accurate fault diagnosis with limited data remains a major challenge. This paper proposes an end-to-end few-shot fault diagnosis model with three key innovations. First, the Multi-scale Large-Kernel Attention Feature Extraction (MLKAFE) module captures both local and global patterns, improving fault feature representation. Second, the Spatial and Channel Attention Aggregation (SCAA) module refines multi-scale features by highlighting informative spatial and channel-wise cues. Finally, the Prototype Matching Module with Attention (PMMA) employs self- attention and cross-attention for robust similarity measurement, addressing the shortcomings of conventional Euclidean and cosine metrics. Experiments on the CWRU and PU datasets show that our model achieves higher accuracy than state-of-the-art methods, especially under extremely limited data. These results demonstrate strong generalization and diagnostic performance, making the model well suited for industrial applications with constrained data availability.

title: Inverse Rendering for High-Genus 3D Surface Meshes from Multi-View Images with Persistent Homology Priors
authors: Xiang Gao, Xinmu Wang, Yuanpeng Liu, Yue Wang, Junqi Huang, Wei Chen, Xianfeng Gu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Reconstructing 3D objects from images is inherently an ill-posed problem due to ambiguities in geometry, appearance, and topology. This paper introduces collaborative inverse rendering with persistent homology priors, a novel strategy that leverages topological constraints to resolve these ambiguities. By incorporating priors that capture critical features such as tunnel loops and handle loops, our approach directly addresses the difficulty of reconstructing high-genus surfaces. The collaboration between photometric consistency from multi-view images and homology-based guidance enables recovery of complex high-genus geometry while circumventing catastrophic failures such as collapsing tunnels or losing high-genus structure. Instead of neural networks, our method relies on gradient-based optimization within a mesh-based inverse rendering framework to highlight the role of topological priors. Experimental results show that incorporating persistent homology priors leads to lower Chamfer Distance (CD) and higher Volume IoU compared to state-of-the-art mesh-based methods, demonstrating improved geometric accuracy and robustness against topological failure.

title: Flow Intelligence: Robust Feature Matching Via Temporal Signature Correlation
authors: Jie Wang, Caoqi Wei, Chen Ye Gan, Jiangtao Wen, Yuxing Han
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Robust video feature matching, particularly across different modalities, remains a significant challenge as existing methods rely on spatial appearance features that are inconsistent in dynamic, real-world conditions. This paper introduces Flow Intelligence, a paradigm-shifting framework that completely abandons spatial appearance in favor of temporal motion patterns. Our training-free approach computes a binary Temporal Motion Signature (TMS) for each video block based on simple frame differencing. Correspondence is then established by efficiently correlating these binary signatures. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method yields a denser set of matches compared to state-of-the-art learning-based approaches, while maintaining competitive regional accuracy. More importantly, it shows exceptional robustness in challenging cross-modal (e.g., RGB-Infrared) scenarios where traditional methods completely fail. By leveraging motion as the sole source of information, Flow Intelligence enables robust, efficient, and adaptable video matching without the need for large datasets or expensive training, opening new possibilities for real-world applications.

title: TAG: Structured Temporal Audio Generation via LLM-Guided Manual Scription and Control
authors: Hanwen Zhang, Jinshen Zhang, Cong Zhang, Shuhui Wang, Wei Yang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Although text-to-audio generation has made significant progress in recent years, generating customized especially temporally structured audio from complex prompts remains challenging. Current approaches construct structured information in an independent manner, ignoring model-specific capabilities and lacking flexibility for adaptation or extension. For structured temporal generation, existing methods either require high computational overhead or demonstrate limited adaptability like refining. In this paper, we propose a two-stage framework: first utilizing Large Language Models (LLMs) to provide structured audio information while considering available capabilities of our generation model, then employing a training-free controllable generation framework with an attention control mechanism. We achieve precise temporal control via crossattention map manipulation, which can be easily integrated into various attention-based diffusion models and attention refinement applications. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms existing methods in temporal precision while preserving generation quality.

title: DiffRIM: A Diffusion-Driven Model for High Efficiency Radar Interference Mitigation
authors: Lulu Liu, Runwei Guan, Kaishen Yuan, Sheng Xu, Fei Ma, Yutao Yue
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Despite its all-weather superiority over optical sensors, the dense deployment of millimeter-wave radar in automotive applications causes severe vehicle-to-vehicle interference, degrading detection integrity and introducing safety risks. However, existing interference suppression methods are often limited by over-smoothing, we propose a Diffusion-based framework for real-time Radar Interference Mitigation (DiffRIM). Its stochastic forward process models the incremental addition of interference, and the learned reverse process conducts iterative removement, thereby significantly enhancing interpretability. We further introduce a Lightweight Autoencoder (LWAE) with Mobile Encoder and Decoder (ME/MD) modules, which extracts multi-scale spatial features through point-wise and hierarchical processing. A dual-residual (DualRes) connection mitigates gradient issues, while depthwise separable convolutions (DSC) and channel attention form an efficient spatio-temporal attention (STA) mechanism to extract sparse patterns. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate the superior mitigation and generalization performance of our approach in both synthetic and real-world datasets. To support the community, our implementation will be made publicly accessible on https://github.com/luluisthebest/DiffRim.

title: Enhancing Dialogue-Related Speech Tasks with Generated Spoken Dialogues
authors: Haitian Lu, Zhihao Bai, Yukun Liu, Xuyang Wang, Gaofeng Cheng, Yonghong Yan
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), speech language models (SLMs) have enabled more natural spoken dialogue generation. However, to make generated dialogues useful for downstream tasks, key challenges remain in ensuring consistency between text and speech as well as providing accurate utterance timestamps. Building upon the SLIDE framework, this work introduces a method for generating dual-channel spoken dialogues with aligned transcriptions and utterance timestamps, and systematically investigates their effectiveness as augmentation data. In particular, we explore how such generated dialogues can be leveraged for downstream training in a task-specific manner—either alone or in combination with real data—across automatic speech recognition, end-to-end neural diarization, voice activity detection, and overlap speech detection. Extensive experiments on the Fisher and CALLHOME corpora demonstrate that our approach yields substantial performance gains across tasks, highlighting its effectiveness and practical value for speech processing applications.

title: Alignment-Aware Unrolling Network for Multimodal MRI Reconstruction
authors: Yi Du, Tingting Wang, Ziyi Wang, Sunzhe Yang, Faming Fang, Guixu Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Deep unrolling networks are effective for multimodal MRI reconstruction but struggle to capture cross-modal relationships, limiting reference modality utilization. Moreover, patient motion may induce spatial misalignments, exacerbating the reconstruction difficulty. To address these challenges, we propose an Alignment-aware Unrolling Network (AUNet) that employs semi-implicit cross-modal modeling to capture structural correlations between target and reference images. Specifically, we utilize convolutional sparse coding (CSC) with a shared dictionary to jointly represent structural features of both modalities in the aligned latent space. Furthermore, we introduce a Bidirectional Deformable Sampler (BDS) within the unrolling stages to accurately estimate cross-modal displacement, thereby mitigating spatial misalignment and enhancing reference feature extraction. Our experimental results show that AUNet outperforms existing methods, effectively handling misalignment and improving reconstruction quality. The complete code has been made publicly available at: https://github.com/Corsilent/AUNet.

title: BHSFlow: Low-Latency Flow Estimation with Block-Wise Huber Loss and Simplified Structure
authors: Tianyi Sun, Yanze Wang, Runkai Chen, Yuedi Chen, Jun Sun
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Optical flow estimation provides motion information between two frames, acting as a key component in many visual tasks. Current methods either come with large computational complexity or suffer a severe accuracy drop to achieve real-time performance, especially when dealing with high-resolution frames. To address this trade-off, this paper proposes a low-latency recurrent pyramid network structure and a novel block-wise Huber loss applicable to all pyramid optical flow networks. By characterizing latency and FLOPs of various convolutional kernels, a fast yet effective pyramid structure is designed accordingly. Standard Huber loss mitigates the impact of small errors but risks allowing large errors to disproportionately influence the loss within a block in certain scenarios. Instead, our block-wise Huber loss first balances opposing errors within each block and selectively applies Huber loss only to the residual errors, ensuring stable optimization. Experiments show that our method achieves the best result among existing lightweight methods and demonstrates strong adaptability to frames in high resolution. Our code is available in https://github.com/cppppp/BHSFlow.

title: APPLE: Attention-Promoted Prototype Learning for Federated Cross-Modal Hashing
authors: Xing Tian, Yifan Zeng, Zihao Zhan, Yuanyuan Li, Wing W. Y. Ng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)

title: Power Consumption Reduction in ELAA-Assisted ISAC Systems
authors: Xiaomin Cao, Mohammadali Mohammadi, Hien Quoc Ngo, Michail Matthaiou
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In this paper, we consider power consumption reduction in extremely large antenna arrays (ELAAs) for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) applications. Although ELAAs are critical for achieving high-resolution near-field sensing, fully activating all antenna elements in conventional digital architectures leads to prohibitive power demands. To address this, we propose an energy-efficient subarray activation framework that selects an optimal subset of subarrays to minimize the total power consumption, subject to quality-of-service (QoS) constraints for both sensing and communication. We formulate a novel optimization problem and solve it using a successive convex approximation (SCA)-based iterative algorithm. The simulation results confirm that the proposed method significantly reduces power consumption while maintaining dual-function performance.

title: ViTex: Visual Texture Control for Multi-Track Symbolic Music Generation via Discrete Diffusion Models
authors: Xiaoyu Yi, Qi He, Gus Xia, Ziyu Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In automatic music generation, a central challenge is to design controls that enable meaningful human-machine interaction. Existing systems often rely on extrinsic inputs such as text prompts or metadata, which do not allow humans to directly shape the composition. While prior work has explored intrinsic controls such as chords or hierarchical structure, these approaches mainly address piano or vocal-accompaniment settings, leaving multitrack symbolic music largely underexplored. We identify instrumentation, the choice of instruments and their roles, as a natural dimension of control in multi-track composition, and propose ViTex, a visual representation of instrumental texture. In ViTex, color encodes instrument choice, spatial position represents pitch and time, and stroke properties capture local textures. Building on this representation, we develop a discrete diffusion model conditioned on ViTex and chord progressions to generate 8-measure multi-track symbolic music, enabling explicit texture-level control while maintaining strong unconditional generation quality. The demo page and code are avaliable at https://vitex2025.github.io/.

title: CG-DMER: Hybrid Contrastive-Generative Framework for Disentangled Multimodal ECG Representation Learning
authors: Ziwei Niu, Hao Sun, Shujun Bian, Xihong Yang, Lanfen Lin, Yuxin Liu, Yueming Jin
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Accurate interpretation of electrocardiogram (ECG) signals is crucial for diagnosing cardiovascular diseases. Recent multimodal approaches that integrate ECGs with accompanying clinical reports show strong potential, but they still face two main concerns from a modality perspective: (1) intra-modality: existing models process ECGs in a lead-agnostic manner, overlooking spatial–temporal dependencies across leads, which restricts their effectiveness in modeling fine-grained diagnostic patterns; (2) inter-modality: the existing methods directly align ECG signals with clinical reports, introducing modality-specific biases due to the free-text nature of the reports. In light of these two issues, we propose CG-DMER, a contrastive-generative framework for disentangled multimodal ECG representation learning, powered by two appealing designs: (1) Spatial-temporal masked modeling is designed to better capture fine-grained temporal dynamics and inter-lead spatial dependencies by applying masking across both spatial and temporal dimensions and reconstructing the missing information. (2) Representation disentanglement and alignment strategy is designed to further mitigate unnecessary noise and modality-specific biases by designing modality-specific and modality-shared encoders, which ensures a clearer separation between modality-invariant and modality-specific representations. Experiments on three public datasets demonstrate that CG-DMER achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse downstream tasks.

title: 2025 Urgent Speech Enhancement Challenge Multilingual P.808 Listening Tests: Approach and Results
authors: Marvin Sach, Yihui Fu, Kohei Saijo, Wangyou Zhang, Samuele Cornell, Robin Scheibler, Chenda Li, Zhaoheng Ni, Anurag Kumar, Wei Wang, Yanmin Qian, Shinji Watanabe, Tim Fingscheidt
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In speech quality estimation for speech enhancement (SE) systems, subjective listening tests so far are considered as the gold standard. This should be even more true considering the large influx of new generative or hybrid methods into the field, revealing issues of some objective metrics. Efforts such as the Interspeech 2025 URGENT Speech Enhancement Challenge also involving non-English datasets add the aspect of multilinguality to the testing procedure. In this paper, we provide updated challenge results on the full multilingual test set and surprising insights into URGENT Challenge results, questioning the reliability of (P.808) absolute category rating (ACR) subjective testing as gold standard in the age of generative AI. Particularly, it seems that for generative SE methods, subjective (ACR MOS) and objective (DNSMOS, NISQA) reference-free metrics should be accompanied by objective phone fidelity metrics to reliably detect hallucinations. We briefly recap the ITU-T P.808 crowdsourced listening test method and detail our proposed and employed localization protocol for text and audio in subjective ACR tests, that we open-source, enabling reproducible evaluation of multilingual speech processing tasks.

title: Robust Colonoscopy Polyp Tracking via Online Coreset Appearance Clustering
authors: Xiao Yang, Zirui Liu, Dongming Yu, Guangzhi Ma, Enmin Song
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In dynamic endoscopic polyp tracking, conventional appearance-based data association relies on redundant historical buffering or rigid feature fusion, limiting their adaptability to rapid viewpoint changes and artifacts. To address these limitations, we propose a novel appearance modeling framework based on online coreset clustering. Instead of a single feature or a redundant buffer, our approach maintains a compact, dynamically updated coreset of historical appearances for each trajectory, designed to preserve feature diversity. This point-to-set matching paradigm evaluates new detections against the representative subset, improving robustness to transient artifacts and viewpoint changes. The compact coreset also enables similarity computation with constant-bounded time complexity. Extensive evaluations on both an internal clinical dataset and the public SUN-SEG benchmark demonstrate competitive performance, achieving 76.3% and 98.7% MOTA respectively, with significant gains in identity preservation metrics.

title: SimToken: A Simple Baseline for Referring Audio-Visual Segmentation
authors: Dian Jin, Yanghao Zhou, Jinxing Zhou, Jiaqi Ma, Ruohao Guo, Dan Guo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Referring Audio-Visual Segmentation (Ref-AVS) aims to segment specific objects in videos based on natural language expressions involving audio, vision, and text information. This task poses significant challenges in cross-modal reasoning and fine-grained object localization. In this paper, we propose a simple framework, SimToken, that integrates a multimodal large language model (MLLM) with the Segment Anything Model (SAM). The MLLM is guided to generate a special semantic token representing the referred object. This compact token, enriched with contextual information from all modalities, acts as a prompt to guide SAM to segment objects across video frames. To further improve semantic learning, we introduce a novel target-consistent semantic alignment loss that aligns token embeddings from different expressions but referring to the same object. Experiments on the Ref-AVS benchmark demonstrate that our approach achieves superior performance compared to existing methods. Code is here.

title: Multimodal Variational Graph Network for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
authors: Yuzhi Ren, Qiang Lu, Yunfei Long, Zhenfang Zhu, Jing Meng, Hongli Pei
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis (MSA) aims to integrate heterogeneous modalities (visual, acoustic and text) for recognizing human sentiment states. Existing methods primarily use non-textual information to enhance text representation, but they still face issues of context misalignment between different modalities and from failing to capture complex dependencies. Therefore, we propose a Multimodal Variational Graph Network (MVGNet) to tackle asynchronous alignment and dependency modeling in MSA. Specifically, first, the Adaptive Cross-modal Graph Interaction Module (ACGIM) dynamically models global–local dependencies by combining a Cross-modal Attention Graph Construction Strategy (CAGS) with a Gated Residual Graph Convolution Strategy (GRGCS). Second, the Modality-Weighted Variational Encoding Module (MWVEM) effectively alleviates semantic ambiguity via variational guidance and modality-weighted fusion, thereby promoting robust multimodal alignment. MVGNet establishes a progressive feature alignment – structural fusion paradigm, significantly improving sentiment analysis robustness. Extensive experiments on CMU-MOSI and CMU-MOSEI benchmark datasets demonstrate that MVGNet outperforms the state-of-the-art methods.

title: Spiking Self-Organizing Maps with Convergence Guarantees for Unsupervised Radar Signal Deinterleaving
authors: Qi Sun, Shumo Yang, Zhen Cao, Shiqing Yang, Biao Hou
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In high-density, multi-source radar pulse environments, signal deinterleaving requires both high precision and real-time performance. Traditional pulse descriptor word (PDW)-based statistical or rule-based methods are limited in complex scenarios and incur high computational costs, making them unsuitable for edge deployment. We propose a Spiking Self-Organizing Map (Spiking-SOM) that integrates LIF spiking neurons with SOM unsupervised learning rules, enabling BMU selection and weight updates in the spike domain while avoiding floating-point distance computation. Theoretically, we prove that BMU firing rates are monotonically increasing and weight updates satisfy the Robbins-Monro conditions, ensuring topological stability and convergence. During inference, Spiking-SOM leverages event-driven accumulation for similarity computation and sparse weight updates, with arithmetic complexity and memory access decreasing linearly with input sparsity. Experiments demonstrate that Spiking-SOM achieves high accuracy while enabling sparse, low-power computation, making it suitable for deployment on FPGA and other edge hardware.

title: TASU: Text-only Alignment for Speech Understanding
authors: Jing Peng, Yi Yang, Xu Li, Yu Xi, Quanwei Tang, Yangui Fang, Junjie Li, Kai Yu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Recent advances in Speech Large Language Models (Speech LLMs) have paved the way for unified architectures across diverse speech understanding tasks. However, prevailing alignment paradigms rely heavily on large-scale audio-text paired data and computationally intensive training, yet often exhibit limited generalization to unseen domains or tasks. To address these limitations, we propose TASU (Text-only Alignment for Speech Understanding), a novel alignment paradigm that can leverage only unpaired text data to guide cross-modal alignment. Experiments show that TASU achieves competitive zero-shot speech recognition. Leveraging this property, it can further function as a pre-training stage in curriculum learning, enhancing domain generalization in speech recognition. Ultimately, TASU can extend its zero-shot generalization to a wide range of speech understanding tasks and notably outperforms prominent Speech LLMs including GLM-4-Voice and Step-Audio on the MMSU benchmark, establishing TASU as an efficient and scalable alignment paradigm for Speech LLMs.

title: Enhance Balance between Generalization and Personalization for Vision-Language Models in Federated Learning
authors: Ziyun Cai, Yizhou Lu, Yawen Huang, Jie Song, Ye Liu, Xiao-Yuan Jing
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We propose FLORA, a novel Federated Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning framework that combines orthogonal low-rank adaptation for global prompt with attention-guided visual adapter to address the balance between generalization and personalization under data heterogeneity. Specifically, each client personalizes the global prompt through orthogonal low-rank adaptation term with an additional orthogonal loss, thereby achieving efficient local adaptation while maintaining the generalization of the global prompt. Furthermore, we design a lightweight attention-guided visual adapter for the image encoder to enhance cross-modal alignment, further balancing generalization and personalization. Extensive experiments on multiple datasets demonstrate that our FLORA achieves superior performance in balancing generalization and personalization over state-of-the-art methods.

title: Directed Hypergraph Framelet Neural Network
authors: Zhen Wang, Xiaoyu Tang, Xinyu Qi, Yongheng Wang, Yan Pang, Ji Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Hypergraphs model multi-way directional interactions, yet most Hypergraph Neural Networks ignore direction and collapse higher-order structure. We propose Di-HFNN, which combines a complex-valued incidence encoding yielding a Hermitian Laplacian with multiresolution wavelet frames for spectral filtering. This framework captures directional asymmetries across multiple scales. Di-HFNN achieves state-of-the-art performance on eight benchmarks and demonstrates superior directional modeling on synthetic tasks.

title: An Enhanced Memory Attention and Content-Guided Model for Mini Led Anomaly Detection
authors: Lei Zhou, Long Chen, You Zhou, Zongze Wu, Wei Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: During the manufacturing process, the Mini Light-Emitting Diode (Mini LED) develops various defects. As new defect types continually emerge with limited samples, conventional supervised deep learning-based methods demonstrate poor performance in this scenario. To address this challenge, we propose an unsupervised anomaly detection model, named MACG, that only requires normal samples for training. First, we introduce Memory Attention (MA), which employs learnable parametric memory templates for efficient normal pattern learning. Second, we design a Content-Guided Decoder (CGD) to filter out anomalous features. Finally, we propose a Multi-Branch Downsampler (MBD) that minimizes feature information loss during downsampling through a parallel branch architecture. Extensive experiments show that MACG achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches on both our custom Mini LED datasets and the public MVTec LOCO dataset.

title: Place Anywhere: Learning Spatial Reasoning for Occlusion-Aware Image Composition
authors: Zan Li, Bingnan Wang, Wencheng Wang, Fei Hou
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Image composition aims to seamlessly blend objects into new environments, which is a fundamental task in computer vision and graphics. Existing methods often cannot fully understand the 3D contents. They only allow to insert an object into part of the image, where there are no occlusion issues. To address this limitation, occlusions between foreground and background objects must be carefully identified to freely generate a reasonable composited image. We propose Place Anywhere, a framework with strong spatial reasoning capability that learns to resolve occlusion relationships in arbitrary compositions. Given a foreground object and a background scene, our model predicts a visibility mask for the foreground, indicating which regions should remain visible after considering occlusion. This is achieved through our carefully designed feature extraction, fusion, and depth-guided modules, along with a novel method for constructing training data that incorporates spatial relationships. Experimental results show that Place Anywhere can generalize well, learn robust 3D spatial reasoning, and produce realistic compositions without explicit placement constraints. The source code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/Zannnn/autoComposition.

title: Dynamic Spectrogram Analysis with Local-Aware Graph Networks for Audio Anti-Spoofing
authors: Yingdong Li, Chengxin Chen, Dong Chen, Nanli Zeng, Kun Zeng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of deepfake techniques has introduced diverse forgery artifacts, posing substantial challenges to audio anti-spoofing. This paper proposes a model that adaptively tailors feature extraction to the specific nature of these artifacts. The approach comprises three key contributions: (i) a context-aware dynamic convolution that selects and weights kernels of multiple sizes conditioned on spectral context; (ii) a streamlined front-end/back-end integration that uses a residual-style shortcut to the AASIST back end, thereby obviating a dedicated feature-fusion module; and (iii) an augmented AASIST framework with two new node types designed to capture fine-grained local patterns. Evaluated on the ASVspoof 2019 LA and the Chinese Fake Speech Dataset (CFSD), our proposed model achieves state-of-the-art performance, attaining Equal Error Rates (EERs) of 0.08% and 0.10%, respectively.

title: Improving Continuous Sign Language Recognition via Lightweight Adaptive Temporal Mixing
authors: Yining Jiang, Sheng Liu, Yuan Feng, Min Xu, Yiheng Yu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Current continuous sign language recognition (CSLR) methods either overlook the dynamics of cross-frame motions or rely on hand-crafted and complex strategies, leading to computational overhead and limited adaptability. To address these issues, we propose DMSign, which integrates two complementary modules: Dynamic Patch Mixing (DPM) and Dynamic Channel Mixing (DCM). DPM reorganizes local spatial patches across frames to flexibly model subtle sign variations, while DCM applies a learnable per-channel temporal filter that dynamically allocates information across frames, thereby promoting complementary feature fusion. Both modules are lightweight and adaptive, balancing efficiency and computational cost. DMSign achieves a new state-of-the-art on three large-scale datasets, i.e., PHOENIX14, PHOENIX14-T, and CSL-Daily.

title: BDRNet:Bidirectional Decomposed and Recalibrated Lightweight Network for Human Pose Estimation
authors: Jing Luo, Yanxia Wang, Xuping Liang, Wenwen Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Lightweight human pose estimation models often struggle to balance accuracy and efficiency in complex scenarios. Their limited receptive fields and feature representation capabilities lead to significant performance degradation under occlusions, crowded environments, etc. Existing methods typically address this issue by introducing multi-scale modules or global attention mechanisms, which often incur substantial computational costs or fail to effectively model the balance between long-range dependencies and local details. To alleviate this problem, this study proposes a lightweight cross-scale attention fusion network named BDRNet. The network innovatively integrates two key modules: the Bidirectional Decomposed Spatial Attention (BDSA) and the Multi-Scale Recalibrated Attention (MSRA). BDSA captures directional local details with minimal computational cost, while MSRA further efficiently models global contextual relationships and integrates local details through the Recalibrated Hybrid Attention (RHA) and Pointwise Shuffle Block (PWSB). Experimental results on the MSCOCO and MPII datasets demonstrate that BDRNet achieves competitive overall performance, excels in complex scenarios, approaches the performance of large-scale network models on average precision (AP), and outperforms most other lightweight models, striking a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency.

title: Joint Reproduction Number and Spatial Connectivity Structure Estimation via Graph Sparsity-Promoting Penalized Functional
authors: E. Lasalle, B. Pascal
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: During an epidemic outbreak, decision makers crucially need accurate and robust tools to monitor the pathogen propagation. The effective reproduction number, defined as the expected number of secondary infections stemming from one contaminated individual, is a state-of-the-art indicator quantifying the epidemic intensity. Numerous estimators have been developed to precisely track the reproduction number temporal evolution. Yet, COVID-19 pandemic surveillance raised unprecedented challenges due to the poor quality of worldwide reported infection counts. When monitoring the epidemic in different territories simultaneously, leveraging the spatial structure of data significantly enhances both the accuracy and robustness of reproduction number estimates. However, this requires a good estimate of the spatial structure. To tackle this major limitation, the present work proposes a joint estimator of the reproduction number and connectivity structure. The procedure is assessed through intensive numerical simulations on carefully designed synthetic data and illustrated on real COVID-19 spatiotemporal infection counts.

title: P-SAM: Parallel Semantic Decoding of SAM for Domain-Driven Prompt Generation in Pore Segmentation
authors: Dongsheng Li, Huijie Zhang, Qiushi Xia
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Precise characterization of pore structures in tight sandstone reservoirs is essential for optimizing unconventional hydrocarbon recovery. However, the microscopic complexity and scarcity of samples pose challenges to the accuracy and generalizability of traditional segmentation methods. While the Segment Anything Model (SAM) demonstrates powerful general segmentation capabilities, its reliance on domain-specific prompts limits its automated application in specialized scenarios. To address these challenges, we propose a Parallel-decoding SAM framework (P-SAM). Unlike existing methods that rely on external models for prompt generation, P-SAM introduces a lightweight prompt decoder that directly produces task-relevant prompts from SAM’s image features while keeping all SAM parameters frozen. Through feature sharing and loss alignment, P-SAM jointly optimizes prompt generation and mask prediction under a unified training goal. Experiments demonstrate that P-SAM surpasses state-of-the-art methods with only 0.16M additional parameters and exhibits strong cross-domain adaptability in tasks such as urban village identification. Our code is available at https://github.com/wudi-ldd/ Parallel-Decoding-SAM.

title: Diversity is All You Need: Self-Supervised Hypergraph Learning for Mitigating Popularity Bias in Conversational Recommender System
authors: Yongsen Zheng, Ruilin Xu, Ye Ma, Guohua Wang, Liang Lin, Kwok-Yan Lam
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Popularity bias is the tendency of recommender systems (RSs) to favor popular items over personalized or niche options, reinforcing the popularity of well-liked items. Most existing methods aim to alleviate popularity bias in offline static environments, but this bias tends to intensify over time in online recommendations. To address these issues, we propose a novel framework, Self-Supervised Hypergraph Learning for Mitigating Popularity Bias in Conversational Recommender System (SHLCRS), which aims to reduce popularity bias in the dynamic user-system feedback loop by capturing diverse user preferences through hypergraphs. SHLCRS establishes a self-supervised learning paradigm to create paired hypergraphs and line graphs for exploring varied preferences. It then employs a Multi-Hypergraph-Oriented CRS to leverage these preferences for accurate item predictions and effective conversational responses. Extensive experiments on two public CRS benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of SHLCRS in mitigating popularity bias.

title: AHM-Net: An Asymmetric Hierarchical Multi-Modal Fusion Network for Robust UAV Detection Using RGB and Event Data
authors: Daohong Niu, Wenhan Yang, Wen Yang, Dexin Bi, Shuai Ma, Jinjian Wu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Detecting unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) robustly is challenging for single-modality sensors: RGB cameras often fail under poor lighting, while event cameras lack sufficient texture information for reliable detection. To address these limitations, we propose AHM-Net, an Asymmetric Hierarchical Multi-modal Fusion Network that effectively integrates RGB and event data for enhanced UAV detection. The core of our approach is an asymmetric hierarchical fusion encoder that incorporates cross-modal interaction modules, enabling adaptive fusion of complementary features from both modalities at different levels. Additionally, we introduce two novel event-aware enhancements: Stochastic Background Suppression (SBS) for motion-salient feature refinement and Event-Aware Query Selection (EAQS) for texture-compensated query guidance. Experiments on the large-scale FRED benchmark demonstrate that AHM-Net outperforms existing methods, while ablation studies confirm the contribution of each component. These results highlight the promise of event-driven fusion for advancing reliable UAV detection.

title: AIMRec: Aligning both Individuals and Modalities for Multimodal Recommendation
authors: Yihan Zhang, Ercan E. Kuruoglu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multimodal recommendation requires learning expressive representations of users and items by integrating multimodal features such as text and images. While prior studies have extensively explored graph convolution-based frameworks and modality fusion strategies, a critical issue that remains underexplored is the alignment of representations across embedding spaces. In this work, we propose a simple yet effective model, AIMRec, which performs graph convolution separately on ID and feature embeddings before combining them into unified user and item representations. To enhance consistency between different representations, we introduce two auxiliary alignment losses: an ID–Feature Alignment loss that enforces coherence between ID and feature embeddings of individuals, and a Cross-Modal Alignment loss that aligns item representations across modalities from both local and global perspectives. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets demonstrate the superiority of AIMRec over baselines. Further ablation studies confirm the effectiveness of each proposed component.

title: Enhancing Audio Question-Answering Performance Through Log-Likelihood Guided Reward Functions
authors: Sam Blouir, Ganesh Ramachandra Kini, Qingming Tang, Raphael Petegrosso, Chieh-Chi Kao, Ankur Gandhe, Chao Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has shown promise in training Large Language Models (LLMs) for text-to-text and multimodal tasks. Current RLVR approaches for audio question-answering often use format adherence and binary correctness signals. We propose a new reward framework for extracting learning signals from multiple-choice questions (MCQs). Our approach uses probability-ratio rewards to increase the likelihood difference between correct and incorrect answer choices, expanding beyond accuracy metrics. We conducted experiments on a standard audio-reasoning benchmark, where our probability-ratio reward framework performed better than the base model and existing RLVR methods. The results suggest that likelihood-based rewards can improve audio reasoning capabilities, offering an enhanced approach for multiple-choice audio question-answering systems. Specifically, we test our methods on MMAU [10], a recent complex audio QA benchmark that requires reasoning capability towards achieving human-level performance.

title: UAFD: Unified Adaptive Frequency-Domain Detector for Generalizable Deepfake Detection
authors: Jialei Zhou, Hongnan Ma, Wenlong Wang, Shuxia Ren, Kexue Fu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: With the rapid development of diffusion-based image generation, distinguishing highly realistic synthetic images from authentic ones has become increasingly crucial. Despite their visual realism, generative models inevitably introduce inconsistencies due to their fundamentally imitative nature. To address this, we propose UAFD (Unified Adaptive Frequency-Domain Detector), a detection framework that maps nuance, illumination, and reconstruction inconsistencies into a unified frequency-domain representation space. Specifically, nuance inconsistencies are captured via multi-scale Haar wavelet decomposition across different subbands; illumination inconsistencies are modeled through gradient analysis combined with multi-directional Gabor filtering to extract directional lighting cues; and reconstruction inconsistencies are redefined as residual differences between the original and diffusion-reconstructed image spectrums. Furthermore, a spectrum-adaptive weighting mechanism dynamically adjusts the importance of each modality based on frequency energy distribution and spectral complexity. This unified design enhances robustness across generative methods while improving generalization, interpretability, and efficiency.

title: Performance-Guided Reinforced Active Learning for Object Detection
authors: Zhixuan Liang, Xingyu Zeng, Rui Zhao, Ping Luo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Active learning (AL) strategies aim to train high-performance models with minimal labeling efforts, only selecting the most informative instances for annotation. Current approaches to evaluating data informativeness predominantly focus on the data’s distribution or intrinsic information content and do not directly correlate with downstream task performance, such as mean average precision (mAP) in object detection. Thus, we propose Performance-guided (i.e.mAP-guided) Reinforced Active Learning for Object Detection (MGRAL), a novel approach that leverages the concept of expected model output changes as informativeness. To address the combinatorial explosion challenge of batch sample selection and the non-differentiable correlation between model performance and selected batches, MGRAL skillfully employs a reinforcement learning-based sampling agent that optimizes selection using policy gradient with mAP improvement as reward. Moreover, to reduce the computational overhead of mAP estimation with unlabeled samples, MGRAL utilizes an unsupervised way with fast look-up tables, ensuring feasible deployment. We evaluate MGRAL’s active learning performance on detection tasks over PASCAL VOC and COCO benchmarks. Our approach demonstrates the highest AL curve with convincing visualizations, establishing a new paradigm in reinforcement learning-driven active object detection.

title: Gaussian Splatting with Hybrid Deformation and Multi-Scale Depth Regularization for Dynamic Single-View Video Reconstruction
authors: Ying Ye, Xian Wu, Jintian Li, Junfeng Yao, Shaoqi Wu, Weixing Xie
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: With the advent of 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) and foundation models, high-fidelity dynamic scene reconstruction from single-view videos has become feasible. However, existing 3DGS-based methods primarily focus on fast reconstruction, often neglecting motion interactions between Gaussians and accurate depth generation, leading to suboptimal reconstruction. To address these challenges, we introduce GS-HDM, a novel dynamic reconstruction framework. First, we design a hybrid deformable representation to capture complex motion interactions, combining Fourier functions and shared hash grids to model individual Gaussian motion and spatiotemporal interactions among Gaussians, respectively. In addition, to achieve accurate and sharp depth, we propose a multi-scale depth regularization strategy that guides the network to learn depth priors at different scales, while leveraging confidence weights to mitigate depth noise from foundation models. Finally, we develop an efficient Gaussian initialization strategy to generate dense geometric priors, further enhancing the performance of GS-HDM. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms existing approaches in both photorealistic rendering and accurate depth reconstruction.

title: LumiDiff: Luminance-Prior Guided Diffusion for Perceptually Balanced Low-Light Image Signal Recovery
authors: Wenwei Luo, Xiangxu Zhao, Zhikun Zheng, Mengmeng Jing, Lin Zuo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Low-light image recovery can be formulated as a two-dimensional signal restoration problem corrupted by non-stationary degradation factors. To recover the image signal, a critical challenge is the non-uniform illumination caused by non-stationary lighting conditions. Traditional signal processing methods often introduce artifacts during recovery. In this paper, we propose Luminance-Prior Guided Diffusion (LumiDiff) to alleviate this problem. Specifically, we construct a luminance distribution map as luminance prior to capture the light distribution in degraded images, ensuring accurate representation of illumination variances. Subsequently, we utilize the luminance distribution map to guide the global-local information aggregation, effectively restoring areas of extreme darkness and reducing the uneven illumination effects. Finally, we utilize the inherent noise modeling capability of diffusion models to perform overall luminance correction while simultaneously removing the amplified noise from the enhancement process. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework achieves superior balance of quantitative metrics and visual quality compared to existing methods.

title: De-Biasing Facial Age Estimation: A Dual-Stage Disentanglement Framework for Cross-Racial Generalization
authors: Shuo Gao, Zhenhua Tan, Mingzhao Xie, Li Li, Li Tan, Yingyou Wen
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Facial age estimation often suffers from racial bias due to limited and imbalanced racial representation in training data. In this paper, we propose a cross-race age estimation method based on monoracial samples training, aiming to mitigate the racial bias correlated with age information. Specifically, the proposed method mainly consists of two parts, i.e., Mixed-Feature Extraction module and De-Bias module. We first introduce the Mixed-Feature Extraction module to explore the facial mixed features, which exist in the form of pixels in facial images. Then, the De-Bias module is designed to mitigate racial bias from the extracted mixed features, thereby achieving cross-race age estimation. Particularly, all experiments evaluation followed a clear and fair evaluation protocol to ensure the reliability of results. Extensive experiments on the popular datasets have validated that the proposed method is more effective on cross-race age estimation.

title: A Comprehensive Ecosystem for Open-Domain Customized Video Generation
authors: Jingxu Zhang, Yuqian Hong, Daneul Kim, Kai Qiu, Qi Dai, Jianmin Bao, Yifan Yang, Xiaoyan Sun, Chong Luo
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Recent progress in video generation has shown impressive visual synthesis capabilities. However, open-domain customized video generation remains limited by the lack of large-scale, annotated datasets capturing diverse identity-specific attributes. To address this, we introduce PexelsCustom-1M, the first publicly available million-scale dataset for identity-preserving video generation, containing one million curated ⟨identity, text, video⟩ triplets across 8,000+ categories. Leveraging this, we propose CustoMDiT, a parameter-efficient framework that adapts a pretrained multimodal Diffusion Transformer into a customized video generator with only 8% additional learnable parameters. Our method surpasses prior state-of-the-art. However, benchmarks such as DreamBooth cover only 100 classes, which is insufficient for real-world applications. To overcome this, we construct OpenCustom, a new benchmark with 1,000+ categories, created via cross-dataset knowledge fusion from ImageNet and MS-COCO. Extensive experiments confirm the advantages of both our dataset and model. We will open-source the entire ecosystem—including dataset, pipeline, benchmark, and implementations—to support further research.

title: A Decomposition-Based State Space Model for Multivariate Time-Series Forecasting
authors: Shunya Nagashima, Shuntaro Suzuki, Shuitsu Koyama, Shinnosuke Hirano
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multivariate time series (MTS) forecasting is crucial for decision-making in domains such as weather, energy, and finance. It remains challenging because real-world sequences intertwine slow trends, multi-rate seasonalities, and irregular residuals. Existing methods often rely on rigid, hand-crafted decompositions or generic end-to-end architectures that entangle components and underuse structure shared across variables. To address these limitations, we propose DecompSSM, an end-to-end decomposition framework using three parallel deep state space model branches to capture trend, seasonal, and residual components. The model features adaptive temporal scales via an input-dependent predictor, a refinement module for shared cross-variable context, and an auxiliary loss that enforces reconstruction and orthogonality. Across standard benchmarks (ECL, Weather, ETTm2, and PEMS04), DecompSSM outperformed strong baselines, indicating the effectiveness of combining component-wise deep state space models and global context refinement.

title: Large Vision Models can Solve Mental Rotation Problems
authors: Sebastian Ray Mason, Anders Gjølbye, Phillip Chavarria Højbjerg, Lenka Tětková, Lars Kai Hansen
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Mental rotation is a key test of spatial reasoning in humans and has been central to understanding how perception supports cognition. Despite the success of modern vision transformers, it is still unclear how well these models develop similar abilities. In this work, we present a systematic evaluation of ViT, CLIP, DINOv2, and DINOv3 across a range of mental-rotation tasks, from simple block structures similar to those used by Shepard and Metzler to study human cognition, to more complex block figures, three types of text, and photo-realistic objects. By probing model representations layer by layer, we examine where and how these networks succeed. We find that i) self-supervised ViTs capture geometric structure better than supervised ViTs; ii) intermediate layers perform better than final layers; iii) task difficulty increases with rotation complexity and occlusion, mirroring human reaction times and suggesting similar constraints in embedding space representations.

title: Mind the Noise, Align the Fine: Confidence-Aware Masked Image Modeling for Text-based Person Re-Identification
authors: Zhengbing Li, Jiaying Chen, Yurong Qian, Huiyong Lv
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Text-based Person Re-identification (TBPR) aims to retrieve person images from natural language descriptions. However, existing approaches face two major challenges. First, training data from real-world scenarios often contain samples with inaccurate or semantically ambiguous image-text correspondences. Second, previous works insufficiently model the visual information. To address these issues, we propose a novel TBPR framework named FiMi, which integrates two key components: the Confidence Modeling Module (CMM) and the Cross-modal Masked Image Modeling (CroMIM) module. CMM models the global alignment loss distribution of image-text pairs using a Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM), and estimates the posterior probability of each pair belonging to the high-confidence group to generate adaptive confidence weights, thereby achieving soft-label supervision. CroMIM introduces a lightweight cross-modal reconstruction task by randomly masking image regions and recovering them using both textual and unmasked visual cues, thereby enhancing fine-grained alignment. Extensive experiments on three public benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of our FiMi framework.

title: MPM-QIR: Measurement-Probability Matching for Quantum Image Representation and Compression via Variational Quantum Circuit
authors: Chong-Wei Wang, Mei Ian Sam, Tzu-Ling Kuo, Nan-Yow Chen, Tai-Yue Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We present MPM-QIR, a variational-quantum-circuit (VQC) framework for classical image compression and representation whose core objective is to achieve equal or better reconstruction quality at a lower Parameter Compression Ratio (PCR). The method aligns a generative VQC’s measurement–probability distribution with normalized pixel intensities and learns positional information implicitly via an ordered mapping to the flattened pixel array, thereby eliminating explicit coordinate qubits and tying compression efficiency directly to circuit (ansatz) complexity. A bidirectional convolutional architecture induces long-range entanglement at shallow depth, capturing global image correlations with fewer parameters. Under a unified protocol, the approach attains PSNR ≥ 30 dB with lower PCR across benchmarks: MNIST 31.80 dB / SSIM 0.81 at PCR 0.69, Fashion-MNIST 31.30 dB / 0.91 at PCR 0.83, and CIFAR-10 31.56 dB / 0.97 at PCR 0.84. Overall, this compression-first design improves parameter efficiency, validates VQCs as direct and effective generative models for classical image compression, and is amenable to two-stage pipelines with classical codecs and to extensions beyond 2D imagery.

title: Dissecting Performance Degradation in Audio Source Separation under Sampling Frequency Mismatch
authors: Kanami Imamura, Tomohiko Nakamura, Kohei Yatabe, Hiroshi Saruwatari
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Audio processing methods based on deep neural networks are typically trained at a single sampling frequency (SF). To handle untrained SFs, signal resampling of the input signal is commonly employed, but it can degrade performance, particularly when the input SF is lower than the trained SF. This paper investigates the causes of this degradation through two hypotheses: (i) the lack of high-frequency components introduced by up-sampling, and (ii) the greater importance of their presence than their spectral contents. To examine these hypotheses, we compare conventional resampling with three alternatives: post-resampling noise addition, which adds Gaussian noise to the resampled signal; noisy-kernel resampling, which perturbs the kernel with Gaussian noise to enrich high-frequency components; and trainable-kernel resampling, which adapts the interpolation kernel through training. Experiments on music source separation show that noisy-kernel and trainable-kernel resampling alleviate the degradation observed with conventional resampling. We further demonstrate that noisy-kernel resampling is effective across diverse models, highlighting it as a simple yet practical option to alleviate the performance degradation.

title: MotionPLLM: LLM-Based Generator for Part-Level Controllable Human Motion in Quantized Latent Space
authors: Jiaqi Zhou, Haofei Wang, Jian Wu, Ju Dai, Lili Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Text-driven human motion generation has advanced with deep models, yet most large language models (LLMs) methods reason over holistic full-body representations, overlooking part-level semantics and limiting control and diversity. We present MotionPLLM, an LLM-based framework that models motion at the part level and encodes dynamics in a quantized latent space. A hierarchical MotionPart-Representation decomposes motion into semantically meaningful body parts, enabling localized control while preserving global coherence. Built on this representation, MotionPart-VQVAE discretizes part-wise dynamics into a structured codebook, supporting efficient tokenization and robust representation learning. Extensive experiments show that MotionPLLM surpasses recent LLM-based baselines in motion quality, generation diversity, and text–motion alignment, offering precise part-level control without sacrificing semantic consistency.

title: FCSTG-INCEPTIONNET: Temporal Lag and Mesoscale Spatio-Temporal Features Modeling for EEG-Based Diagnostics
authors: Xiwen Yang, Yunfei Han, Xun Deng, Zhibin Li, Feng Tan, Yu-An Huang, Zhuhong You, Lun Hu, Le Tong, Pengwei Hu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Analyzing complex Electroencephalography (EEG) signals for brain function research is challenging due to temporal lags between brain regions and difficulties in capturing mesoscale spatio-temporal features. To address this, we introduce FCSTG-InceptionNet, a model designed to capture these intricate dependencies. It integrates a Fully-Connected Spatial-Temporal Graph Neural Network for time-lagged relationships with a multi-path attention mechanism and 3D electrode spatial mapping to effectively capture local, mesoscale, and global features. Validated on the SEED and CHB-MIT datasets for emotion classification and epilepsy detection, FCSTG-InceptionNet demonstrates superior and robust performance over baselines in subject-dependent, cross-session, and cross-subject experiments. These results highlight the model’s potential for advancing EEG-based diagnostics by effectively modeling complex spatio-temporal dynamics.

title: Decision Fusedconv: Efficient Offline Reinforcement Learning Via Fused State-Reward Encoding and Hybrid Temporal Convolution
authors: Zhuojing Tian, Yushu Chen, Haoyu Ma, Wenlai Zhao, Zhao Liu, Hongkun Yu, Wusheng Zhang, Shiming Song, Dianxi Shi
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Decision Transformer (DT) represents return-to-go, state, and action as independent tokens, resulting in inflated sequence length and quadratic attention cost. To address this inefficiency, we propose Decision FusedConv (DFC), which jointly encodes return and state to shorten sequences and employs a gated hybrid convolutional module that integrates global uniform and local heterogeneous convolutions. This design effectively captures both long-range and modality-specific temporal dependencies while avoiding the overhead of self-attention. Experiments on MuJoCo and navigation benchmarks show that DFC consistently outperforms DT and recent variants, achieving up to a 64.9% reduction in FLOPs compared to DT, making it particularly suitable for resource-constrained settings. Our code is available at: https://github.com/Drama1234/Decision-FusedConv

title: EDPOTrans: Enhanced Direct Preference Optimization for Machine Translation Between Low-Resource Language and Chinese with Limited Monolingual Data
authors: Lianxi Wang, Xuming Li, Yuting Su, Songxi Xu, Jiahuan Yang, Nankai Lin, Shengyi Jiang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated superior performance across various machine translation tasks by integrating techniques such as model fine-tuning and reinforcement learning methods like Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), while still facing significant challenges in low-resource languages due to the scarcity of bilingual data. To address this issue, we propose EDPOTrans, a new self-supervised fine-tuning framework for LLMs. Aimed at mitigating the lack of bilingual data, EDPOTrans performs sampling on monolingual data and utilizes Minimum Bayes Risk (MBR) decoding to generate candidate translations and construct preference data. Furthermore, to tackle the potential reward instability in DPO, we introduce Enhanced DPO (EDPO).
