Furthermore, we construct an indoor communication link using standard-gain ridged horn antenna as transmitter (Tx) and an inhouse wide-band printed monopole antenna as a receiver (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R x}$</tex>), and measure corresponding pathloss over the frequency range 8-12 GHz by moving the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R x}$</tex> antenna for fixed Tx antenna location. Specifically, we investigate the effect on pathloss for respective Tx-Rx antenna polarization states as VV, VH, HV and HH (here V is vertical and H is horizontal). The measurement campaign showcases: (i) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-20 \text{dB}$</tex> difference between the copolar (VV, HH) and cross-polar (VH, HV) configurations, (ii) significant difference between frequency dependent pathloss for co-polar (VV, HH) configurations. The results of this paper will be useful for realization of RIS-assisted 6G wireless networks.

title: SentientSec Corporate Management System with AI NLP Avatars & IoT Cyber Defense
authors: Sujanthi S, Sri Harivarshan R, Neya Charlote S A, Visnupriyan P M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: This article focuses on the improvement of a corporate management system named SentientSec that facilitates the exchange of information, enhances strong privacy, and anticipatory user interaction. This includes artificial intelligence and natural language processing that are used to introduce a virtual assistant to handle cyber threats and solve security concerns in a real-time environment. This also includes the perspective of augmented and virtual reality that enables the users to visualize a 3D situational awareness model of cyber security breaches. Through this the users gain knowledge about the attacks and the way to defend and negate them. To track the physical security breaches, IoT devices are used. This system, on the whole, offers AI feedback in real time, physical monitoring through IoT devices, and AR/VR responses to the users.

title: Multi-Classifier and Deep Learning Framework for Robust Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Swathi D R, Kalaiarasan T, Sanjai C, Vinotheni M S, Mariammal K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Credit card fraud remains a major concern in the financial sector, leading to significant financial losses and eroding customer trust. Real-time detection is challenging due to highly imbalanced datasets and constantly changing fraud behaviors. This work presents a unified fraud detection framework that combines supervised learning models such as Random Forest, Stochastic Gradient Descent Classifier, Support Vector Machine and Logistic Regression with unsupervised techniques including Isolation Forest, One-Class Support Vector Machine and Density-Based Spatial Clustering for Applications with Noise to identify both known and previously unseen fraud patterns. Reinforcement learning methods such as Q-Learning and Deep Q-Learning enable adaptive detection strategies that improve over time, while deep learning architectures such as Multilayer Perceptron and Long Short-Term Memory networks capture complex sequential and nonlinear transaction patterns. A key contribution is the extraction and conversion of model parameters into FPGA compatible formats using Vitis High-Level Synthesis, enabling real-time hardware-accelerated inference with significantly reduced latency. Experimental evaluation on benchmark datasets demonstrates superior accuracy, recall, F1-score and ROC-AUC with lower false-positive rates compared to individual models, making the proposed framework scalable, adaptive and effective for real-time credit card fraud detection..

title: Robust Multi-Model Kalman Filtering with Adaptive Noise Tuning for Maneuvering Source DOA Estimation in MIMO Radar
authors: Vijay Kumar N. V. S. V, Rajesh Kumar P
abstract: Dynamic target tracking in MIMO radar systems demands algorithms capable of adapting to varying motion regimes and degraded SNR conditions. This paper develops an adaptive Interacting Multiple Model (IMM) filter specifically designed for Direction of Arrival (DOA) estimation under Rayleigh fading propagation. Six algorithmic improvements are proposed: (1) extension of the Constant Acceleration model to three states for direct acceleration observability, (2) SNR-adaptive process noise scaling (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-5.0$</tex> base values with motion-specific multipliers), (3) SNR-dependent measurement noise parameterization (0.5-3.5), (4) optimized motion-specific model transition matrices with self-transition probabilities ranging from (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.70-0.90$</tex>), (5) robust multi-point weighted least squares initialization, and (6) Joseph-form updates integrated with Rauch-Tung-Striebel smoothing for enhanced numerical stability. Performance assessment across eight scenarios-Constant Velocity, Constant Acceleration, Coordinated Turn, and Mixed maneuvers at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+10 d B$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-5 d B$</tex> SNR—reveals 28.6 % average and 38.1 % peak RMSE reduction relative to single-model Kalman filtering. The enhanced filter achieves stable tracking performance ((0.2-0.5) error) in contrast to the oscillating error behavior (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.2-1.2$</tex>) exhibited by conventional approaches.

title: Performance Evaluation of PBCH Equalization in the Presence of CFO for B5G and 6G Systems
authors: Abhinav Anand, Adarsh Ravi, Preetam Kumar
abstract: The 5G NR initial access (IA) process begins with detecting the synchronisation signal block (SSB), which contains the primary and secondary synchronisation signals (PSS and SSS) as well as the physical broadcast channel (PBCH). However, at millimetre-wave FR2 frequencies, oscillator drift, Doppler rotation, and phase noise significantly impair PBCH decoding. This work evaluates FR2 synchronisation robustness under carrier frequency offset (CFO) using a third-generation partnership project technical report 38.901-compliant link level framework based on the clustered delay line urban macro model. The framework models the full physical-layer synchronisation chain, including SSB based cell search, channel propagation, CFO estimation and compensation, and PBCH demodulation. By comparing compensated and uncompensated receivers, we quantify the sensitivity of PBCH decoding to CFO. Results show that combining PSS based coarse estimation with cyclic-prefix fine refinement keeps the residual CFO below 150 Hz and holds the PBCH EVM near 0.55 %, even for frequency offsets up to 50 kHz in either direction. In contrast, without CFO removal the EVM exceeds 60 %, causing complete PBCH decoding failure despite correct beam alignment. These findings provide concrete evidence that CFO dominates PBCH robustness in FR2 and establish practical tolerance limits for beyond-5G (B5G) and 6G IA under non-ideal oscillator conditions.

title: Artificial Intelligence Enabled Smart Sensors Associated Real-Time Human Fall Detection and Reporting System
authors: M Dinesh, N R Rejin Paul, Lakshmi N, P Lavanya, Rathika S, Ramachandran A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Elderly and physically impaired falls are a pressing issue of importance within the community, as they may result in severe injuries, follow-up hospitalization, and even death unless appropriate help is delivered to them. In this paper, a sophisticated real-time fall detection and alert system using artificial intelligence (AI), and smart embedded sensor systems is discussed. The proposed architecture incorporates tri-axial accelerators and gyroscopes with CNN-LSTM-based classification model running on edge devices such as Raspberry Pi, and thus allows low-latency inference without using cloud computing. The reliability of motion analysis is improved with the help of a sophisticated preprocessing pipeline such as signal smoothing, feature extraction and normalization. Experimental verification with SisFall data set and real-life experiments show that the system is able to recognize fall instances with high accuracy and legitimacy and daily activities. This model has an average accuracy of 97.8, F1-score of 96.8, and a false alarm rate of only 2.1, which is much better in performance compared to the traditional models based on threshold and machine learning based models. When the fall is detected, the system will automatically send alerts through SMS, mobile app, and locally located by GPS on a cloud dashboard. The outcomes are in line with the solution as a viable and effective solution to proactive health surveillance. Offline feature, rapid response, and scalability make it one of the promising additions to smart healthcare and assistive technology worlds.

title: Implementation of the Quine-McCluskey Algorithm in LabVIEW for Minimization of Logical Functions
authors: Ivanka Krasteva, Vladimira Ganchovska, Borislav Milenkov
abstract: This article proposes an automated approach for the minimization of logical functions using the QuineMcCluskey method. A graphical user interface (GUI) has been developed based on the LabVIEW programming environment. The primary objective of this development is to increase processing speed and to visualize all intermediate stages involved in solving such problems. This tool can be especially useful for students and educators by providing clear visualization of the individual steps of the Quine-McCluskey algorithm.

title: Bayesian-Optimized BiLSTM with Tensor-Reshaped Structured Data for Robust Breast Cancer Risk Prediction
authors: Adaleta Gicić, Dženana Đonko
abstract: Deep learning has become increasingly significant in clinical medicine, including breast cancer detection, offering significant potential to improve patient outcomes. However, recurrent architectures like LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) and BiLSTM (Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory) remain underutilized for breast cancer prediction using structured tabular data, primarily due to the absence of explicit temporal dependencies, which are unsuitable for sequence-based modeling. This work presents a novel approach that redefines how LSTM architecture can be applied to the publicly available non-sequential Wisconsin Diagnostic Breast Cancer (WDBC), consisting of 569 samples and 30 features. The flat tabular input is reshaped into a fixed-length 3D tensor using a sliding window approach to adapt the data for sequence modeling. This transformation enables the model to leverage LSTM's sequential processing capabilities in a fundamentally new way, capturing implicit feature interactions across structured attributes without temporal context. Furthermore, Bayesian hyperparameter optimization techniques are applied to enhance the model's performance. The proposed model is evaluated against standard LSTM and state-of-the-art tabular Transformer architectures (FT-Transformer and SAINT). Results show that BiLSTM achieves the best overall performance (AUC 0.9985, accuracy 0.9824, RMSE 0.0964), while the LSTM baseline also surpasses both Transformerbased tabular models (AUC 0.9958, accuracy 0.9719). Performance gains are consistent across seven evaluation metrics, with statistical significance confirmed via paired t-tests <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$({p}<0.05)$</tex>. These findings demonstrate that, when appropriately adapted, recurrent architectures can outperform even advanced self-attention models in structured clinical prediction tasks.

title: A Herd Effect based Incentive Scheme for Age of Information Minimization in Mobile Crowdsensing_supp1-3685315.pdf
authors: Anfeng Liu
abstract: Mobile Crowdsensing (MCS) has emerged as an effective paradigm for large-scale data collection, yet it faces two inherently conflicting challenges: minimizing platform cost and reducing the Age of Information (AoI). Existing approaches primarily focus on task assignment optimization or assume fully rational worker behavior, which often limits their effectiveness in achieving timely data updates without incurring substantial costs. To address these challenges, a Herd Effect-based AoI-aware (HEA) data collection scheme is proposed to minimize AoI under budget constraints. Specifically, the HEA scheme incorporates insights from behavioral economics to influence workers’ mobility decisions by exploiting the herd effect. By incentivizing a subset of pioneer workers to adopt a high-effort mobility mode (i.e., the Boosted Mode), the observable behaviors of these pioneers induce subsequent workers to adjust their decisions accordingly, thereby forming a self-reinforcing herd behavior. This mechanism enables timely data collection while effectively controlling the overall incentive expenditure. The effectiveness of the proposed scheme is validated through rigorous theoretical analysis and extensive experiments conducted on multiple real-world datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that HEA consistently outperforms representative baseline schemes, achieving significant AoI reductions with marginal additional cost and substantially improving the trade-off between data timeliness and budget efficiency.

title: GaussianFlow SLAM: Monocular Gaussian Splatting SLAM Guided by GaussianFlow
authors: Dong-Uk Seo, Jinwoo Jeon, Eungchang Mason Lee, Hyun Myung
abstract: Gaussian splatting has recently gained traction as a compelling map representation for SLAM systems, enabling dense and photo-realistic scene modeling. However, its application to monocular SLAM remains challenging due to the lack of reliable geometric cues from monocular input. Without geometric supervision, mapping or tracking could fall in local-minima, resulting in structural degeneracies and inaccuracies. To address this challenge, we propose GaussianFlow SLAM, a monocular 3DGS-SLAM that leverages optical flow as a geometry-aware cue to guide the optimization of both the scene structure and camera poses. By encouraging the projected motion of Gaussians, termed GaussianFlow, to align with the optical flow, our method introduces consistent structural cues to regularize both map reconstruction and pose estimation. Furthermore, we introduce normalized error-based densification and pruning modules to refine inactive and unstable Gaussians, thereby contributing to improved map quality and pose accuracy. Experiments conducted on public datasets demonstrate that our method achieves superior rendering quality and tracking accuracy compared with stateof- the-art algorithms. The source code is available at: https: //github.com/url-kaist/gaussianflow-slam.

title: Joint Optimization of Hybrid Beamforming and Decoding Order for Rate-Splitting Multiple Access-Based Uplink mmWave Systems
authors: Yangyang Cai, Liqun Fu, Dehua Zhao, John M. Cioffi
abstract: To enhance the spectral efficiency and access capacity of communication networks, this work proposes an innovative architecture that integrates rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) with hybrid beamforming (HBF) for uplink millimeter wave (mmWave) communications. In the RSMA transmission, each user’s message is split into two sub-messages, contributing to the overall transmission rate, and the base station (BS) decodes them using successive interference cancellation (SIC) technique. To maximize the system’s sum-rate, users optimize HBF, while the BS determines the decoding order for all sub-messages. The joint optimization is formulated as a non-convex mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem, subject to power, minimum rate, decoding order, and constant modulus constraints. To meet this challenge efficiently, this work introduces a two-stage optimization framework. The first stage optimizes the analog beamformer, while the second stage utilizes alternating optimization (AO) to optimize the decoding order and digital beamformer. Specifically, for given hybrid beamformers, a low-complexity strategy is derived to obtain the optimal SIC decoding order by ranking the sub-messages according to equivalent channel gains and minimum rate constraints. Furthermore, a fast online-learning-based algorithm for digital beamforming, named Learning-aided differentiable explicit Projection Gradient Descent (LPGD), accelerates the optimization process. Simulation results show that the LPGD-based digital beamforming algorithm converges over 100 times faster than traditional fractional programming (FP)-based methods, while maintaining comparable performance. Moreover, the proposed RSMA scheme achieves up to 26.4% and 34.4% improvement in sum-rate over NOMA and SDMA schemes, respectively.

title: Deep Semantic-Attention Proxy Hashing With Coarse-to-Fine Representation for Multi-Label Remote Sensing Image Retrieval
authors: Chunyu Yan, Lei Wang, Qibing Qin, Lei Huang, Wenfeng Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Deep hashing techniques are widely used in remote sensing image retrieval due to their fast retrieval and low cost. However, existing multi-label retrieval methods encounter two main challenges. First, the input sample pixels commonly include irrelevant backgrounds, degrading semantic feature representation. Second, traditional vision transformer models directly adopt fine-grained feature extraction, which generates redundant long sequences and significantly increases computational costs. To address the mentioned problems, a Deep Semantic-attention Proxy Hashing framework (DSaPH) is proposed for multi-label remote sensing image retrieval. Specifically, to achieve the dynamic feature extraction of images, a coarse-to-fine representation module with two stages of coarse segmentation and fine segmentation is proposed, which identifies the object-related patches in the coarse-grained segmentation through critical region detection and aggregates the global features of the original image in combination with the feature reuse mechanism. To exclude background interference and focus on object information, the object-focus loss highlights the object information through Grad-CAM and generates object-fused image-focus hash codes. Furthermore, class-related proxy learning is introduced to use proxy hash codes to supervise the semantic representation of object-fused hash codes, and embed proxy hash codes and object-fused hash codes into a unified Hamming space to capture the Hamming similarity of neighboring multi-label data. Extensive experimental evaluations on three benchmark datasets show that our proposed DSaPH framework achieves excellent performance in multi-label remote sensing image retrieval. The code for our DSaPH framework is publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/QinLab-WFU/DSaPH.git</uri>.

title: High-Resolution Strain Demodulation via Spectrogram Reassignment-Assisted OFDR for Esophageal Manometry
authors: Zihang Ji, Lei Tu, Tianle Chen, Zhou Xu, Liang Wang, Ming Tang
abstract: High-resolution manometry (HRM), as a critical diagnostic method for gastrointestinal diseases, is widely employed for esophageal manometry. However, existing water-perfused catheters and expensive solid-state catheters to realize HRM suffer from either limited frequency response or insufficient spatial resolution. On the other hand, fiber-optic manometry sensor based on optical frequency domain reflectometry (OFDR) can realize distributed measurement at high spatial resolution. Nevertheless, there is a tradeoff between strain resolution and sensing resolution in conventional OFDR, which prevents it from achieving both high sensing resolution and pressure resolution in esophageal manometry, making it difficult to satisfy the HRM requirement. To address this, we propose a spectrogram reassignment (SR) method for strain demodulation, which simultaneously enhances the sensing resolution and strain resolution in OFDR but without much deterioration of the demodulation speed. The experiment results demonstrate a sensing resolution of 1.4 mm and strain resolution of 1.9 μϵ, showing over 4 times improvement when compared with the conventional OFDR. Using the fabricated fiber-optic manometry sensor with 80 μm diameter bend insensitive fiber, our SR-assisted OFDR demonstrates a pressure sensitivity of 1.97 μϵ/mmHg and pressure resolution of 0.96 mmHg within traditional esophageal pressure range of 0∼300 mmHg, satisfying the HRM requirement. This is the first work to present an OFDR fiber-optic manometry sensor that meets the HRM requirement, and it provides a competitive method for esophageal manometry.

title: Distributed Estimator-Based Adaptive Fuzzy Prescribed-Time Consensus Control for Nonlinear Multiagent Systems
authors: Jiguang Peng, Zhi Liu, C. L. Philip Chen, Yun Zhang
abstract: This article investigates the problem of the prescribed-time consensus control for nonlinear multiagent systems under performance constraints and actuator attacks. First, a distributed output-feedback-based prescribed-time estimator is designed for each follower. This enables the followers to precisely estimate the leader’s signal. Subsequently, to achieve the control objective with prescribed performance, a prescribed-time performance function is constructed. Integrated with an asymmetric barrier Lyapunov function approach, it guarantees that asymmetric time-varying performance constraints are satisfied within the preset time. Furthermore, fuzzy logic systems are adopted to approximate unknown nonlinear functions. In addition, to handle the issue of unknown control directions, Nussbaum gain functions are incorporated into the controller design. The proposed controller ensures the boundedness of all signals in the closed-loop system, while simultaneously guaranteeing that the tracking errors remain within the prescribed performance bounds. Finally, simulation results demonstrate the advantages and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Black-Box Adaptation for Deepfake Detection via Local Relation Guided AUC Optimization
authors: Xiaotian Si, Linghui Li, Bingyu Li, Liwei Zhang, Ziduo Guo, Kaiguo Yuan, Qi Tian
abstract: Deepfake technologies pose a growing threat to the Internet of Things (IoT), enabling identity spoofing and the spread of misinformation. Although numerous face forgery detectors have been developed to counter these risks, their realworld deployment is often limited by inherent dataset biases. Existing domain adaptation techniques offer potential remedies, but typically rely on access to raw source data and employ data-dependent alignment strategies under a transductive learning paradigm, raising substantial privacy concerns for source domain individuals. This study revisits the problem from the perspective of black-box domain adaptation and introduces a detection framework that leverages only the predictions from the source model. The method is grounded in a local relation-guided AUC optimization strategy, which leverages the robustness of AUC-based objectives in noisy environments while addressing the limitations of conventional AUC optimization, particularly its vulnerability to confirmation bias and reliance on a fixed decision threshold. To this end, two key components are introduced. First, a nearest-neighbor calibration mechanism is presented, where the local relation feature (LRF), a parameter-free representation, captures differences between real and fake images without favoring specific forgery types, thereby reducing bias inherited from the source model. Second, a GMM-based adaptive thresholding scheme is employed to address asymmetric predictions by dynamically determining the optimal decision boundary for real and fake images. Experiments across multiple datasets show that our approach achieves superior generalization compared to state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, the framework is compatible with a broad range of source and target model configurations to enhance detection performance.

title: Decentralized Federated Learning with Period Gradient Tracking over Time-Varying Networks_supp1-3685244.pdf
authors: Yuehong Chen
abstract: To address the communication challenges associated with Federated Learning (FL), Decentralized Federated Learning (DFL) eliminates the central server and trains the model with decentralized method, enabling each client to only communicate with its neighbors. However, per our analysis, model trained with DFL experiences performance degradation because of data-heterogeneity and time-varying topologies. To address these issues, we propose a Dynamic K-step Gradient Tracking (DKGT) method to enhance the performance of DFL over time varying networks. Specifically, DKGT employs K-step local updates and gradient tracking to reduce the communication cost and the variance from heterogeneous data distribution, and we use dynamic gradient tracking parameter to correct gradient over time varying graph. Theoretically, we derive a universal convergence rate for smooth and non-convex problem at the rate of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal{O}\left(\frac{\left(f(\textbf{x}_0)-f(\textbf{x}^*)\right)}{\sqrt{T}(L\sqrt{KN})^{-1}-\tau(pKL\sqrt{TKN})^{-1}}+\frac{\sigma^2}{KTN\tau(pK-\tau)}\right)$</tex-math></inline-formula>, that τ and p respectively represent the time window length and the connectivity of time-varying networks. Experimentally, we illustrate the robustness and effectiveness of this heterogeneity correction on extensive non-convex neural network training tasks over different topologies and dynamic network settings.

title: Enhancing Auditory Brainstem Response Extraction From Noised EEG With Adaptive Kalman Denoising Technique_supp1-3681602.pdf
authors: Xin Wang
abstract: Auditory brainstem response (ABR) is a weak evoked EEG signal that provides an objective measure for assessing auditory function. However, the traditional extraction method, namely, averaging is noise-sensitive and needs thousands of trials, which places high demands on the subjects and the experimental environment. Kalman weighted (KW) technique has the potential to extract ABR with high quality but relies heavily on expert experience for precise parameter tuning. In this article, an adaptive Kalman denoising technique, which can adaptively adjust the parameter, was developed. A comprehensive investigation was carried out on different noise types (pink noise/Gaussian white noise/uniform noise), proportions (20%/40%/60%/80%/100%), amplitudes (20/40/60/80 μV), and integrated manners (early-noised/intermittent-noised/late-noised). Multiple metrics, such as Pearson correlation coefficient, root mean square error, latency and amplitude of characteristics wave, and the wave recognition rate were calculated for evaluation. The simulation results showed that the proposed method outperformed the averaging and KW techniques over these evaluation metrics. Also, these evaluation metrics of the proposed method were much more stable than those of averaging the KW. Finally, we verified the proposed method in the real scenario. It is believed that the proposed method opens a window for daily ABR-based auditory health condition screening, which can benefit the early detection and diagnosis of auditory diseases.

title: Rochester Institute of Technology’s Open Community eXperiment (ROCX) 2025
authors: John P. Kerekes, Nina G. Raqueno, Dean N. Riley
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: TensorTouch: Calibration of Tactile Sensors for High Resolution Stress Tensor and Deformation for Dexterous Manipulation
authors: Won Kyung Do, Matthew Strong, Aiden Swann, Boshu Lei, Monroe Kennedy
abstract: Advanced dexterous manipulation requires identifying and controlling multiple simultaneous contacts, including interactions with compliant objects where deformations are large. Raw optical tactile images are information rich but lack calibrated physical meaning, limiting cross-sensor use and real-world deployment. We present TensorTouch, a calibration framework that combines finite element analysis and learning to infer dense deformation and stress/force fields from a single tactile image. Across real sensors, TensorTouch achieves contact localization errors under 1.29 mm and mean force errors under 0.139 N per axis. In a multi-object selective grasp task with two simultaneously contacted objects (including identical cables), the system achieves up to 90.0% success. We further demonstrate robustness under repeated loading, yielding 38.295 dB PSNR between the initial tactile image and the image after 20,000 contacts. The learned model runs in real time at 95 Hz on an RTX 5090, proving to be suitable for contact-rich, dexterous manipulation.

title: Forecasting River Water Levels At Virtual Stations of Altimetry Satellites_supp1-3681863.pdf
authors: Michal Halicki

title: IEEE Computer Society Grants for Emerging Technology Activities
authors: N/A

title: Intrusion Detection Model Using Fused Quantum NN–LeNet With Improved Elliptic Curve Cryptography Based Data Security
authors: V Kalyanasundaram, Sivakami Raja, S Kanthimathi
abstract: An intrusion detection system (IDS) is a network security tool originally designed to identify vulnerability exploits against a target program or computer. Once the attacker has access to the network, they can rapidly collect information. Hence, there is a need of intrusion detection system, which also extends to secure the data as well. This work focusses on a new DL-based IDS model along with data security assurance by deploying enhanced ECC model. Initially, the input data is pre-processed using min-max normalization process. Subsequently, Quantum based Hybrid model is introduced that combines the models like flatten layer proposed feature map with EVD-based QNN (FLPF with EVD-QNN) and LeNet models. Here, the final fused detection results are determined by Double Sigmoid Median min-max normalization-based Score Level Fusion (DSMMN-SLF). Subsequently to the intrusion detection, this work concentrates on secured data transmission, for which Enhanced Elliptic Curve Cryptography (EECC) is proposed that encrypts the data to assure its privacy. From analysis, the precision of detecting the intrusions using FLPF with EVD-QNN+ LNt was 97% which is high when comparing to existing methods.

title: Blockchain-Assisted Time-Bound Encrypted Matching for Trustworthy Agentic-AI Edge Networking
authors: Zhicheng Li, Jian Xu, Zexin Wang, Huiyang He, Jinyi Zhu, Fucai Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Agentic edge networking relies on autonomous agents running closed-loop perception-reasoning-planning pipelines to coordinate in real time. A central coordination primitive is to decide whether two exchanged risk descriptors refer to the same event, while preventing privacy leakage and ensuring trustworthy execution under zero-trust, multistakeholder participation. Public-key encryption with equality test (PKEET) enables matching over encrypted values, yet existing designs are poorly suited to agentic edge deployments because they often assume a designated tester or trusted authority, provide coarse delegation that amplifies token-leakage damage, and lack decentralized accountability against dishonest or selective execution. We propose Blockchain-assisted Time- Bound PKEET (BT-PKEET) that offers secure multi-agent coordination with decentralized trust and provenance. BTPKEET binds encryption and authorization to short time windows via PRF-derived window keys, and supports three least-privilege token types (ciphertext-bound, peer-bound, and event-bound) to constrain inference abuse and collusion surfaces in low-entropy descriptor spaces. We further develop a contract-friendly, unified on-chain verification equation that supports arbitrary token combinations and records auditready provenance for accountability. To enable continuous tracking without costly re-encryption or re-authorization, we introduce bridge tokens that incrementally refresh cross-window capability, with fast revocation by halting bridge publication. Experiments on a Hyperledger Fabric prototype show stable on-/off-chain Test overhead (7<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$times$</tex-math></inline-formula>-11<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$times$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and reduced window migration cost (1.7<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$times$</tex-math></inline-formula>-2.0<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$times$</tex-math></inline-formula>), achieving order-of-magnitude gains over pairing-heavy baselines while providing consortium-wide auditable coordination outcomes.

title: Run-to-run control of chemical mechanical polishing head with adjustable partition pressure based on deep reinforcement learning
authors: Yuxiang Li, Wentao Liu, Guoshan Zhang
abstract: The polishing head is one of the most important components in the chemical mechanical polishing (CMP) process, used to achieve an ultra-smooth surface on the wafer. Due to the effect of the retaining ring and the replacement of the polishing pad, the polishing process is subjected to drift and shift interference. The CMP head with partition pressure is designed to exert pressure separately according to the situation of the wafer edge being affected, and compensate for the disturbance caused by slight changes in different areas inside the wafer. A run-to-run (R2R) control method based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is proposed to adjust the process parameters between runs. A dual experience replay mechanism is adopted to effectively utilize the interactive information between the agent and the CMP environment designed. Simulation experiments are conducted under the conditions of pad drift and pad replacement. Results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance in MRR optimization. The WIWNU is optimized to a minimum of 0.3%, and the maximum difference of the polished wafer thickness under the two experimental conditions is below 25 nm. Thus, the proposed method can effectively compensate for the drift caused by equipment aging, and can sense environmental changes and adjust process parameters.

title: Specular-Aware Lambert Reconstruction for Monocular Endoscopic Depth Estimation
authors: Jingyang Shan, Shaolin Zhang, Tianyi Wang, Jun Wu, Xiaolin Qin
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Monocular endoscopic depth estimation benefits from the near-field lighting prior but remains fragile under specular highlights that violate photometric assumptions. We propose an optical-flow-guided Lambert surface reconstruction for robust highlight suppression and depth recovery. First, we introduce Frequency–Intensity Joint Prompting (FIJP) to generate reliable specular prompts by fusing high-frequency responses, brightness percentiles, and low-saturation cues, which guide SAM for fine masks. Second, we propose an optical-flow-guided Lambert surface reconstruction, where valid pixels are propagated across frames before inpainting, and LaMa is adapted to the endoscopic domain to suppress dark artifacts and preserve texture. Finally, we develop a temporal near-field light fusion framework that refines depth by combining per-pixel shading with inter-frame geometric consistency. Our method achieves superior highlight removal on C3VD, CoLON10K, and a clinical dataset, and obtains reliable depth results on C3VD, demonstrating robustness to specular reflections. The source code is available at https://github.com/sjyzy/LRED.

title: Acoustic Feedback Cancellation in Hearing Aids Exploiting an Inertial Sensor
authors: Sina Miran, Henning Schepker, Ivo Merks, Martin McKinney
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Acoustic feedback is a fundamental problem in hearing aids limiting the maximum applicable gain and causing artifacts such as whistling and howling. Adaptive feedback cancellation is one solution to increase the maximum applicable gain and reduce artifacts, where different adaptive filtering solutions can be used, e.g., the NLMS algorithm. Real-time adjustment of the adaptation speed, i.e., the step size, in these filters is crucial in obtaining both a small steady-state error and rapid convergence under significant feedback path changes. Existing step-size adjustment methods only operate on the audio signals in the hearing aid, and hence, are heavily susceptible to biases caused by characteristics of input audio. In this work, we propose to modify the adaptation speed based on head movement acceleration measured by an inertial measurement unit (IMU) in the hearing aid. Experimental results show that feedback path changes can be detected reliably from inertial measurements, and our approach outperforms audio-based adjustment methods in particular under steady-state conditions. Thus, our study encourages a multi-modal approach to feedback cancellation in hearing aids combining both inertial and audio signals.

title: A Low-Complexity Equalizer Design for OTFS Modulation in Doubly-Dispersive Channels
authors: Rabah Ouchikh, Thierry Chonavel, Abdeldjalil Aïssa-El-Bey, Mustapha Djeddou
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulation has emerged as a promising technique for reliable communication over rapidly time-varying channels, thanks to its robustness against Doppler effects and delay spread. However, the design of efficient equalizers for OTFS systems remains a key challenge due to the high computational complexity involved. In this paper, we propose a low-complexity linear equalizer for OTFS modulation that exploits the inherent structure of the effective channel matrix. Specifically, the proposed method leverages the block-circulant property and the sparse nature of the OTFS channel matrix and applies a Cholesky-based decomposition to significantly reduce computational cost. Simulation results confirm that the proposed equalizer achieves notable improvements in both bit error rate performance and computational efficiency, outperforming existing state-of-the-art techniques.

title: Audio Classification Models are Vulnerable to Filter Perturbations
authors: Justin Dettmer, Annelot Bosman, Igor Vatolkin, Holger Hoos
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Deep learning models are known to suffer from a lack of robustness to adversarial attacks. While the majority of research on the robustness of neural networks stems from the image classification domain, audio classifiers have also come under scrutiny, as their use in tasks such as speech processing is increasing. We introduce a novel adversarial attack method that creates band-pass filters as perturbations for mel spectrograms. These perturbations are closer to real-life conditions than conventionally created adversarial examples, as they can simulate the use of different recording devices or other acoustic properties. We demonstrate that state-of-the-art audio classifiers lack robustness against these adversarially-created filters, and we show that adversarial training with our attack method can help mitigate this effect on the widely studied NSynth, ESC-50 and SpeechCommands datasets.

title: Dataset-Driven Channel Masks in Transformers for Multivariate Time Series
authors: Seunghan Lee, Taeyoung Park, Kibok Lee
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Capturing channel dependency (CD) is essential for modeling multivariate time series (TS), and attention-based methods have been widely employed for this purpose. Nonetheless, these methods primarily focus on modifying the architecture, often neglecting the importance of dataset-specific characteristics. In this work, we introduce the concept of partial channel dependence (PCD) to enhance CD modeling in Transformer-based models by leveraging dataset-specific information to refine the CD captured by the model. To achieve PCD, we propose channel masks (CMs), which are integrated into the attention matrices of Transformers via element-wise multiplication. CMs consist of two components: 1) a similarity matrix that captures relationships between the channels, and 2) dataset-specific and learnable domain parameters that refine the similarity matrix. We validate the effectiveness of PCD across diverse tasks and datasets with various backbones.

title: RecSum: Reconstruct Complementary and Consistent Information in Multiplex Graph for Unsupervised Social Summarization
authors: Ruifang He, Lamu Songlang, Tanglong Zhao, Sen Yao, Bo Wang, Xiaohong Li
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Social summarization aims to obtain concise summaries that describe the core content of a collection of social texts (called posts) on a specific topic. Post content is short, and recent work has alleviated insufficient information in individual posts by introducing multiple relationships among them. However, contacting posts’ representations from different relationships directly can lead to redundancy and overlook the private information inherent in each relationship, which can be mitigated by extracting and fusing common and private information from different relationships. In this paper, we introduce RecSum, an unsupervised model for social summarization. RecSum reconstructs the clean common information of posts, as well as more-complementary and less-noisy private information from multiplex graph to obtain the post representation. It is then applied to a sparse reconstruction extractor that selects the most representative posts as summarization. Experiments on two real-world social media datasets demonstrate that RecSum outperforms other alternatives.

title: Adversarial Defense via Generative Speech Enhancement Module
authors: Chi-Tao Chen, Chun-Shien Lu, Jia-Ching Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Deep learning models excel in speech processing but remain highly vulnerable to adversarial attacks, posing critical risks in safety-sensitive applications. We introduce a defense framework that integrates Gaussian noise injection with a speech enhancement module to mitigate adversarial perturbations while preserving perceptual quality and semantic integrity. Evaluations on SC09, VCTK, and QKWS demonstrate that our approach achieves superior robustness against diverse white-box and black-box attacks, including PGD with varied steps, norm constraints, EOT budgets, and the query-based FakeBob. Additionally, our framework offers significantly faster inference and a compact 2M-parameter footprint, enabling deployment in real-time and resource-constrained settings.

title: PMSS-NeXt: An Efficient Polarized Multi-Scale Synergy Network for Complex Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Qingxue Zhao, Di Wu, Jiakang Gao, Siqi Wang, Jun Tian
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)

title: Diffface-Edit: a Diffusion-Based Facial Dataset for Forgery-Semantic Driven Deepfake Detection Analysis
authors: Feng Ding, Wenhui Yi, Xinan He, Mengyao Xiao, Jianfeng Xu, Jianqiang Du
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Generative models can now produce imperceptible, fine-grained facial edits, raising serious privacy risks. Yet existing AI-generated face datasets seldom cover subtle, region-level manipulations, and the effect of splice attacks between real and edited samples on detectors remains underexplored; we term these detector-evasive samples. To fill these gaps, we introduce DiffFace-Edit, a dataset with over two million fake images and edits across eight facial regions with diverse single- and multi-region combinations. We further analyze the impact of detector-evasive samples, provide a comprehensive dataset study, and propose a cross-domain IMDL-based evaluation. Dataset will be available at https://github.com/ywh1093/DiffFace-Edit.

title: AMFN:Adaptive Multi-view Fusion Network Framework
authors: Guanzheng Jiang, Zhimin Feng, Hui Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multi-view learning aims to fuse data from diverse sources or feature sets to obtain more discriminative representations. However, existing methods struggle with view heterogeneity, dynamic weight assignment, and noise robustness. To address these challenges, we propose an Adaptive Multi-view Fusion Network (AMFN), which enables efficient representation learning and fusion via learnable view-importance weights and joint optimization. AMFN first employs autoencoder-based modules preserve view-specific information, then integrates views using a cross-modal attention mechanism, and finally enhances feature discriminability through graph-based contrastive learning. Experiments on six benchmark datasets—including Prokaryotic, Synthetic3d, YouTubeFace, and the Caltech series—demonstrate that AMFN significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods clustering accuracy, normalized mutual information, and purity. Notably, it achieves 0.9421 accuracy on Caltech-5V (8.46% higher than the best baseline) and improves accuracy on YouTubeFace by 7.25%. These results confirm the effectiveness, robustness, and scalability of AMFN, offering new insights for multi-view learning research.

title:
MH-A
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    : Metropolis-Hastings Anomaly-Aware Augmentation For Contrastive Graph Anomaly Detection
authors: Maoji Wen, Chaohao Yuan, Jiacheng Hou, Ercan Engin Kuruoglu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Contrastive unsupervised graph anomaly detection (UGAD) hinges on augmented views. Fixed-strength, heuristic perturbations often erase rare signals or yield easy negatives: they tune a deletion budget rather than the node-perceived impact, and they do not adapt diversity as the encoder evolves. MH-A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> reframes augmentation as sampling from a target over views via a Metropolis–Hastings sampler. A K-hop message-accumulation statistic aligns each candidate’s effective strength to a desired level, preserving anomaly evidence; a global uncertainty signal sets the target’s variance, widening exploration when the model is unsure and tightening it when confident to avoid easy negatives. Sampling is coupled with learning in an accept-once, learn-once loop, keeping views matched to the current encoder. The plug-in is backbone-agnostic and leaves inference unchanged; attached to CoLA, ANEMONE, SL-GAD and GRADATE, it lifts ROC–AUC up to +1.84 with an average +0.93 across five benchmarks and yields hard-but-valid contrasts under standard UGAD protocols.

title: Cross-Modality Self-Supervision for Unimodal Physiological Signals Representation Learning
authors: Xiaomao Fan, Shuangdu Li, Jikui Liu, Jinzhou Cao, Jingyan Jiang, Yumeng Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Deep learning methods for physiological signals offer significant promise for medical diagnosis, health monitoring, and treatment guidance. However, their development is hampered by the reliance on large, high-quality annotated datasets. Self-supervised learning (SSL) provides a viable path forward by learning representations from unannotated data. Although existing SSL methods using token vocabularies have advanced representation learning for unannotated physiological data, their reliance on a single signal modality restricts the generality and performance of the resulting models. To overcome this limitation, we present a Cross-Modal Self-Supervision (CM-SSL) Learning framework designed to learn robust representations from single-modality physiological signals. This work specifically focuses on the prevalent modalities of Electrocardiogram (ECG) and Photoplethysmogram (PPG). The core advantage of CM-SSL is its construction of a shared token vocabulary from cross-modal signals, which enhances the quality and generality of the learned unimodal representations. Extensive evaluation across a diverse suite of downstream tasks demonstrates that the CM-SSL based unimodal model achieves superior performance over baseline models. The source code can be available at URL(https://github.com/ximosss/CM-SSL.git).

title: Let More Experts Speak: Balancing Exploration and Exploitation in PEFT for Mixture-of-Experts Models
authors: Yi-Zeng Fang, Juinn-Dar Huang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Large language models achieve remarkable performance across a wide range of NLP tasks, but their increasing scale makes full finetuning prohibitively expensive. Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) offers a lightweight alternative; however, most existing studies focus on dense transformer architectures, leaving its effectiveness on Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models largely unexplored. We identify a substantial performance gap between PEFT and full fine-tuning in MoE models, primarily caused by imbalanced expert activation and suboptimal capacity utilization. To bridge this gap, we introduce ExpertTUNE, a two-stage PEFT framework that explicitly balances exploration and exploitation in expert routing. The Warm-up Routing stage encourages broader expert activation, while the Focused Routing stage concentrates on refining the experts. Combined with LoRA, ExpertTUNE improves expert balance and consistently outperforms existing PEFT approaches. Specifically, it delivers gains of up to +3.17% on Switch Transformers-32 and +2.01% on OLMoE. Our results underscore the importance of exploration-aware PEFT strategies for MoE models.

title: PHPTrigger: Multi-Engine Assisted Vulnerability Auditing and Verification in PHP Applications
authors: Yayuan Zhang, Zhisheng Yang, Jiangyong Shi, Yongjun Wang, Jianzhe Yang, Xi Xuan, Hao Peng, Zhiyuan Jiang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of web applications and IoT firmware, PHP vulnerabilities have become a frequent and high-impact attack vector. Existing PHP vulnerability auditing approaches, which mainly rely on rule-based detection or precise causal modeling, often struggle to balance high recall with practical exploitability verification.To address this challenge, we propose PHPTrigger, an intelligent auditing framework that integrates multi-engine analysis with large language model reasoning. Without requiring complete causal modeling of vulnerabilities, PHPTrigger combines inter-procedural semantic reasoning with cross-modal semantic generation to automatically locate vulnerabilities and generate environment-adaptive PoCs.We implemented a prototype of PHPTrigger and systematically evaluated it on 150 vulnerabilities collected from 7 open-source projects. Experimental results show that, compared with the state-of-the-art tool ZIPPER, PHPTrigger improves recall by 46% and precision by 62%. For vulnerability verification, PHPTrigger achieved 100% executable PoC rate on 147 detected vulnerabilities. Moreover, PHPTrigger discovered 46 0-day vulnerabilities, of which 34 were assigned CVE identifiers. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and practical value of our approach in real-world vulnerability auditing and verification.

title: Compression meets Sampling: LZ78-SPA for Efficient Symbolic Music Generation
authors: Abhiram Gorle, Connor Ding, Sagnik Bhattacharya, Amit Kumar Singh Yadav, Tsachy Weissman
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Recent advances in symbolic music generation rely on deep learning models that achieve high-quality results but demand heavy compute, limiting scalability. We introduce LZMidi, a lightweight framework based on an LZ78-induced sequential probability assignment (SPA) that enables efficient CPU-only generation with minimal training and inference cost. First, beyond universal convergence results, we also provide finite-sample performance guarantees for LZ78-SPA. Second, on the Lakh MIDI dataset, we benchmark LZMidi against classical and deep generative baselines: achieving competitive Fréchet Audio Distance (FAD) and Wasserstein Distance (WD) while significantly reducing compute: with up to 30× faster training, 300× faster generation, and orders-of-magnitude lower energy. These results position universal compression as a scalable, theoretically grounded alternative to resource-intensive generative models.

title: Beamspace Model and Beam Alignment Method for Reconfigurable Holographic Surface Antenna Systems
authors: Ashna K K, Visa Koivunen
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Reconfigurable holographic surface (RHS) is a cost-effective and power-efficient multi-antenna solution to the emerging 6G and ISAC systems. Traditional beamforming methods can be challenging for RHS systems. Beamspace processing generates deterministic orthogonal beams that are allocated to different spatial processing tasks or form demanding beampatterns. In this paper, a novel approach for generating a beamspace of orthogonal beams in RHS systems is proposed. Moreover, a method for dynamically aligning these beams with actual target/user equipment (UE) directions is developed. The beam alignment problem operates on the Stiefel manifold to ensure orthogonality and reduce interference among the beams. Simulation results demonstrate that our proposed algorithm efficiently aligns the beamspace beams to the target/UE directions while maintaining the beams as orthogonal as possible. The algorithm provides significant signal-to-interference-noise-ratio (SINR) improvement for misaligned targets/UEs.

title: FLAME: Empowering Frozen LLMS for Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Bo Xue, Yi Xu, Bolei Ma, Yunchong Song, Jiaxin Ding, Luoyi Fu, Xinbing Wang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Traditional knowledge graph completion (KGC) methods rely solely on structural information and struggle with sparsity, while Large Language Models (LLMs) address these limitations through rich world knowledge and strong context modeling. Fine-tuning LLMs is effective but costly, while non-fine-tuned LLMs are efficient but suboptimal. To address this trade-off, we propose FLAME, a framework that extracts context-aware hidden states from intermediate layers of frozen LLMs to train data-efficient KGC classifiers. We bridge LLM-KG semantic gaps via subgraph-based entity descriptions and employ sliced mutual information (SMI) to quantify task-relevant information in representations. Experiments demonstrate that FLAME achieves 47% improvement over non-fine-tuned LLM baselines and, to our knowledge, is the first to achieve fine-tuned performance with 188× memory efficiency and 26.11× speedup.

title: Efficient Plug-and-Play Method for Dynamic Imaging Via Kalman Smoothing
authors: Benjamin Hawkes, Mike Davies, Víctor Elvira, Audrey Repetti
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: State-space models (SSM) are common in signal processing, where Kalman smoothing (KS) methods are state-of-the-art. However, traditional KS techniques lack expressivity as they do not incorporate spatial prior information. Recently, [1] proposed an ADMM algorithm that handles the state-space fidelity term with KS while regularizing the object via a sparsity-based prior with proximity operators. Plug-and-Play (PnP) methods are a popular type of iterative algorithms that replace proximal operators encoding prior knowledge with powerful denoisers such as deep neural networks. These methods are widely used in image processing, achieving state-of-the-art results. In this work, we build on the KS-ADMM method, combining it with deep learning to achieve higher expressivity. We propose a PnP algorithm based on KS-ADMM iterations, efficiently handling the SSM through KS, while enabling the use of powerful denoising networks. Simulations on a 2D+t imaging problem show that the proposed PnP-KS-ADMM algorithm improves the computational efficiency over standard PnP-ADMM for large numbers of timesteps.

title: H3GM: History-Guided Global Geometric Metric for Single Image to 3D Scene Generation
authors: Yuki Katayama, Ren Togo, Keisuke Maeda, Takahiro Ogawa, Miki Haseyama
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We propose H3GM, a novel no-reference metric for "single image to 3D scene generation". H3GM detects History-Dependent Contradiction (HDC)—incoherence that arises when a previously observed viewpoint is revisited—and quantifies global geometric coherence using only the generated frames. Quantitative detection of HDCs has been difficult for existing no-reference metrics, which are typically limited by either a reliance on 2D appearance or a confinement to local, adjacent-frame evaluation. We address these limitations by sequentially reconstructing the generated sequence to build a single common coordinate system. By then restricting our evaluation to geometrically overlapping frame pairs, we robustly measure the scene’s global geometric coherence. In this work, we advocate for HDC as a critical aspect for the quantitative evaluation of generated 3D scenes.

title: Fake Image Detection on Noise Residual Spectra Via Random-Feature Single-Layer Neural Networks
authors: Emanuele Mele, Angelo Coluccia
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Discriminating between real and AI-generated images in the frequency domain (noise residual spectra) is typically addressed via deep neural networks. This paper investigates instead the use of a single-layer neural network with random features, so-called Extreme Learning Machine (ELM), in which the weights between input and hidden layer are randomly initialized and not updated during training. Compared to deep networks, this offers significant advantages in terms of complexity, while maintaining good accuracy. We show that, based on asymptotic formulas from random matrix theory, hyperparameter optimization can be conducted in closed form, so greatly reducing its computational cost. In terms of classification results, our experiments with popular generative AI algorithms show competitive performance. Ablation study confirms that detection based on noise residual spectra are much more accurate compared to pixel-domain detection.

title: LDINET: A Lightweight Dual Domain Interaction Network for Human Pose Estimation
authors: Xuping Liang, Yanxia Wang, Jing Luo, Guoyu Xia, Xinliang Liu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Human pose estimation is one of the key tasks in the field of computer vision. However, achieving a balance between model parameter volume, computational complexity, and human pose estimation accuracy remains a challenge for current pose estimation models. To address the challenge, we propose a novel lightweight dual domain interaction network for human pose estimation (LDINet), which contains three innovative modules. First, a High Frequency Filtering Module (HFFM) is designed, which high-frequency responses generated by a high-pass filter are used as mask weights to enhance the small keypoint features in the original feature map along the spatial dimension. Second, a Spatial Perception Module (SPM) is proposed to capture spatial dependencies that are lacking in the backbone network’s output stage. Finally, a Frequency Domain Decoder (FDD) is introduced to enhance frequency domain feature extraction capabilities. Extensive experiments on the MSCOCO 2017 and MPII datasets validate the performance of LDINet, and ablation studies on the MPII dataset confirm the effectiveness of HFFM, SPM, and FDD.

title: KGER: Knowledge Graph Error Detection and Refinement with Reinforcement Learning
authors: Aishan Maoliniyazi, Chaohong Ma, Xiaofeng Meng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) are essential for AI applications. However, pervasive KG errors substantially impact their performance. Existing error correction methods often distinguish detection from refinement, underutilize KG embeddings, and largely depend on scarce labeled data for generalization and scalability. This paper introduces KGER, a novel approach for knowledge graph error detection and refinement with reinforcement learning. KGER frames KG detection and refinement as a unified reinforcement learning process. It employs a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent that integrates KG embeddings and uses a staged training approach. This allows it to learn robust correction policies applicable across varying levels of supervision, from supervised to unsupervised settings. Evaluations on various datasets show that KGER effectively identifies and refines erroneous KG triples, enhances knowledge graph quality, and outperforms existing methods. The code and data are available at https://github.com/kazgu/KGER.

title: Active Sensing Based Beam Alignment for Backscatter Communication
authors: Xiyu Wang, Xiao Cai, Hei Victor Cheng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper addresses the beam alignment problem for mono-static full-duplex backscatter communication systems. This is crucial for an access point (AP) to acquire data from a backscatter device (BD) without either information on the BD location or channel state information. Conventional beam alignment methods, including beam sweeping and channel estimation, require long preambles to achieve acceptable performance. To address this challenge, this work proposes an active sensing based method in which the AP actively interacts with the environment and uses historical observations collected thus far to adaptively adjust the beamforming vectors, thereby maximizing the signal-to-noise ratio (SINR) of the BD signal. Specifically, a long-short-term memory (LSTM) network is exploited to capture correlations over different observations, and deep neural networks are employed to map the hidden state to beamforming vectors. Simulation results demonstrate that the active sensing based method effectively optimizes the beamforming vectors that maximize SINR with only a few interactions with the environment, thus facilitating subsequent BD data transmission.

title: Enhancing Knowledge base Question Answering with Reinforced Hop-Wise Logical form Generation
authors: Jinghua Tang, Jian Cao, Jianqi Gao, Ranran Bu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Knowledge-Based Question Answering (KBQA) focuses on extracting accurate answers from structured knowledge bases (KBs) to address natural language queries. The semantic parsing approach has become a dominant methodology in this domain, delivering impressive results by converting user questions into formal query structures, specifically logical forms (LFs). Despite the adoption of large language models (LLMs) for LF generation, these representations may remain non-executable owing to model hallucinations and the inherent complexity of KBQA tasks. To solve the above problems and inspired by autoregressive token-wise generation and boosting strategy, we propose a novel learning framework, ReSON (Reinforced Hop-wise Search for Logical Form Generation). ReSON first generates a hop-by-hop search path (preliminary logical form, PLF) for each topic entity, similar to autoregressive token-wise content generation. It then effectively integrates each PLF to generate a final refined query statement (refined logical form, RLF). Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that ReSON significantly improves the performance of LLMs on KBQA tasks.

title: Adversarial Label Recovery with Multi-Modal Fusion and Dual-Task Contrastive Learning
authors: Haoran Fu, Wenhui Cheng, Chundong Wang, Lijun Shu, Zhiyong Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Adversarial examples pose serious threats to the reliability of AI systems, especially in safety-critical applications. Existing detection methods often exhibit poor robustness, undermining their trustworthiness under adversarial attacks. To address this challenge, we propose a multi-modal feature-fusion framework for adversarial label recovery. Our framework integrates visual–semantic embeddings from CLIP, spatial representations from VGG16, gradient-based structural features, and frequency-domain signatures. These heterogeneous features are unified through a transformer architecture that captures cross-modal dependencies, yielding a more discriminative and robust representation space. The framework is optimized with a dual-task contrastive learning scheme, jointly performing image classification for label recovery and adversarial detection. The detection module is further reinforced with contrastive learning to improve discriminability. Extensive experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art label recovery accuracy on CIFAR-10, significantly surpassing existing approaches.

title: Collaborative Graph Contrastive Network for Semi-Supervised Graph Node Classification
authors: Xiaoyong Huang, Heli Sun, Guanlan Yue, Pan Lou, Liang He
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Semi-supervised node classification reduces reliance on labeled data. While GNNs (e.g., GCN, GAT) exploit local structures, they often miss global topology; contrastive methods (e.g., MVGRL) face rigid embeddings or poor view design. We propose the Collaborative Graph Contrastive Network (CGCN) to jointly capture local and global semantics via a dual-encoder framework with cross-view alignment. A fine-grained collaborative constraint preserves node similarity by softly regularizing cross-view correlation matrices, avoiding over-compression. Additionally, a category-aware contrastive loss enhances class compactness and separation with minimal labels. Experiments on six benchmarks show CGCN outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Ablation studies validate each component and demonstrate robustness under complex topologies.

title: GRASP: Group-Shapley Feature Selection for Patients
authors: Yuheng Luo, Shuyan Li, Zhong Cao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Feature selection remains a major challenge in medical prediction, where existing approaches such as LASSO often lack robustness and interpretability. We introduce GRASP, a novel framework that couples Shapley value–driven attribution with group-L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</inf> regularization to extract compact and non-redundant feature sets. GRASP first distills group-level importance scores from a pre-trained tree model via SHAP, then enforces structured sparsity through groupL<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</inf>–regularized logistic regression, yielding stable and interpretable selections. Extensive comparisons with LASSO, SHAP, and deep learning–based methods show that GRASP consistently delivers comparable or superior predictive accuracy, while identifying fewer, less redundant, and more stable features.

title: Text2Try3D: Text-Guided 3D Garment Generation on Parametric Human Models
authors: Weilong Peng, Xi Chen, Meng Xing, Keke Tang, Meie Fang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Text-driven 3D human modeling has emerged as a powerful paradigm for controllable avatar creation, with growing applications in virtual try-on, gaming, and digital content production. However, existing generative methods often entangle identity, pose, and clothing representation, leading to limited garment controllability and weak structural consistency. We introduce Text2Try3D, a modular framework that disentangles appearance synthesis and geometry refinement for customizable clothed human avatars. Our approach leverages a UV-structured diffusion prior to generate high-fidelity textures from text, and applies score-based optimization to align mesh geometry with the synthesized appearance. Built upon parametric models like SMPL, it ensures compatibility with animation pipelines while enabling realistic and structured garment generation. Experiments demonstrate strong performance in appearance quality, geometric alignment, and prompt consistency.

title: Graph Distribution-Valued Signals: A Wasserstein Space Perspective
authors: Yanan Zhao, Feng Ji, Xingchao Jian, Wee Peng Tay
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: We introduce a novel framework for graph signal processing (GSP) that models signals as graph distribution-valued signals (GDSs), which are probability distributions in the Wasserstein space. This approach overcomes key limitations of classical vector-based GSP, including the assumption of synchronous observations over vertices, the inability to capture uncertainty, and the requirement for strict correspondence in graph filtering. By representing signals as distributions, GDSs naturally encode uncertainty and stochasticity, while strictly generalizing traditional graph signals. We establish a systematic dictionary mapping core GSP concepts to their GDS counterparts, demonstrating that classical definitions are recovered as special cases. The effectiveness of the framework is validated through graph filter learning for prediction tasks, supported by experimental results.

title: Online Time-Series Contrastive Learning for Soft Sensing of Biopharmaceutical Processes
authors: Yinlong Li, Licheng Pan, Hu Xu, Xinggao Liu
abstract: Biopharmaceutical processes typically generate abundant high-frequency online sensor data but suffer from sparse and delayed offline quality measurements, creating a “data-rich but label-poor” dilemma that hinders effective process monitoring. Furthermore, these processes are subject to significant distribution shifts due to batch-to-batch variability and time-varying metabolic states. To address these challenges, this article proposes a novel online time-series contrastive learning framework for soft sensing, validated on an industrial erythromycin fermentation process. We introduce a mechanism-informed soft contrastive learning strategy that utilizes information derived from domain knowledge to construct instance-level and temporal similarity matrices, guiding the model to learn physically meaningful representations from unlabeled data. In addition, we develop a dual-timescale online learning architecture comprising a slow branch for robust representation learning and a fast branch for real-time adaptation. Experimental results on a large-scale industrial dataset demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms state-of-the-art time-series contrastive learning baselines, particularly in predicting complex rheological indicators like broth viscosity under temporal distribution shifts.

title: History-of-Error Neural Network Disturbance Rejection With Application to Motion Control of a Cylindrical Robot
authors: Samira Asadi, Amin Farjah, Mehrdad Moallem
abstract: Precision motion control is essential for the reliable and efficient operation of industrial and autonomous systems. This article presents an adaptive neural network (NN) controller that achieves high-accuracy trajectory tracking and disturbance rejection in the presence of nonlinear effects, such as cogging and friction forces. The key contribution lies in incorporating a history-of-error learning mechanism into the NN objective function, enabling the controller to adapt based on both instantaneous and past tracking dynamics. This temporal formulation enhances learning efficiency and disturbance suppression compared to conventional instantaneous-error methods. The controller employs a single-layer feedforward NN to estimate unknown dynamics, with a modified backpropagation algorithm and e-modification ensuring stable weight adaptation. A Lyapunov-based stability analysis is presented that guarantees bounded tracking errors and weight convergence. The resulting control law achieves robust real-time implementation without compromising computational efficiency. Experimental results on a cylindrical robot subjected to friction and cogging torque disturbances show over 60% improvement in tracking accuracy and up to 40% enhancement in disturbance rejection relative to NN and proportional integral derivative (PID) baselines. These results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed history-enhanced adaptive neural control framework for precision motion systems.

title: TransSeg: 3-D Semantic Segmentation Based on 2D–3D Trans-Modal Fusion for Autonomous Driving
authors: Mingxi Zhuang, Guangming Wang, Hesheng Wang
abstract: With the rapid development of multimodal data fusion based on deep learning, semantic segmentation using the complementary information from the camera and LiDAR sensor can enhance the environmental perception of autonomous driving. However, most fusion methods overlook critical issues arising during the fusion process, including: first, insufficient fusion effectiveness in one-to-one scenarios due to a lack of precise feature alignment; second, low efficiency in the fusion process and a large computational burden on the model. To address these issues, we propose a trans-modal fusion method based on state space model and deformable transformer module. The proposed trans-modal fusion method makes use of the 2-D prior information of the image, such as texture and appearance information. By distilling knowledge from fused 2D–3D representations, it significantly enhances the feature extraction capability of the 3-D point cloud. First, we design a lightweight global gap adjustment attention module based on a linear state-space model, named global gap attention, to better bridge the information gap between point clouds and images. Then, to enhance the efficiency of trans-modal fusion, we adopt a local cross fusion module, which utilizes a many-to-many fusion approach to achieve more accurate feature alignment. Finally, to strengthen the channel information of point cloud features, we introduce an efficient channel attention during the feature extraction process. The experimental results show that the proposed method achieves a better segmentation accuracy compared with baseline. We show superiority of our method on two popular urban datasets SemanticKITTI and nuScenes (achieving mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) scores of 70.9% and 80.4%, respectively).

title: Synergistic regulation of polar groups and weak conjugation: preparation of polyimide and improvement of high-temperature energy storage performance
authors: Houyu Wang, Ling Weng, Xiaoming Wang, Xue Li, Lizhu Guan, Lin Zhang, Yingtang Zhang, Li Kong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Polymer film capacitors are widely used in electronic power systems due to their high power density, good self-healing ability, and ease of large-scale production. With the rapid progress of new energy vehicles, aerospace and other fields, there is an urgent need for polymer film capacitors that can work stably under extreme conditions such as high temperature and high electric field. Under the synergistic effect of multiple physical fields such as thermal and electric fields, the electrical conductivity loss of traditional polymer films significantly increases, resulting in a sharp decline in their insulation performance and energy storage characteristics. To overcome this limitation, this study introduces different types and contents of cycloaliphatic structures into polyimide to regulate the π-π conjugation effect within its chain. Then, by controlling the reaction kinetics to retain the polar groups in polyimide, the synergistic effect of cycloaliphatic structure and polar groups is achieved to enhance the energy storage performance of the polymer at high temperatures. Compared with traditional aromatic materials, these materials exhibit significantly improved discharge energy density (U<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>) and efficiency (η) under high temperature conditions. It is worth noting that the semi cyclic PI copolymer achieves an excellent energy density of 6.57 J/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> and an efficiency of 88.68 % at 150 ℃; it achieves an excellent energy density of 4.79 J/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> and an efficiency of 82 % at 200 ℃.
