abstract: Synthetic aperture radar automatic target recognition (SAR ATR) aims to classify targets such as vehicles, ships, and aircraft in complex remote sensing environments. A major challenge in SAR ATR is clutter, which biases recognition toward spurious background signals and significantly limits robustness under extended operating conditions. To address this issue, we propose UDT, a novel unsupervised feature-level clutter reduction module that requires no target mask annotations. UDT adopts a dual-path structure: a gating path that reduces clutter and localizes targets, and a magnitude path that amplifies target informations to enhance discriminability. Furthermore, a residual compensation mechanism progressively strengthens target representations across layers, ensuring stable feature learning. Extensive experiments demonstrate that UDT effectively reduces clutter while preserving essential features, improving ATR performance under diverse operating conditions.

title: A Novel Self-Correcting Direct Position Determination in Asynchronous Sensor Networks
authors: Yuan Zhang, Zihan Yao, Guizhou Wu, Fucheng Guo, Xi Li, Min Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper presents a novel self-calibration direct position determination (SC-DPD) method in asynchronous sensor networks without the aid of anchor sources. By leveraging the consistency of asynchronous clock bias across different intercepted signal batches, the localization cost function is derived to effectively mitigate asynchronous effects. Simulation results demonstrate enhanced performance of the proposed method over existing studies: (1) superior localization accuracy, particularly in low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) conditions, (2) lower system complexity through anchor-free operation, (3) reduced computational complexity without clock bias estimation in the asynchronous sensor networks. The proposed method bridges the gap between theoretical DPD frameworks and practical deployment constraints, particularly for large-scale sensor networks.

title: Near-Field Channel Estimation and Localization with Coprime Arrays
authors: Rafik Guellil, Karim Abed-Meraim, Adel Belouchrani
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper addresses uplink near-field, semi-blind, channel estimation and localization for Extremely Large Coprime Antenna Arrays explicitly accounting for multipath propagation. We develop a subspace-based estimation strategy that jointly exploits pilot and data symbols to recover channel responses and localization parameters. Our approach is compared to the conventional Uniform Linear Array (ULA). While ULAs provide slightly better accuracy under ideal conditions with the same aperture, Coprime Arrays (CAs) require significantly fewer elements, reducing computational complexity, and are more robust to mutual coupling (MC). Overall, CAs provide a practical and efficient solution for near-field channel estimation and localization for 6G communication systems.

title: Ultralight IPM-DAE: An Ultra-Lightweight ECG Denoising Autoencoder Via Parallel Mamba and Multi-Scale Fusion
authors: Ruizhe Wang, Zhanyi Liu, Honghao Li, Jiaqi Liang, Jie Sun, Min Xiang, Xiaolin Ning
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) is an important tool for diagnosing cardiovascular diseases. Clinically acquired ECG signals are often contaminated by various types of noise, severely impairing subsequent analysis. However, traditional methods are limited in denoising long-term complex ECG signals and often struggle to balance performance and efficiency. Therefore, we propose an ultra-lightweight denoising autoencoder (Ultralight IPM-DAE) for efficiently removing mixed noise in ECG signals. Specifically, Ultralight IPM-DAE integrates a parallel Mamba based on independent state matrices and a multi-scale information fusion module. The former is used to enhance the model’s ability to capture long-range dependencies in ECG signals, while the latter facilitates the integration and complementation of multi-scale information for more comprehensive feature representation. Experimental results demonstrate that Ultralight IPM-DAE achieves superior denoising performance with lower computational complexity, indicating higher clinical acceptability and validating the potential of Mamba as an ultra-lightweight backbone for electrophysiological signal analysis. The source code is available at: https://github.com/RZWangbuaa/Ultralight-IPM-DAE.

title: Multilayer LTCC Bandpass Filter Using Broadside Coupled Stripline with Microstrip Feed
authors: Sayyad Shahwaz Ali, Dillibabu E
abstract: A compact three-layer broadside coupled stripline resonator is used to design an interdigital bandpass filter with a microstrip feed. Multi-folds structure of the resonator is used to introduce strong capacitive coupling to reduce the overall size of the filter. A comparison of the resonant frequency analysis between a single-layer resonator and a three-layer resonator is carried out using EM simulation and further verified by analyzing with electrical equivalent circuits. Filter prototype is fabricated using low temperature co-fired ceramic (LTCC) technology. Measured results of the filter show return loss better than 10 dB and maximum insertion loss of 3 dB over the band over the band from 1 GHz to 1.4 GHz.

title: Hybrid Power Supply of Alarm Stations in the Surveillance, Alert and Warning Systems
authors: Željko V.Despotović, Marko.R. Tajdić
abstract: Surveillance, alert and warning systems (SAWS) have the task of carrying out all necessary measures in accordance with legal regulations and rules in order to monitor, notify, and if necessary, partially or generally alert the population in the endangered area. The basic element of the SAWS is the alarm station, which is based on alarm sirens and the associated electronic and telecommunication system. The power supply of the alarm station is very important for the reliable functioning of the entire system. The power supply in these cases is usually obtained from a permanent mains connection <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$230 ~\mathrm{V}, 50 ~\text{Hz}$</tex>. In the event that the connection is not permanent but occasional (as is very often the case in practice), it is necessary to base the power supply system on a hybrid power supply, which is a combination of mains power supply and power supply from renewable energy sources (RES). The most common combination of this type of power supply is mains and photovoltaic (PV) power. This paper presents the implementation of PV power, which was realized as part of the hybrid power supply of the alarm station in the flood wave protection system at the “Bovan” dam in the municipality of Aleksinac.

title: Wavelet Coherence Analysis of Sensor Location Effects on OLTC Vibrations in Power Transformers
authors: Srđan Jokić, Petar Matić, Budimir Kovačević, Bojana Čolić
abstract: Vibration measurement of on-load tap changers (OLTCs) offers a non-invasive means of assessing their mechanical condition, but the diagnostic value of these measurements is often limited by structural resonances, oil-borne attenuation, and the complex dynamics of the OLTC mechanism. Because vibration responses are highly sensitive to sensor placement, understanding how measurement location influences signal characteristics is essential for reliable interpretation. This study investigates vibration signals recorded at multiple positions on an OLTC and analyses their relationships using wavelet coherence, enabling time-frequency correlation and phase relationship assessment. The results reveal clear location-dependent coherence patterns, demonstrate the influence of resonant structural modes and burst events, and show that mechanical interactions within the OLTC drive system are reflected in the motor stator current. The results provide a basis for improved sensor placement and more reliable interpretation of OLTC vibration diagnostics.

title: Microservices Optimization with Container Orchestration and Auto-Scaling Strategies in Cloud Environments
authors: Arun Kumar Rajamandrapu, Aparna Krishna Penumatcha, Surya Teja Meesala, Ajay Krishna Borra, Nagireddy Vasipally, Sathish Kuppan Pandurangan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Microservices have become the dominant framework for developing scalable, resilient cloud-native applications, albeit optimizing such systems under workloads that are inherently dynamic is surprisingly difficult. Current orchestration and auto-scaling approaches are typically static threshold-based, resulting in inefficient uses of cost, latency, or energy. This paper addresses the problem with an adaptive approach that combines container orchestration, workload forecasting, and reinforcement-learning defined auto-scaling. A heterogeneous Kubernetes cluster was used to evaluate the approach against 5 state-of-the-art methods for an e-commerce application workload. The framework delivered a 20% latency reduction, a 15% throughput increase, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times$</tex> faster provisioning signal (or scale) load response, a 12% lower cost, and SLA compliance of 99.5%. The results demonstrate how intelligent orchestration and predictive workload scaling could address the challenges that cloud-native resilience, efficiency, and sustainability pose. Building from the conclusions, proactive, AI-based frameworks can deliver improved performance for cloud-native systems and help motivate further research in the fields of multi-cloud, edge-cloud, and energy-aware orchestration.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 27th International Symposium on Symbolic and Numeric Algorithms for Scientific Computing (SYNASC)

title: Comparison of ROS 2 QoS Policies for Real-Time Quadruped Robot Control
authors: Gokhan Erdemir
abstract: Real-time legged robot control requires communication mechanisms that balance reliability with strict timing determinism. In high-frequency closed-loop systems, even moderate latency variance can degrade tracking performance and compromise locomotion stability. ROS 2, built on the Data Distribution Service (DDS) standard, provides configurable Quality of Service (QoS) policies that allow fine-grained control over communication behavior. However, selecting appropriate QoS profiles for control-critical data streams remains a practical challenge in wireless robotic deployments. This study presents an experimental evaluation of ROS 2 QoS configurations for real-time quadruped robot control using a Unitree Go2 EDU platform operating at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ H z}$</tex>. Rather than evaluating communication streams in isolation, the analysis focuses specifically on control-critical feedback and command streams within the closed-loop pipeline. Two representative scenarios are considered: high computational load under stable wireless conditions and degraded wireless communication with induced packet loss and jitter. System performance is quantified by end-to-end latency, jitter, packet loss, deadline miss ratio, and control-level metrics such as RMSE, overshoot, and settling time. Experimental results demonstrate that latency dispersion, particularly tail latency and jitter, has a stronger impact on control stability than packet delivery guarantees alone. Best-Effort configurations with limited buffering achieved more consistent timing behavior and improved transient response than strictly Reliable modes under stressed conditions. The findings highlight the importance of timing-aware and stream-specific QoS tuning to preserve stability margins in high-frequency legged robotic systems.

title: Extended Phase Shift Modulation for Dual Active Bridge Converters in Battery-Based DC Microgrids
authors: Kecheng Zhao, Priya Singh Bhakar, Shamsodin Taheri, Edris Pouresmaeil
abstract: With the rapid advancement of power electronic devices and high penetration of renewable energy sources and battery energy storage systems, the dual active bridge converter is widely adopted in DC microgrids. It provides high voltage gain, soft switching capability, and galvanic isolation between the primary and secondary sides. Among different modulation techniques, extended phase shift modulation has been shown to effectively control power losses and improve energy transfer performance in dual active bridge converters. In this paper, an extended phase shift modulation method presented that continuously adjusts the switching pulses of a converter enable power transfer from a battery to a DC bus. The proposed approach provides the optimal internal and external phase shifts corresponding to the reduced inductor peak current through peak current control. A feedforward control mechanism maps the phase shifts to their associated current references. These references are processed through an inductor current comparator and logic circuitry to generate switching pulses. This modulation improves power transfer efficiency and reduces inductor peak current when compared with conventional single phase shift control. The advantage of the proposed approach is verified by simulations results conducted in the MATLAB/Simulink environment.

title: $\mathbb{F}_{q}$-Linear Qudit Data Syndrome Codes
authors: Goli Nagamani, Pradeep Kiran Sarvepalli
abstract: Quantum codes which can correct both errors in the data as well as in the syndromes are called quantum data syndrome codes. An important framework for constructing quantum data syndrome codes is due to Ashikhmin et al. Their approach can be used to construct a quantum data syndrome code for any stabilizer code. While some constructions are known there are not many families of quantum data syndrome codes known. Furthermore, there has not been much work in the construction of qudit data syndrome codes. In this paper, we extend the framework of Ashikhmin et al. to the qudit setting focusing on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbb{F}_{q}$</tex>-linear codes. Then we propose a systematic construction for CSS type data syndrome codes. We then construct a few families of data syndrome codes based on cyclic codes including the ReedSolomon codes, and BCH codes.

title: Comparative Benchmarking of 2D LiDAR SLAM Algorithms with ROS 2 on Raspberry Pi 5
authors: Ulas Sahin, Gokturk Can, Ezgi Altiok, Ibrahim Cavdar, Necip Gozuacik
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: Mobile robotics increasingly relies on SLAM for robust autonomous navigation. While many algorithms exist, systematic comparisons within the ROS 2 framework under real-world conditions remain limited. This study addresses this gap by benchmarking three widely used 2D LiDAR-based methods-GMapping, Hector SLAM, and Cartographer-on a wheeled mobile robot. Using both simulation and on-device experiments, we evaluate mapping accuracy, localization quality, and computational efficiency. Results show that Cartographer achieves the highest accuracy in structured environments, Hector SLAM demonstrates robustness without odometry, and GMapping performs reliably only in small-scale settings. These findings highlight trade-offs relevant to embedded deployment. The main contributions are: (i) a reproducible evaluation pipeline on ROS 2, (ii) quantitative analysis of accuracy versus resource usage on Raspberry Pi 5, and (iii) practical guidelines for algorithm selection in autonomous systems. This work advances the understanding of ROS 2-based SLAM and supports informed deployment in robotics applications.

title: Cactus-inspired ionic liquid functionalized MOF-based humidity sensor for high response ultra-low humidity detection_supp1-3684636.pdf
authors: Ke Wu
abstract: Accurate monitoring of low humidity exhibits critical application value in semiconductor manufacturing, precision agriculture and related fields. However, most reported low humidity sensors commonly face the limitations of low response value and linearity within ultra-low humidity range (<10% RH). Herein, we develop a cactus-inspired ionic liquids (ILs) modified metal organic frameworks (MOFs), in which the UiO-66-2SH was prepared through solvothermal method firstly, and then the IL with specific length was introduced via click reaction, retaining the pore structure and enhancing the hydrophilicity and ionic conductivity of UiO-66. The optimized sensor exhibits excellent sensing performance in the ultra-low humidity range of 0.02–10.3% RH, showing high response of 23.2, narrow humidity hysteresis of 0.6% RH, good linearity (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.994), and fast response time of 23.0 s. The sensing mechanism of the sensor is discussed combined with water molecule adsorption process and proton conduction. Furthermore, the potential application of the sensor in water source location is demonstrated. This work provides a promising sensitive material for high performance ultra-low humidity detection.

title: DOA Estimation Exploiting Moving Sparse Uniform Linear Array With Coprime Frequencies
authors: Long Zhang, Sheng Wang, Chengcheng Liu, Guan Gui
abstract: This paper presents an innovative aperture synthesis strategy for moving sparse arrays with coprime frequencies, effectively integrating array motion and frequency diversity to significantly enhance the virtual array aperture. The proposed synthesis method achieves a substantial increase in virtual array elements compared to existing sparse array aperture synthesis techniques. A closed-form expression for the distributions of the synthetic virtual array is derived to characterize its structural properties. Building on this foundation, a direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation method is developed within a group sparse framework, effectively leveraging the phase differences induced by coprime frequencies. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing aperture synthesis techniques in both DOA estimation accuracy and achievable degrees of freedom (DOFs), highlighting its superiority for high-resolution array signal processing.

title: A Minimal Hardware System For Impedance Measurement
authors: Paolo Carbone, Alessio De Angelis
abstract: This article describes the realization of a low-complexity impedance measurement system. Besides the analog part designed to realize a voltage-controlled current source, data acquisition is based on one-bit quantization. The acquisition framework is fully described. It extends a known technique for estimating sine wave parameters from one-bit quantized data by including the available a priori information on the properties of the dither signal. The realized instrument is applied to the two-wire measurement of the dynamic impedance of a ${\bf 18650}$ rechargeable battery. Experimental results are compared with impedance measurements based on a full-resolution quantizer and an LCR meter on a reference impedance, showing good agreement while achieving significantly lower complexity.

title: Camera-Trajectory-Driven Signed Distance Fields for Automated Object Isolation
authors: Yusuf Şevki Günaydın, Emre Demirbaş, Ömer Faruk Özüyağlı, Fatih Nar
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: Accurate isolated object reconstruction from drone footage is often challenging because of background clutter, sparse camera coverage, and viewpoint limitations. We introduce a geometric method in this study that leverages the drone's camera positions and viewing directions, extracted from Structure-fromMotion, to construct a kernel-based Signed Distance Field. This field explicitly encodes whether points lie inside, on, or outside the drone's flight route, providing a precise spatial representation of the trajectory. By evaluating the SDF at candidate points, the method can identify regions corresponding to the object of interest versus the surrounding environment. In typical SfM pipelines such as COLMAP, unbounded scenes or spiral drone captures often lead to sparse reconstructions that unintentionally include distant background structures, which in turn causes Gaussian Splats to represent unwanted terrain or sky regions. Our trajectory-aligned SDF mitigates this issue by enabling posttraining filtering of Gaussian Splat reconstructions, removing background splats (e.g., terrain/sky/background buildings) without changing the original splatting pipeline. We validate the approach on synthetic spiral trajectories that include wind and sensor disturbances, as well as on real drone flights. Results demonstrate that the field generates smooth, consistent spatial representations, offering a foundation for trajectory-informed 3-Dimensional reconstruction, automated object isolation, and targeted scene editing. This method provides a practical and flexible framework for future drone-based data capture and geometric analysis.

title: Impact of Employer Branding Determinants on Employee Retention: A PLS-SEM Approach
authors: Jayalakshmi K, Preetha Subrahmanyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: The retention of employees has emerged as a dilemma to the organizations in the urban areas like Chennai where talent mobility is heightened by intense competition in the workplace. The paper address how relationship exists between the major employer-branding predictors that include Organizational Leadership (OL), Corporate Reputation (CR), and Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) and employee retention intentions. The study based on the data of 255 employees who are employed in various organizations in Chennai adopts Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA) and Structural Equation Modelling Partial Least Squares (PLS-SEM) to test the measurement model, and to test the structural relationships. Findings indicate that there is high reliability, convergent, and discriminant validity between all the constructs with leadership being the most predictive of employee retention, followed by corporate reputation and CSR. The model identifies 35.5% variance in the retention intention, which proves that employer branding is a strong strategic tool to stabilize the workforce. The results not only provide theoretical contributions to the Social Exchange Theory and Signaling Theory but also offer practical implications of organizational retention practices in Chennai where leadership development, CSR integration, and reputation-building activities can be suggested.

title: Explainable Lightweight Deep Learning-Based Intrusion Detection for Healthcare 4.0 Using Biomedical and Network Signals
authors: Othman Saad Salman, Anas Abdulsatar Faraj, Mohanad Mahmood Abdulraheem, Shokhan M. Al-Barzinji, Abdlelah Abdlatef Abdlhamed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Healthcare 4.0 using the Internet of Things (IoT) has seen a rapid pace of development, and as a result, experts are showing an increasing level of interest in the field of network security. The use of deep learning (DL) has greatly improved the effectiveness of Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDSs). Nevertheless, due to the complex nature and the “black box” issue that arises when it comes to implementing DL-based NIDS models in real-world situations, there are several difficulties that must be addressed, such as the requirement for a lightweight design and the interpretability of the model. This study proposed the novel approach of the NIDS system for healthcare 4.0. The model consists of network and biomedical signal acquisition, automatic FL-based feature engineering, and SoftMax-based classification with its interpretation. The automatic feature engineering is performed by using lightweight DL layers followed by the Feature Selection (FS) to reduce the dimensionality and computational overhead. The SoftMax classifier is performed to detect the intrusions in the acquired signal, followed by the interpretation of the outcomes using the explainable approach. Simulation results revealed the efficiency of the proposed model compared to the existing methods using the research NIDS dataset.

title: Multi-Layer Standardized Coding Method for Power Supply Chain Materials Based on Hash Algorithm
authors: Tao Wang, Xiaozhe Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: The more intricate and networked management of power supply chains and the expansion of digitised supply chains have made it important that distinct material data can be made both unique and traceable and also compatible with one another. This study has presented a multi-layer standardized coding method for power supply chain materials, based on the hash algorithm trying to increase the material identification, and cut down the redundancy and a view to strengthening the intelligent management of information. It combines a tree-like encoding system with cryptographic hashing to encode multiple forms of material into collision-resistant IDs in their various stages of distribution. In the first level, the attributes of materials are essentially assimilated in a standardized format. the second stratum includes the supply chain metadata including the source, batch, and quality; the final layer hashes data using a secure hash function such as secure hash algorithm (e.g., SHA-256) for data integrity and compactness. The presented method can also support fast indexing, reliable authentication and also the integration of data across platforms. In the real-world experiment, a power supply chain dataset shows that our data management and search mechanism improve data consistency, search speed, as well as the material traceability compared to traditional identification approaches. Postscript There is potential for future research to also include the application of the blockchain technology to incorporate artificial intelligence and analytics in improving the power supply chain system.

title:
S
                    <sup>3</sup>
                    Mamba-Pan: Spectral-Spatial-Scale Mamba with Frequency-Decoupled Dual-Stream for Pansharpening
authors: Zishun Song, Yao Zhang, Haoyu Li, Yanlin He, Jiawei Zhao, Yi Yang, Wei Zhang, Dezhen Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Pansharpening plays a critical role in remote sensing image processing by generating high-resolution multispectral (HRMS) images through the fusion of low-resolution multispectral (LRMS) and high-resolution panchromatic (PAN) images. In recent years, deep learning–based approaches have significantly advanced fusion performance via end-to-end modeling. However, existing methods still face notable limitations, including insufficient modeling of long-range dependencies, tight coupling between spectral and spatial information that may introduce spectral distortion and high computational complexity that restricts practical deployment in high-resolution scenarios. To address these issues, we propose Spectral-Spatial-Scale Mamba for Pansharpening (S3Mamba-Pan), a frequency-aware state space framework that jointly models spectral structure, spatial texture and multi-scale context in a coordinated yet decoupled manner. The method first extracts a global spectral anchor from the LRMS input to guide fusion, then performs wavelet-based frequency decoupling to separate low-frequency spectral components from high-frequency spatial details. A dual-stream Mamba architecture conducts parallel state space modeling for spectral correlation learning and PAN-guided texture enhancement and an adaptive distribution recalibration module aligns channel-wise statistics before reconstruction. Experiments on WV3, QuickBird and GF2 demonstrate consistent improvements over representative CNN, Transformer and SSM-based baselines under reduced-resolution evaluation. Under full-resolution no-reference assessment on WV3, S3Mamba-Pan achieves Dλ 0.0144, Ds 0.0303 and HQNR 0.960, indicating improved spectral fidelity and spatial consistency. Ablation and visualization analyses further corroborate the contribution of each component and provide interpretable evidence for the effectiveness of frequency decoupling and spectral anchoring. Code is available at https://github.com/FreeZS-a/S3Mamba.

title: Integrated Sensing and Covert Communications: A Robust Design Against Mobile Surveillance
authors: Jiaqi Wang, Lu Lv, Yilin Wang, Bingtao He, Long Yang
abstract: This paper investigates a two-stage covert enhancement beamforming design based on the extended Kalman filter (EKF) to combat the mobile eavesdropper (Willie) in the integrated sensing and covert communications. The proposed scheme employs artificial noise (AN) to interfere with and track Willie, while the beamwidth of AN is controlled by the number of antennas. In the initial stage, due to the lack of prior information, the EKF suffers from large prediction errors, preventing the jamming beam from fully aligning with Willie. Thus, a wide AN beam is adopted to guarantee the jamming effect on Willie. In the second stage, as the prediction error of the EKF converges after iterative updates, the scheme switches to a narrow AN beam to track Willie effectively. Considering the imperfect channel state information of the covert user, we jointly optimize the time allocation and transmit beamforming for the two stages, aiming to minimize transmission energy, and propose the joint wide and narrow beam optimization algorithm to address the non-convex problem. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme reduces the transmission energy by approximately 23% compared with the benchmark schemes.

title: Statistical Horizontal Squeezing Transform and Its Application in Reflection Interface Identification of Deep Carbonate Reservoirs
authors: Yunli Fei, Hui Chen, Ying Hu, Xuping Chen, Yutao Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Carbonate reservoirs exhibit complex internal structures and strong heterogeneity. Their seismic responses commonly show multicomponent superposition and pronounced nonstationary characteristics, which readily induce inter-component interference in time-frequency (TF) representations and pose challenges to the characterization of reflection interfaces. To address this issue, we propose a statistical horizontal squeezing transform (SHST) that reformulates group delay (GD) estimation from a pointwise derivative operation to a statistical estimation problem within local TF neighborhoods. Specifically, a statistical group delay estimator (SGDE) is constructed by introducing a weighted short-time Fourier transform (STFT) spectrum, allowing GD to be inferred through local TF statistical consistency rather than single-point gradients. This strategy enhances robustness against component interference and stabilizes energy reassignment. Experiments on synthetic signals and simulated geological models demonstrate that SHST achieves superior TF concentration and robustness under conditions of closely spaced multicomponent signals and noise. Applications to real deep carbonate seismic data further verify the effectiveness of the proposed method in characterizing the time-depth distribution of reflection interfaces, providing a reliable TF analysis tool for seismic interpretation of complex carbonate reservoirs.

title: Leveraging Edge Impulse for Multi-Class Error-Related Potentials: Edge AI Brain-Robot Interface for Correcting Navigation Errors in Assistive Mobile Robot Wheelchairs
authors: Karameldeen Omer, Andrea Monteriù
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: This study explores the integration of BrainComputer Interface (BCI) technology with microcontroller chips to be used for real-time signal processing and event classification, aiming to create a standalone and efficient system deployed as machine learning models on edge devices, optimizing classification pipelines to process Error-Related Potential (ErrP)-based EEG signals. All classification models are multi-class classifiers to differentiate among four classes: “no navigation error” and three levels of navigation error severity (small, medium, and large). The main aim is to investigate the feasibility of utilizing edge computing for real-time signal processing and classification in a BCI system. A pilot study was conducted with 10 subjects to obtain preliminary results and evaluate the system's performance. This approach allowed for testing the viability of the proposed BCI paradigm and refining the data acquisition protocol. These initial findings guide adjustments to the experimental setup, ensuring the system is optimized before scaling up to involve a larger group of participants for extensive data collection. The results underscore the potential of integrating BCI technology with edge AI platforms for real-time processing. However, the findings also reveal significant challenges, particularly the highly unbalanced datasets in multi-class classification tasks. Moreover, the study highlights the need to shorten trial lengths to improve the detection of ErrPs. Addressing these challenges is essential for refining the experimental protocol and optimizing system design before scaling to larger datasets and broader subject participation. Overall, the study demonstrates the promise of edge-based BCI systems in enabling low-latency, energy-efficient, and scalable assistive devices. This approach has the potential to significantly enhance the autonomy and safety of individuals with severe motor disabilities, providing a practical and accessible solution for assistive robotic applications.

title: Accuracy Analysis of Wear Error Compensation Through its Prediction
authors: Svetlana Koleva
abstract: Ensuring the accuracy of diametrical dimensions in turning operations depends on a large number of disturbing factors, among which tool wear is one of the most significant. Tool wear is influenced by the cutting process, the machinability of the workpiece material, and the parameters of the cutting tool itself, which exhibit specific behavior under different operating conditions. A cause-and-effect diagram has been developed to graphically represent these influences. An experiment was conducted to obtain real production data on the tool wear process under industrial conditions. One of the possible approaches for compensating tool wear is its modelling and prediction. The accuracy of tool wear modelling is determined by the stochastic nature of the process. The total dispersion field of wear rate caused by technological factors has been determined. The accuracy of predicting cutting tool wear is a function of time and of variations in the wear rate intensity.

title: IoT-Assisted Multi-Robot System: Impact of Information Sharing on Control
authors: Satwik Mondal, Manish H. Kausik, Sudipta Saha
abstract: In a decentralized multi-robot system, inter-robot communication plays a crucial role in achieving coordination among the robots. However, the expected precision in the coordination to achieve the goal widely varies from application to application. In this work, we demonstrate the effect of nondeterministic communication delays on a multi-robot system through theoretical and empirical studies. The study reveals that uneven and uncertain communication delays may cause a multi-robot system to experience significant jitter, potentially leading to unexpected state transitions, which can be detrimental in several applications. We demonstrate that recent advances in Synchronous-Transmission (ST)-based protocols, which have proven to be a highly efficient tool for information sharing in the context of the Internet of Things (IoT), can mitigate this problem quite effectively. Furthermore, to demonstrate the concept through simulation, we modify an existing network simulation tool, called Cooja, to include support for a multirobot system along with ST-based inter-robot data sharing.

title: Ziv-Zakai Bound For Distributed-Array-Based Doa Estimation
authors: Zongyu Zhang, Zhiguo Shi, Maria Sabrina Greco, Fulvio Gini
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The existing Ziv–Zakai Bound (ZZB) for multi-target direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation is not directly applicable to distributed array systems. The main challenge lies in incorporating unknown subarray position offsets into the ZZB derivation. Moreover, the high sidelobes in the beam-pattern make the approximations used in conventional ZZB derivations overly relaxed. In this work, we address these challenges and derive the ZZB for multi-target distributed DOA estimation. Specifically, we first establish the conditional ZZB given the subarray position offsets, where a new approximation method is introduced to account for the effect of sidelobes in the beampattern, yielding a tighter ZZB than those in existing studies. The final ZZB is then obtained by averaging the conditional ZZB over the unknown subarray position offsets. Simulation results provide insights into distributed array configurations from the perspective of the ZZB and validate the effectiveness of the proposed approximation.

title: GMAMBAFLOW: Global-Aware Mamba Based Cost Volume Aggregation for Optical Flow
authors: Yuhao Yang, Yining Miao, Wenming Yang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Mamba architecture has demonstrated excellent performance in various visual tasks. It outperforms many CNN and transformer based architectures due to its long-sequence modeling capability and linear complexity. However, when we aggregate cost volume for occluded regions, Mamba module fails to properly match features of the same category due to its token-independent parameter generation mechanism. To address this issue, we propose a novel aggregation scheme called Global-aware Mamba Cost volume Aggregation, which can enhance matching ability of pixels within the same category by introducing prompts with global semantic information into the discretization step size of state space model. Additionally, we introduce an uncertainty-guided loss to enhance the network’s self-optimization ability for poorly predicted regions during iteration. Experiments show that our design achieves outstanding results on the Sintel and KITTI datasets.

title: DCR-MUCL:Dual Granularity Consistency Routing Multimodal Unified Contrastive Learning For Rumor Detection Network
authors: Xinyu Zheng, Yicun Liu, Zhengnan Qiao, Xianguo Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Social network rumors pose a threat to social stability, and existing methods have issues such as redundant noise in comments, insufficient diversity in multimodal features, and inadequate mining of sample similarities and differences. To address these problems, we propose the Dual Granularity Consistency Routing Multimodal Unified Contrastive Learning for Rumor Detection Network (DCR-MUCL). This network first enhances modality dependencies through a text-visual interactive feature integration module. Then, it compresses redundancy and filters noise using a dual-granularity consistency routing hypergraph framework, encoding social associations. Next, it strengthens interactions with a cross-modal co-attention mechanism. Finally, it designs a multimodal unified virtual augmented contrastive learning network to explicitly model the collaborative anomalies of the three modalities, combining real labels and virtual features to enhance contrastive learning, which is then input into a classifier for detection. Experiments show that this method significantly surpasses existing best methods.

title: Flowiid: Single-Step Intrinsic Image Decomposition Via Latent Flow Matching
authors: Mithlesh Singla, Seema Kumari, Shanmuganathan Raman
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Intrinsic Image Decomposition (IID) separates an image into albedo and shading components. It is a core step in many real-world applications, such as relighting and material editing. Existing IID models achieve good results, but often use a large number of parameters. This makes them costly to combine with other models in real-world settings. To address this problem, we propose a flow matching-based solution. For this, we design a novel architecture, FlowIID, based on latent flow matching. FlowIID combines a VAE-guided latent space with a flow matching module, enabling a stable decomposition of albedo and shading. FlowIID is not only parameter-efficient, but also produces results in a single inference step. Despite its compact design, FlowIID delivers competitive and superior results compared to existing models across various benchmarks. This makes it well-suited for deployment in resource-constrained and real-time vision applications.

title: Evaluating Emotion Recognition in Spoken Language Models on Emotionally Incongruent Speech
authors: Pedro Corrêa, João Lima, Victor Moreno, Lucas Ueda, Paula Costa
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Advancements in spoken language processing have driven the development of spoken language models (SLMs), designed to achieve universal audio understanding by jointly learning text and audio representations for a wide range of tasks. Although promising results have been achieved, there is growing discussion regarding these models’ generalization capabilities and the extent to which they truly integrate audio and text modalities in their internal representations. In this work, we evaluate four SLMs on the task of speech emotion recognition using a dataset of emotionally incongruent speech samples, a condition under which the semantic content of the spoken utterance conveys one emotion while speech expressiveness conveys another. Our results indicate that SLMs rely predominantly on textual semantics rather than speech emotion to perform the task, indicating that text-related representations largely dominate over acoustic representations. We release both the code and the Emotionally Incongruent Synthetic Speech dataset (EMIS) to the community.

title: Early Prediction Method for Learners at Risk Based on Multi-Source Feature Fusion
authors: Haoxin Xu, Changyong Qi, Tong Liu, Bohao Zhang, Bingqian Jiang, Longwei Zheng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Early identification of at-risk learners is crucial for improving learning outcomes in Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs). This paper presents an early prediction method for identifying such learners using multi-source feature fusion, aiming to enhance the accuracy of risk detection in MOOCs. The proposed method incorporates a TF-IDF algorithm to effectively evaluate the significance of learning behavior data and integrates learners’ profile information with behavioral features, which are then processed by a sequential model for risk prediction. Furthermore, explainable AI techniques are employed to reveal the key factors influencing the predictions and diagnose the underlying causes of academic risk. Experimental results on real-world datasets demonstrate that the method significantly outperforms traditional machine learning models in early prediction, particularly during the one to ten week period. This research provides a robust approach to predicting at-risk learners, offering valuable insights for developing personalized educational interventions and contributing to improved completion rates in MOOCs.

title: Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Aggregation in Semi-Supervised Federated Learning with non-IID Data
authors: Rene Glitza, Luca Becker, Rainer Martin
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables distributed training of machine learning models while preserving data privacy. However, FL struggles with heterogeneous, non-IID client data distributions, resulting in sub-optimal and biased global models. In this paper, we propose pFedMARL, a novel approach leveraging Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) with Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) to dynamically adapt aggregation strategies in FL settings. Our method employs a server-side agent adjusting client contributions to optimize global model robustness and client-side agents balancing global and local updates to personalize models effectively without pre-training. We demonstrate superior performance of pFedMARL for training a semi-supervised audio spectrogram transformer, matching or outperforming FedAvg, Ditto, and local training approaches across multiple non-IID scenarios and in the presence of adversarial clients. Our results indicate that pFedMARL actively improves accuracy, robustness, and fairness, making it suitable for real-world deployments.

title: Complex-Valued Phase Synchrony Reveals Directional Coupling in fMRI and Tracks Medication Effects
authors: Sir-Lord Wiafe, Najme Soleimani, Masoud Seraji, Bradley Baker, Robyn Miller, Ashkan Faghiri, Vince D. Calhoun
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Understanding interactions in complex systems requires capturing the relative timing of coupling, not only its strength. Phase synchronization captures this timing, yet most methods either reduce the phase to its cosine or collapse it into scalar indices such as the phase-locking value, discarding relative timing. We propose a complex-valued phase synchrony (CVPS) framework that estimates phase with an adaptive Gabor wavelet and preserves both cosine and sine components. Simulations confirm that CVPS recovers true phase offsets and tracks non-stationary dynamics more faithfully than Hilbert-based methods. Because antipsychotics are known to modulate the timing of cortical interactions, they provide a rigorous context to evaluate whether CVPS can capture such pharmacological effects. CVPS further reveals cortical neuro-hemodynamic drivers, with occipital-to-parietal and prefrontal-to-striatal lead–lag flows consistent with known receptor targets, confirming its ability to capture pharmacological timing. CVPS, therefore, offers a robust, generalizable framework for detecting relative timing in complex systems such as the brain.

title: CIFC-MFD: End-To-End Multi-Face Forgery Detection Using Cross-Image Face Contrast
authors: Xiaoyu Zhou, Jingchang Xie, Peipeng Yu, Chengfu Ou, Jianwei Fei, Zhihua Xia
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: The misuse of deepfake technology poses a serious threat to societal security. Existing detection methods are primarily designed for single-face scenarios, making them ineffective for complex multi-face scenes. Although recent end-to-end detection frameworks have emerged, they are confined to analyzing features within a single image, thus failing to leverage relational information across different images. To address this, we propose the Cross-Image Face Contrast Multi-Face Forgery Detection (CIFC-MFD) framework. This framework enhances scene-level consistency through an Intra-Image Face Relation Module (IFRM) and introduces the key innovation of a Cross-Image Face Contrast (CIFC) module, which extracts forgery-related features across different images by comparing face features with a global memory bank. Experimental results demonstrate that CIFC-MFD outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving improvements of 4.6% in detection and 4.8% in segmentation Average Precision (AP) on the test-challenge set of the OpenForensics dataset. Notably, in complex scenarios, the model improves the detection AP for small-sized faces by 14.4%, showcasing its substantial advantages.

title: AmbiDrop: Array-Agnostic Speech Enhancement Using Ambisonics Encoding and Dropout-Based Learning
authors: Michael Tatarjitzky, Boaz Rafaely
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multichannel speech enhancement leverages spatial cues to improve intelligibility and quality, but most learning-based methods rely on specific microphone array geometry, unable to account for geometry changes. To mitigate this limitation, current array-agnostic approaches employ large multi-geometry datasets but may still fail to generalize to unseen layouts. We propose AmbiDrop (Ambisonics with Dropouts), an Ambisonics-based framework that encodes arbitrary array recordings into the spherical harmonics domain using Ambisonics Signal Matching (ASM). A deep neural network is trained on simulated Ambisonics data, combined with channel dropout for robustness against array-dependent encoding errors, therefore omitting the need for a diverse microphone array database. Experiments show that while the baseline and proposed models perform similarly on the training arrays, the baseline degrades on unseen arrays. In contrast, AmbiDrop consistently improves SI-SDR, PESQ, and STOI, demonstrating strong generalization and practical potential for array-agnostic speech enhancement.

title: Mind the Shift: Using Delta SSL Embeddings to Enhance Child ASR
authors: Zilai Wang, Natarajan Balaji Shankar, Kaiyuan Zhang, Zihan Wang, Abeer Alwan
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) models have achieved impressive results across many speech tasks, yet child automatic speech recognition (ASR) remains challenging due to limited data and pretraining domain mismatch. Fine-tuning SSL models on child speech induces shifts in the representation space. We hypothesize that delta SSL embeddings, the differences between embeddings from a fine-tuned model and those from its pre-trained counterpart, encode task-specific information that complements fine-tuned features from another SSL model. We evaluate multiple fusion strategies on the MyST children’s corpus with different models. Results show that delta embedding fusion with WavLM yields up to a 10% relative WER reduction for HuBERT and a 4.4% reduction for W2V2, compared to fine-tuned embedding fusion. Notably, fusing WavLM with delta W2V2 embeddings achieves a WER of 9.64, setting a new state-of-the-art among SSL models on the MyST corpus. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of delta embeddings and highlight feature fusion as a promising direction for advancing child ASR.

title: Listen, But Don't Leak: Sensitive Data Protection for Privacy Aware Automatic Speech Recognition with Acoustic Triggers
authors: Trinita Roy, Ngoc Thang Vu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems, concerns about unintentional transcription of sensitive or private information have grown significantly. In this study, we introduce Protective Acoustic Triggering (PAT), a novel method for embedding privacy redaction directly into ASR models. We propose a privacy-preserving strategy that leverages a high-frequency acoustic trigger, originally conceptualized as an adversarial attack on the ASR model, as a controlled mechanism to enforce transcription redaction. When activated, this trigger forces the ASR system to redact entire utterances or sensitive phrases by substituting them with a dedicated <Redacted> token, thereby embedding privacy awareness directly into the ASR pipeline rather than relying on post-hoc filtering. We fine-tune several Whisper variants on LibriSpeech as well as a synthetically generated privacy-sensitive data that allows Whisper to appropriately redact content while preserving transcription accuracy on insensitive speech. Our experiments show that in the utterance-level setup, the model achieves a 99.47% redaction success rate, and, for phrase-level redaction, the model successfully protected 97.7% of utterances (90.6% of sensitive spans), while maintaining near-baseline speech recognition utility on insensitive segments.

title: Expectation Propagation Detector Exploiting Overlapping Block Structures for Highly Correlated MIMO Systems
authors: Kabuto Arai, Takumi Yoshida, Takumi Takahashi, Koji Ishibashi
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: This paper proposes an expectation propagation (EP)-based detector, termed overlapping block partitioning EP (OvEP), for highly correlated multiple-input-multiple-output (MIMO) systems. In this method, large-scale measurements are partitioned into partially overlapping blocks, for which low-complexity linear minimum mean square error (LMMSE) filters are designed. The filter outputs are then combined to generate tentative estimates. By subtracting the outputs of overlapping parts from those of the block parts in the combining process, inter-block correlation induced by high spatial correlation can be effectively suppressed, leading to an improved detection performance. Numerical results demonstrate that OvEP attains performance comparable to LMMSE-EP without requiring full-dimensional matrix inversion.

title: Syncspeech: Efficient and Low-Latency Text-to-Speech Based on Temporal Masked Transformer
authors: Zhengyan Sheng, Zhihao Du, Shiliang Zhang, Zhijie Yan, Liping Chen
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Current text-to-speech (TTS) models face a persistent limitation: autoregressive (AR) models suffer from low generation efficiency, while modern non-autoregressive (NAR) models experience high latency due to their unordered temporal nature. To bridge this divide, we introduce SyncSpeech, an efficient and low-latency TTS model based on the proposed Temporal Mask Transformer (TMT) paradigm. TMT synergistically unifies the temporally ordered generation of AR models with the parallel decoding efficiency of NAR models. TMT is realized through a meticulously designed sequence construction rule, a corresponding training objective, and a specialized hybrid attention mask. Furthermore, with the primary aim of enhancing training efficiency, a high-probability masking strategy is introduced, which also leads to a significant improvement in overall model performance. During inference, SyncSpeech achieves high efficiency by decoding all speech tokens corresponding to each newly arrived text token in a single step, and low latency by beginning to generate speech immediately upon receiving the second text token from the streaming input. Evaluations show that Sync-Speech maintains speech quality comparable to the modern AR TTS model, while achieving a 5.8-fold reduction in first-packet latency and an 8.8-fold improvement in real-time factor. Speech samples are available at https://SyncSpeech.github.io/.

title: DiffusionCom: Structure-Aware Multimodal Diffusion Model for Multimodal Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Wei Huang, Peining Li, Meiyu Liang, Xu Hou, Yawen Li, Junping Du, Zhe Xue, Zeli Guan
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multimodal knowledge graphs (MKGs) are widely used in recommendation systems, information retrieval, and question answering, but they remain highly incomplete, driving the need for multimodal knowledge graph completion (MKGC) methods. Most existing approaches rely on discriminative models that maximize conditional likelihood, neglecting the underlying data distribution and limiting their ability to capture complex real-world relations. To address this, we propose DiffusionCom, a structure-aware multimodal diffusion model for MKGC. DiffusionCom frames MKGC as generating the joint probability distribution of (head, relation) pairs and candidate tail entities from noise. We also propose Structure-MKGformer as its encoder, which captures structural information via a multi-modal graph attention network (MGAT) and adaptively fuses it with entity representations. Trained with both generative and discriminative losses, DiffusionCom outperforms state-of-the-art models on FB15k-237-IMG and WN18-IMG datasets—notably, achieving a 27.5% relative improvement in Hits@1 on FB15k-237-IMG.

title: Establishing Stochastic Object Models from Noisy Data via Ambient Measurement-Integrated Diffusion
authors: Xiaoning Lei, Jianwei Sun, Wenhao Cai, Xichen Xu, Yanshu Wang, Hu Gao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Task-based assessments of image quality (IQ) are critical for evaluating medical imaging systems, which must account for randomness, including anatomical variability. Stochastic object models (SOMs) provide a statistical description of such variability. However, conventional mathematical SOMs fail to capture realistic anatomy, while most data-driven approaches typically require clean data, which are rarely available in clinical tasks. To address this challenge, we propose an unsupervised method called Ambient Measurement-Integrated Diffusion (AMID) with noise decoupling, which establishes clean SOMs directly from noisy measurements. AMID introduces a measurement-integrated strategy aligning measurement noise with the diffusion trajectory, and explicitly models coupling between measurement and diffusion noise across steps, and thus an ambient-consistency loss is thus designed based on it to learn clean SOMs. Experiments on real CT and mammography datasets show that AMID outperforms existing methods in generation fidelity and yields more reliable task-based IQ evaluation, demonstrating its potential for unsupervised medical imaging analysis.

title: Multi-Physics: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal LLMs Reasoning on Chinese Multi-Subject Physics Problems
authors: Zhongze Luo, Zhenshuai Yin, Yongxin Guo, Zhichao Wang, Jionghao Zhu, Xiaoying Tang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: While multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) demonstrate remarkable reasoning progress, their application in specialized scientific domains like physics reveals significant gaps in current evaluation benchmarks. Specifically, existing benchmarks often lack fine-grained subject coverage, neglect the step-by-step reasoning process, and are predominantly English-centric, failing to systematically evaluate the role of visual information. Therefore, we introduce Multi-Physics for Chinese physics reasoning, a comprehensive benchmark that includes 5 difficulty levels, featuring 1,412 image-associated, multiple-choice questions spanning 11 high-school physics subjects. We employ a dual evaluation framework to evaluate 20 different MLLMs, analyzing both final answer accuracy and the step-by-step integrity of their chain-of-thought. Furthermore, we systematically study the impact of difficulty level and visual information by comparing the model performance before and after changing the input mode. Our work provides not only a fine-grained resource for the community but also offers a robust methodology for dissecting the multimodal reasoning process of state-of-the-art MLLMs, and our dataset and code have been open-sourced <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.

title: EMS-Mixer: Extreme Multi-Scale Mixing for Time Series Forecasting
authors: Jiali Deng, Bolun Cai, Yuqing Chen, Ying Hao, Kunkun Liu, Zhanpeng Jin
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: As a fundamental task, time series forecasting remains highly challenging due to the inherent multi-scale characteristics of temporal data. Existing methods often model spatial and frequency domains separately, limiting their ability to capture both fine- and coarse-grained patterns as well as slow- and fast-varying dynamics. To address this issue, we propose EMS-Mixer, an extreme multi-scale framework that unifies spatial and frequency perspectives for comprehensive temporal modeling. EMS-Mixer leverages multi-scale sampling to disentangle low- and high-frequency components, a dual-path architecture to capture local and global structures, and a cross-scale mixing mechanism that maximizes complementary interactions across resolutions. Extensive experiments on 18 real-world benchmarks show that EMS-Mixer consistently surpasses 13 state-of-the-art baselines in both long- and short-term forecasting, achieving significant gains in accuracy and robustness. Code: https://github.com/jelly1130/EMS-Mixer.

title: LVC: A Lightweight Compression Framework for Enhancing VLMS in Long Video Understanding
authors: Ziyi Wang, Haoran Wu, Yiming Rong, Yunlong Zhao, Yixin Zhang, Bo Xu
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Long video understanding is a complex task that requires both spatial detail and temporal awareness. While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) obtain frame-level understanding capabilities through multi-frame input, they suffer from information loss due to the sparse sampling strategy. In contrast, Video Large Language Models (Video-LLMs) capture temporal relationships within visual features but are limited by the scarcity of high-quality video-text datasets. To transfer long video understanding capabilities to VLMs with minimal data and computational cost, we propose Lightweight Video Compression (LVC), a novel method featuring the Query-Attention Video Compression mechanism, which effectively tackles the sparse sampling problem in VLMs. By training only the alignment layer with 10k short video-text pairs, LVC significantly enhances the temporal reasoning abilities of VLMs. Extensive experiments show that LVC provides consistent performance improvements across various models, including the InternVL2 series and Phi-3.5-Vision. The InternVL2-40B-LVC achieves scores of 68.2 and 65.9 on the long video understanding benchmarks MLVU and Video-MME, respectively, with relative improvements of 14.6% and 7.7%.

title: SAKA: Spatially-Adaptive and Keyframe-Anchored Graph Network for Continuous Sign Language Recognition
authors: Weiyi Ye, Xu-Hua Yang, Gang-Feng Ma, Dong Wei, Sheng Liu, Yuan Feng
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Continuous Sign Language Recognition (CSLR) technology facilitates communication for individuals with hearing impairments by converting dynamic sign language videos into text sequences. However, current methods are often constrained by the inadequate spatial feature extraction of CNNs from critical regions and the inefficient modeling of long-range temporal dependencies. To address these limitations, we propose SAKA, a novel CSLR framework leveraging spatially-adaptive and keyframe-anchored graph networks. In the spatial domain, our adaptive graph construction strategy dynamically builds adjacency by predicting content-aware coordinate offsets, thereby enhancing discriminative features in information-rich areas while suppressing background noise. For temporal modeling, we propose a keyframe-anchoring mechanism that identifies semantically significant keyframes through motion saliency and establishes sparse connections to the current frame, capturing long-range temporal dependencies with low computational overhead. Extensive experiments on PHOENIX14, PHOENIX14-T, and CSL-Daily datasets demonstrate that SAKA outperforms state-of-the-art approaches without any additional annotations.

title: SAR-Caption Ranker: Optimizing Automatic SAR Image Descriptions via RLAIF
authors: Xinjun Cheng, Qiwei Ma, Junjie Zhu, Xichuan Zhang, Chunping Qiu, Jun Wang, Qiangjuan Huang, Ke Yang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Automatic captioning of Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images is a challenging task due to speckle noise, complex textures, and imaging distortions. Existing vision-language models (VLMs), such as BLIP2, InstructBLIP, LLaVA, and Qwen-VL, demonstrate strong captioning ability in optical imagery but perform suboptimally when directly applied to SAR data. In this paper, we propose a novel framework for SAR image captioning enhanced with Reinforcement Learning with AI Feedback (RLAIF). Our method first generates multiple candidate captions using several pretrained VLMs, then evaluates them with a SAR-specific reward function that measures object relevance, spatial relations, semantic completeness, and linguistic fluency. Based on these reward scores, captions are re-ranked or sampled to produce the final output. Experiments on benchmark SAR datasets show that our approach significantly improves BLEU, CIDEr, and SAR-specific consistency scores compared to direct VLM baselines. The proposed framework is model-agnostic, annotation-free, and provides an efficient pathway to adapt large-scale captioning models to SAR imagery.

title: SWAN: Boosting Image Super-Resolution with Stochastic Wavelet Attention
authors: Shengyi Xiong
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Convolutional neural networks have significantly advanced single image super-resolution (SR), where Non-Local (NL) attention is crucial for accurate restoration. However, NL attention is computationally expensive and often over-emphasizes global context while underutilizing local correlations essential for SR. We propose a Stochastic Wavelet Attention (SWA) mechanism that efficiently models global-local dependencies in both spatial and frequency domains. SWA consists of two sparse NL attentions: Intra-Wavelet Attention (IWA) for global context within each wavelet sub-band, and Cross-Wavelet Attention (CWA) for local correlations across sub-bands. By operating on compact wavelet representations, SWA drastically reduces computational complexity. Moreover, we introduce stochastic aggregation to capture the one-to-many nature of SR, enabling diverse predictions and uncertainty estimation. A subsequent uncertainty reduction algorithm improves generalization to out-of-distribution images. Experiments demonstrate that SWA achieves state-of-the-art performance with higher efficiency.

title: Background Disambiguation Contrastive Loss for Robust Binary Segmentation
authors: Pantelis Mentesidis, Vasileios Mygdalis, Ioannis Pitas
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Binary region segmentation, critical for applications such as natural disaster response (e.g., flood mapping) and industrial inspection (e.g., pipe defect detection), remains challenging due to the high variance and ambiguity within background regions. Standard Deep Neural Network (DNN) training often produces distorted feature spaces where diverse background patterns collapse into indistinct clusters. To address this, we propose a Background Disambiguation Contrastive Loss (BDCL), introduced as an auxiliary objective alongside the task loss. BDCL encourages compact intra-class clustering while simultaneously enforcing stronger inter-class separation, effectively disambiguating heterogeneous background features and leading to more discriminative representations. When applied to a state-of-the-art (SOTA) model, our approach achieves absolute performance gains of +2.4% on flood segmentation and +4.8% on pipe segmentation over baseline training. Beyond quantitative improvements, BDCL consistently yields sharper and semantically meaningful segmentations in cluttered scenes, outperforming conventional training and existing contrastive learning strategies.

title: MarkSweep: A No-Box Removal Attack on AI-Generated Image Watermarking via Noise Intensification and Frequency-Aware Denoising
authors: Jie Cao, Zelin Zhang, Qi Li, Jianbing Ni
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: AI watermarking embeds invisible signals within images to provide provenance information and identify content as AI-generated. In this paper, we introduce MarkSweep, a novel watermark removal attack that effectively erases the embedded watermarks from AI-generated images without degrading visual quality. MarkSweep first amplifies watermark noise in high-frequency regions via edge-aware Gaussian perturbations and injects it into clean images for training a denoising network. This network then integrates two modules, the learnable frequency decomposition module and the frequency-aware fusion module, to suppress amplified noise and eliminate watermark traces. Theoretical analysis and extensive experiments demonstrate that in-visible watermarks are highly vulnerable to MarkSweep, which effectively removes embedded watermarks, reducing the bit accuracy of HiDDeN and Stable Signature watermarking schemes to below 67%, while preserving perceptual quality of AI-generated images.

title: SQP-Based Passive Coherent Localization via Joint DTD and AOA Measurements
authors: Sining Liu, Yaxing Yue, Boyang Jia, Guisheng Liao
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: In passive coherent localization, differential time delay and angle of arrival can significantly reduce the required number of illuminators and receivers. However, traditional methods like weighted and iterative constrained weighted least squares often suffer from threshold effects and convergence failures due to the highly nonlinear measurement model. To address these issues, we propose a sequential quadratic programming approach that decomposes the non-convex problem into quadratic programming subproblems and iteratively seeks the local optimum. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method approaches the Cramér-Rao lower bound and achieves higher localization accuracy than existing methods.

title: ECSA: Dual-Branch Emotion Compensation for Emotion-Consistent Speaker Anonymization
authors: Chenghan Lin, Junjie Li, Tingting Wang, Meng Ge, Longbiao Wang, Kong Aik Lee, Jianwu Dang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Speaker anonymization techniques have achieved remarkable success in preserving linguistic content while protecting speaker identity. However, these methods often degrade the emotional information in speech, limiting their reliability in emotion-sensitive scenarios. To mitigate this issue, we propose an emotion-preserving speaker anonymization framework. Our approach eliminates the external emotion encoder used in prior methods to avoid speaker information leakage, and instead introduces a novel dual-branch emotion compensation module. This module consists of a static compensator that captures dataset-level emotion distributions via soft labels and non-linear deep learning models, and a dynamic compensator that enhances emotional expression by exploiting utterance-level residuals. Furthermore, we incorporate an emotion consistency loss into the HiFi-GAN training process to ensure effective use of emotional information during anonymization. Experiments on the VPC 2024 benchmark show that our method significantly improves emotion preservation without compromising anonymization performance. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of each component, and additional evaluations on IEMOCAP demonstrate superior Unweighted Average Recall (UAR) and class-wise recall compared to baseline systems.

title: Semantic Mining and Cross-Center Synergy for Cross-Modal Person Re-Identification
authors: Yanyan Lv, Zhihao Chen
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification (VI-ReID) involves matching pedestrian images across visible and infrared modalities, which faces a substantial modality discrepancy due to their distinct imaging principles. While existing methods attempt to reduce this discrepancy through image translation or feature alignment, they often overlook high-level semantic consistency and insufficiently explore intra-class and inter-class relationships for cross-modal metric learning. To address these issues, we propose a novel Semantic Mining and Cross-Center Synergy (SMCCS) network. Our framework introduces three key components: a Semantic-Aware Interactive (SAI) module that adaptively focuses on identity-relevant high-level semantic regions across modalities; a Fine-Grained Cross-Alignment (FGCA) loss that encourages a compact intra-class distribution in the shared feature space; and an Enhanced Cross-Triplet (ECT) loss that improves inter-class discriminability of cross-modal representations. Extensive experiments on two standard datasets, SYSU-MM01 and RegDB, demonstrate that our method achieves the state-of-the-art performance. Our code is available at https://github.com/GuYan66/ICASSP2026-SMCCS.

title: Gaussian Processes for Sensor Repositioning in PDE-Driven Systems
authors: Maan Pandya, Bianca Giovanardi, Raj Thilak Rajan
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Field estimation in spatio-temporally evolving environments remains challenging, particularly when limited sensor resources must capture dynamic features while contending with modeling errors and measurement noise e.g., in environmental monitoring using aerial vehicles, where system dynamics interact with practical sensing limitations. In this work, we consider a scenario where a network of mobile sensor nodes measure an advection-diffusion field, where the sensor locations can be dynamically optimized based on PDE residuals e.g., sensors on-board drones. Our novel two-stage framework strategically integrates Gaussian Process regression with PDE constraints. An initial inference stage estimates key parameters (e.g., advection velocity, diffusion coefficient) through stationary sensor measurements and finite-difference derivative approximations, while a subsequent mobility stage employs forward-Euler time-stepping to dynamically relocate the sensors toward regions of high PDE residual. Simulations based on a 2D advection-diffusion field experiment reveals upto an order magnitude improvement in field reconstruction error, as compared to information theoretic deployments. We conclude with future directions of extending our work and suggest applications.

title: SURE: Synergistic Uncertainty-Aware Reasoning for Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Conversations
authors: Yiqiang Cai, Chengyan Wu, Bolei Ma, Bo Chen, Yun Xue, Julia Hirschberg, Ziwei Gong
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition in conversations (MERC) requires integrating multimodal signals while being robust to noise and modeling contextual reasoning. Existing approaches often emphasize fusion but overlook uncertainty in noisy features and fine-grained reasoning. We propose SURE (Synergistic Uncertainty-aware REasoning) for MERC, a framework that improves robustness and contextual modeling. SURE consists of three components: an Uncertainty-Aware Mixture-of-Experts module to handle modality-specific noise, an Iterative Reasoning module for multi-turn reasoning over context, and a Transformer Gate module to capture intra- and inter-modal interactions. Experiments on benchmark MERC datasets show that SURE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, demonstrating its effectiveness in robust multimodal reasoning. These results highlight the importance of uncertainty modeling and iterative reasoning in advancing emotion recognition in conversational settings. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: Disentangled Representation Learning with Cross-Factor Constraints for Medical Image Analysis
authors: Xie Huang, Linlu Xu, Yipin Zhang, Ziqi Yu, Shengjie Zhang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Accurate medical image diagnosis requires models that are both robust and interpretable. Existing deep learning methods often rely on end-to-end pipelines, which overlook individual-specific variations and limit clinical utility. We present a disentanglement framework that integrates contrastive learning with reconstruction regularization, decomposing latent representations into disease-specific, private, and shared components to enable individual-aware disease modeling. Experiments on two datasets show that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, improving diagnostic accuracy and interpretability. These results suggest the proposed framework as a reliable tool for medical image analysis, and the code is released at https://github.com/huangxie666/MVDL.

title: Construction of Binary Sequence Pairs with Equal Periodic Autocorrelation
authors: Soo-Chang Pei, Kuo-Wei Chang
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)

title: MEDCOREF: Co-Training with Pseudo-Label Refinement for Robust Medical Image Classification
authors: Shiming Guo, Yining Zhao, Haodong Chen, Chenghao Yang, Yang Wang, Ali Anaissi, Ali Braytee
publish: ICASSP 2026 - 2026 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
abstract: Deep learning holds great promise for medical image classification; however, its success hinges on large-scale, high-quality annotations that are costly and time-consuming to obtain from expert clinicians, leading to a persistent scarcity of labeled data. While semi-supervised approaches aim to leverage unlabeled images to alleviate this challenge, they frequently suffer from error accumulation in pseudo-labels, which biases model training and degrades generalization.
