title: Distributed Direct Position Determination of Moving Sources in Asynchronous Sensor Networks
authors: Bowen Ding, Dan Song, Weijie Wang, Wei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Existing direct position determination (DPD) methods for moving radiation sources based on time difference of arrival (TDOA) and frequency difference of arrival (FDOA) typically assume synchronous sensor networks, rendering them unsuitable for asynchronous networks affected by clock and frequency biases. To address this limitation and extend applicability to large-scale sensor networks, this paper proposes a distributed DPD algorithm for moving sources in asynchronous sensor networks based on calibration sources. To achieve distributed global optimization of parameters including clock and frequency offsets between neighboring sensors as well as the position and velocity of the source, this study introduces a distributed multiple particle filtering (MPF) framework. Utilizing the coarse estimates provided by the distributed MPF, the algorithm then applies a diffusion-based gradient method to obtain the refined estimates. Throughout the entire process, the radiation source is regarded as an increasingly reliable calibration source. The algorithm jointly utilizes information from the radiation source and the calibration sources to estimate clock and frequency biases. Meanwhile, information exchange in the algorithm is restricted to neighboring sensors, eliminating the need for multi-hop transmission. Experimental results demonstrate consistent convergence of the estimated parameters across sensors and validate the superior performance of the proposed algorithm, even under challenging conditions of low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and limited observation duration.

title: Deep Learning–Enabled Multisource Partial Discharge Diagnostics for Online Monitoring of High Voltage Rotating Machine Insulation
authors: Aryan Raj, Shobhan Barua, J.C. Pandey, Arup Kumar Das, Sivaji Chakravorti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: In this paper, a deep learning–based approach is proposed to identify single and multiple source partial discharge (PD) activities in high-voltage rotating machine insulation employing high-frequency current transformer (HFCT) measurements. To this end, HFCT-acquired PD signals are converted to phase-resolved partial discharge (PRPD) patterns. A comprehensive PRPD database is developed through controlled laboratory experiments involving different insulation materials and discharge mechanisms. Thereafter, a lightweight custom ConvNeXt model is designed for automated feature extraction and classification of the PRPD pattern. The proposed custom ConvNeXt model achieves an overall classification accuracy of 98.3%. Comparative studies with other deep learning models further validate the effectiveness and computational efficiency of the proposed approach. Hence, the proposed method is potentially worthy for online condition monitoring of high-voltage rotating machines.

title: A Frequency Dithering Technique to Attenuate Conducted Electromagnetic Emission of SiC MOSFET Based DC-DC Converter
authors: Vibhav Pandey, Kousik Ghosh, Kamalesh Hatua
abstract: Fast-switching wide-bandgap (WBG) devices in power electronic converters introduce substantial electromagnetic interference (EMI), resulting in supply-side disturbances. Conventional EMI mitigation predominantly relies on passive filters, which negatively affect converter power density. While existing frequency-dithering techniques are effective in reducing EMI and facilitating filter size reduction, they generally neglect the influence of the 9 kHz resolution bandwidth (RBW) specified by CISPR standards. This paper demonstrates that higher RBW values limit the achievable EMI attenuation and therefore must be explicitly considered in dithering design. A frequency-dithering methodology that incorporates the RBW effect and the converter noise-floor characteristics is proposed and evaluated for three nominal switching frequencies of 100 kHz, 300 kHz, and 500 kHz. The proposed algorithm systematically selects the switching and dithering frequencies to enhance EMI attenuation by reducing the number of spectral components within the RBW and by positioning low-amplitude sidebands near the converter noise floor or frequency-response peaks. Experimental validation using a 1kW SiC MOSFET-based DC-DC converter demonstrates up to 11 dB reduction in conducted EMI measured at 9 kHz RBW and enables EMI filter reductions of up to 63%.

title: Techno-Economic Analysis for the Implementation of a Photovoltaic System in Goat Farming Facilities in the Coquimbo Region
authors: Daniel Castro, Gonzalo Toledo, Luis Gonzalez, Héctor Chávez
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: The aim of this paper is to assess the technical and economic feasibility of increasing the support pillar height of a photovoltaic (PV) system for installation on goat farms in Chile's Coquimbo Region. This strategic integration of PV and agriculture results in agrivoltaic systems. The findings indicate that this height increase does not compromise project viability. This research presents a comprehensive review of existing agrivoltaic structural modifications, goat behavioral characteristics, and typical PV configurations. The technical evaluation applied fundamental principles of structural mechanics. The validation was carry on MATLAB and ANSYS of the modified structure performance under the change of the wind pressure. Concurrently, the economic assessment was developed through a cash flow model of the project.

title: An Affordable Robotic Manipulation Platform with Flexible Gripper and Vision for AI-Driven Control
authors: Ismael Lopez-Juarez, Kieran I Lopez-Valadez, Roman Osorio-Comparan, Luis A. Castillo-Barrientos, Daniel Arreguin-Jasso
publish: 2025 IEEE CHILEAN Conference on Electrical, Electronics Engineering, Information and Communication Technologies (CHILECON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of a low-cost, vision-guided robotic system for automated object recognition and manipulation. The platform integrates a MyCobot 280 Pi robotic arm with a custom 3D-printed, stepperactuated gripper featuring compliant TPU fingers. A fixed overhead camera provides real-time visual feedback, enabling HSV-based color segmentation and homographic transformation for accurate object localization within a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$400 \text{mm} \times 400 \text{mm}$</tex> workspace. Experimental results validate the system's ability to reliably detect, grasp, and relocate colored objects. Designed for flexibility and extensibility, the prototype supports research in AI-enhanced robotics, including one-shot learning with large language models (lLMs), tactile sensing, and closed-loop control strategies. Potential application areas include educational robotics, lab automation, light manufacturing, and AI-driven manipulation research.

title: Trusted and Transparent Time-Stamping Through TEEs and Network Time Security
authors: Tobias Nießen, Andrew Paverd
publish: Proceedings of the 9th Workshop on System Software for Trusted Execution
abstract: None

title: Physics-Informed Deep Gated Recurrent Unit Network for Lithium-Ion Battery State of Health Estimation Under Field Conditions
authors: Laijin Luo, Yu Wang, Yang Li, Qiushi Cui
abstract: Accurate estimation of lithium-ion battery state of health (SOH) in electric vehicles (EVs) under real-world conditions is much more challenging than using well-designed laboratory cycling data due to unreliable SOH labeling and segmented charging behavior, and the results often lack interpretability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a physics-informed deep gated recurrent unit (PIDGRU) architecture for robust and interpretable SOH estimation without requiring explicit physical modeling. First, a modified inverse ampere-hour integral method is combined with the Bayesian estimator of abrupt, seasonality, and trend (BEAST) algorithm to estimate battery capacity and characterize SOH uncertainty. A universal feature extraction and selection framework is then developed to handle segmented EV charging data, utilizing a hybrid linear-nonlinear redundancy analysis to ensure an optimal input feature set. The PIDGRU integrates empirical degradation modeling and nonlinear dynamic degradation learning through a deep gated recurrent unit network, utilizing a deep hidden physics model (DeepHPM). A Bayesian inference uncertainty-constrained (BIUC) strategy is introduced to enhance training reliability and uncertainty quantification. Extensive evaluations on both in-vehicle and cross-vehicle datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high accuracy, robustness, and generalizability, with the mean absolute error and root mean squared error consistently below 1.10% and 1.31%, respectively.

title: Split Learning Over NOMA-Enabled HetNets for Scalable IoT-Based Precision Agriculture
authors: Mohammad Arif Hossain, Nazmus Sadat, Nirwan Ansari, Fathi Amsaad
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: The convergence of artificial intelligence (AI) and precision agriculture is driving the rapid deployment of machine learning (ML) models within Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems. However, traditional centralized ML frameworks pose significant challenges in energy efficiency, communication overhead, and real-time responsiveness, especially in remote agricultural environments. To address these limitations, we propose a novel hierarchical split learning framework enabled by non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and deployed over a heterogeneous network (HetNet) topology. In the proposed system, each geographically distributed low-power edge node equipped with a nano-GPU executes a segment of a common deep neural network (DNN) on locally collected agricultural data and transmits intermediate activations to a central aggregator edge node (AEN), which completes the remaining layers and coordinates the backpropagation process. NOMA enables simultaneous data uploads from multiple sensors over shared frequency resources, improving spectral efficiency and supporting scalability. HetNet-based zone segmentation further enhances resource reuse and system coverage. We formulate a comprehensive optimization problem aimed at minimizing total system energy consumption, accounting for both local computation and wireless communication. To solve it, we develop an efficient distributed solution with proven convergence. Extensive simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed framework in reducing energy consumption and transmission delay while maintaining high model accuracy, establishing it as a scalable and sustainable solution for intelligent agricultural systems.

title: AgriGS-SLAM: Orchard Mapping Across Seasons Via Multi-View Gaussian Splatting SLAM
authors: Mirko Usuelli, David Rapado-Rincon, Gert Kootstra, Matteo Matteucci
abstract: Autonomous robots in orchards require real-time 3D scene understanding despite repetitive row geometry, seasonal appearance changes, and wind-driven foliage motion. We present AgriGS-SLAM, a Visual–LiDAR SLAM framework that couples direct LiDAR odometry and loop closures with multi-camera 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) rendering. Batch rasterization across complementary viewpoints recovers orchard structure under occlusions, while a unified gradient-driven map lifecycle executed between keyframes preserves fine details and bounds memory. Pose refinement is guided by a probabilistic LiDAR-based depth consistency term, back-propagated through the camera projection to tighten geometry-appearance coupling. We validate the system on a field platform in apple and pear orchards across dormancy, flowering, and harvesting, using a standardized trajectory protocol that evaluates both training-view and novel-view synthesis to reduce 3DGS overfitting in evaluation. Across seasons and sites, AgriGS-SLAM delivers sharper, more stable reconstructions and steadier trajectories than recent state-of-the-art 3DGS-based SLAM methods while maintaining real-time performance on-tractor. While demonstrated in orchard monitoring, the approach can be applied to other outdoor domains requiring robust multimodal perception.

title: Assessment of modelling fidelity for predicting transmission error in dynamically coupled spur gear systems
authors: Guruparan Pitchai, Lakshmi Pathi Jakkamputi
abstract: In the present study, the transmission error (TE) behaviour of an identical involute spur gear pair dynamically coupled with an attached subsystem is investigated through numerical modelling. The surrounding mechanical system is represented by an equivalent spring–mass subsystem, and the transmission error response is examined over a range of operating conditions associated with different resonance regimes. Two finite element–based modelling approaches are considered to describe the local elastic deformations occurring at the gear mesh. In the fully coupled finite element formulation, the interaction between meshing gear teeth is described using nonlinear contact conditions implemented through an augmented Lagrangian approach, in which tooth contact behaviour, elastic deformation, and subsystem dynamics are treated simultaneously. In the reduced hybrid finite element formulation, elastic deformations arising from tooth bending and contact compliance are evaluated separately under quasi-static equilibrium and subsequently combined to estimate the overall mesh deformation and transmission error. Quasi-static analyses are conducted to assess the accuracy of both modelling approaches through comparison with published benchmark results at multiple load levels. Dynamic simulations based on the fully coupled formulation are then used to investigate transmission error behaviour in quasi-static, near-resonant, resonant, and post-resonant operating regimes of the attached subsystem. The effect of the subsystem resonance on the amplitude and harmonic content of transmission error is examined. The results indicate that subsystem dynamics have a pronounced influence on transmission error behaviour, with peak-to-peak values increasing by up to approximately 70% at the fundamental resonance of the attached subsystem and with associated changes in harmonic content that persist beyond the resonance region. The modelling approaches are compared in terms of predictive capability across resonance regimes, computational effort, and relevance to automotive gear transmission applications.

title: Optimal Fractional-Order Terminal Synergetic Controller for Grid-Connected DFIG-based Wind-PV Hybrid Energy Conversion Systems
authors: Cheyma Aribi, Messaouda Azzouzi, Motab Turki Almousa, Hegazy Rezk
abstract: Hybrid renewable energy systems (HRESs) require advanced control strategies to ensure grid stability and reliable integration under variable operating conditions. Although Conventional Synergetic Control (SC) is an effective nonlinear controller, it often lacks robustness and adaptability. This limitation arises from fixed, manually tuned parameters, limited dynamic flexibility, and suboptimal performance under parametric uncertainties and external disturbances. This paper proposes a Fractional-Order Terminal Synergetic Controller (FOTSC) for both the rotor-side and grid-side converters of a doubly-fed induction generator (DFIG) in a hybrid wind-photovoltaic (PV) system, where the PV system acts as a supplementary source. The controller parameters are optimally tuned using an Enzyme Activation Optimizer (EAO), which minimizes a formulated objective function based on the Integral of Time multiplied by the Absolute Error (ITAE) criterion to systematically balance the transient response and steady-state accuracy. The FOTSC introduces fractional-order dynamics and terminal attractors to ensure finite-time convergence, whereas EAO optimization enhances the robustness and consistency across operating conditions. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results highlight that the optimized FOTSC (OFOTSC) outperforms both the manually-tuned FOTSC and Conventional SC, reducing the overshoot in active power, reactive power, rotor currents, DC-link voltage, and grid currents by up to 58%, and shortening the settling times by up to 43%. In terms of power quality, the OFOTSC achieves a Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) of 1.37% for the grid-injected currents, which is well below the IEEE 519-2014 standard limit of 5%. This represents a significant reduction of 84% compared to the Conventional SC (THD = 8.75%) and 67% compared to the manually-tuned FOTSC (THD = 4.14%). These findings confirm that the EAO-tuned FOTSC significantly enhances the stability, power quality, and robustness of DFIG-based HRES.

title: A Survey on Spiking Neural Network Foundation And Recent Progress
authors: Nhan Trong Luu, Duong Trung Luu, Pham Ngoc Nam, Truong Cong Thang
abstract: Over the past decade, deep neural networks (DNNs) have achieved remarkable success across numerous fields. Despite this progress, DNNs remain highly demanding in terms of computational power, energy consumption, and data requirements. As the demand for autonomous systems such as self-driving cars, drones, and collaborative robots, etc. continues to rise, the deployment of DNNs in these real-world scenarios has gained significant attention. However, these applications require energy-efficient and computationally lightweight models to ensure real-time performance under limited power resources. A promising alternative has emerged in the form of biologically inspired spiking neural networks (SNNs), which offer a more energy-efficient and temporally dynamic computing paradigm. By mimicking the behavior of biological neurons, SNNs provide a bridge between neuroscience and machine learning, leveraging neural sparsity and temporal coding mechanisms inherent to biological systems. In this work, we make the following contributions: provide a comprehensive overview of biological neuron theories; summarize existing spike-based neuron models from neuroscience; describe synaptic modeling approaches; review conventional artificial neural networks; outline several methodologies for preprocessing, training and conversion approaches for spike-based models; explore SNN applications in various fields; discuss major frameworks supporting SNN implementation.

title: Spectroscopic Characterization of Plasmoid Properties During Pellet Fueling in W7-X
authors: Edgardo Villalobos, Jürgen Baldzuhn, Ameer I. Mohammed, Larry Baylor, Nerea Panadero
abstract: This study utilizes a spectroscopic approach to investigate the properties of plasmoids that are formed during the process of cryogenic hydrogen pellet fueling in the Wendelstein 7-X (W7-X) stellarator. An analysis of the Balmer series emissions was conducted using a diagnostic that was installed during the 2024 operational campaign. Electron temperature, density, and plasma beta (b) values can be inferred from the emissions of radiation from the ablation plasmoid. These values are essential for validating pellet ablation models and, in the future, optimizing fueling strategies in steady-state fusion devices.

title: Power Diagram Enhanced Adaptive Isosurface Extraction from Signed Distance Fields
authors: Pengfei Wang, Ziyang Zhang, Wensong Wang, Shuangmin Chen, Lin Lu, Shiqing Xin, Changhe Tu, Wenping Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Extracting high-fidelity mesh surfaces from Signed Distance Fields (SDFs) has become a fundamental operation in geometry processing. Despite significant progress over the past decades, key challenges remain—namely, how to automatically capture the intricate geometric and topological structures en coded in the zero level set of SDFs. In this paper, we present a novel isosurface extraction algorithm that introduces two key innovations: 1) An incrementally constructed power diagram through the addition of sample points, which enables repeated updates to the extracted surface via its dual—regular Delaunay tetrahedralization; and 2) An adaptive point insertion strategy that identifies regions exhibiting the greatest discrepancy between the current mesh and the underlying continuous surface. As the teaser figure shows, our framework progressively refines the extracted mesh with minimal computational cost until it sufficiently approximates the underlying surface. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the art methods, particularly for models with intricate geometric variations and complex topologies

title: A SAC-GHULSTM Algorithm for Consumer Robot Navigation in Complex Indoor Environments with Progressive Training_supp1-3685311.pdf
authors: Yuemin Ding
abstract: To improve the robustness and efficiency of consumer mobile robot navigation in complex indoor environments, this paper presents a deep reinforcement learning method named SAC-GHULSTM. The method integrates a gated highway unit enhanced long short-term memory (GHULSTM) into the soft actor-critic (SAC) framework, combined with a progressive training strategy. Standard LSTM networks tend to exhibit limited effectiveness in preserving informative temporal features when processing long-sequence navigation data, which can lead to slow convergence and suboptimal stability performance. To address this issue, a GHULSTM network structure is proposed. By integrating GHU into each hidden layer of the LSTM, the hidden state can directly retain and transmit key historical sequence information while fusing with current inputs. This design enhances long-term information retention and improves the effectiveness of temporal dependency modeling in long-horizon navigation scenarios. Additionally, a curriculum-based progressive training mechanism is designed during the training phase. This approach ensures the algorithm quickly learns effective strategies in initial simple scenarios and gradually adapts to complex environments, thereby improving its generalization ability. Experimental results in scenarios with output noise demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly accelerates training convergence and enhances stable-phase performance. In particular, the robot’s average collision rate is substantially reduced, the target arrival rate is notably improved, and the GHULSTM-based strategy combined with progressive training achieves superior target arrival efficiency in identical test scenarios, enabling reliable target reaching.

title: LEO Satellite-Enabled ISAC: Bistatic Framework and Beamforming Design
authors: Yuexia Zhang, Yuanshuo Gang, Tianqi Mao, Ruiqi Liu, Meng Hua, Qingqing Wu
abstract: With advantages such as wide coverage, high data rates, and precise target localization, low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites show great potential in integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) and are expected to play a pivotal role in 6G networks. However, LEO satellite ISAC systems face several challenges such as highly dynamic channel environments, limited onboard payloads, and significant attenuation of long-link sensing echoes, which hinder their effectiveness in high-precision sensing and high-quality communication tasks. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a LEO satellite-enabled bistatic ISAC framework, wherein the LEO satellite performs both communication and sensing tasks, while a terrestrial gateway is responsible for receiving the echo signals. This design reduces path loss and enhances airborne target detection accuracy. The paper derives the achievable rate expression based on statistical channel state information and the Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) for two-dimensional angle of arrival estimation. Furthermore, an optimization problem is formulated to minimize the CRB while satisfying communication constraints: 1) For the hybrid beamformer (HBF), a penalty dual decomposition method is employed, and a two-layer iterative optimization algorithm is proposed to optimize beamforming while satisfying the unit-modulus constraint. 2) For the fully digital beamformer (FDBF), a globally optimal solution is derived using the semidefinite relaxation technique, and a closed-form solution is obtained to reduce computational complexity. Simulation results demonstrate that: 1) Compared with the monostatic framework, the bistatic framework significantly improves CRB performance, enabling higher-precision target sensing in LEO satellite ISAC systems. 2) The proposed algorithms achieve excellent target sensing accuracy while effectively ensuring communication quality for ground users. 3) The proposed HBF design approaches the performance of the FDBF design while requiring fewer radio frequency chains.

title: A Keypoint-Based Approach to Estimating Spinal Curvature from 2D Images
authors: Rafał Wysocki, Aleksandra Świetlicka
abstract: Pose estimation is one of the most frequently discussed topics in the field of machine vision. Nevertheless, there are not enough models that effectively solve problems related to spinal detection and assessment. The subject of this project is the development and evaluation of a system for the automatic detection of spinal curvature, followed by the use of the obtained model to detect abnormalities. The system is dedicated to strength training; nonetheless, it could serve as a supportive tool for individuals engaged in other physical exercises. The assessment is based on a keypoint estimation model, with points selected to provide the most informative representation while maintaining a minimal model structure. In this research, several model variants are presented, ranging from lightweight networks to advanced architectures. The results obtained allow us to conclude that the models created for the project meet the requirements set for them in terms of both stability and effectiveness. The models presented in this publication are suitable for further development and wider use in areas such as rehabilitation and motion analysis.

title: Delayed Backdoor: Let the Trigger Fly for a While in Backdoor Attack on Internet of Things
authors: Bo Liu, Peiwen Zhu, Shaofeng Zhao, Xiang Chen, Haojie Huang, Liling Shi, Xiaokang Wang, Zhigao Zheng, Laurence T. Yang
abstract: Since Large Language Models (LLMs) have gained wide attention as the core of AI agents in IoT for conversation and generation tasks, their security issues have become more prominent, especially regarding backdoor attacks. Traditional backdoor attacks often rely on fixed triggers and static outputs, failing to fully exploit the conversational characteristics and generativity of LLMs, which limits their stealth and attack effectiveness in complex human-agent interactions. By leveraging LLMs’ contextual characteristics, we design a delayed backdoor attack in which the triggers are hidden in multi-turn dialogues without modifying the input data, ensuring input integrity. This delayed attack makes the trigger dissociate from poisoned data to enhance stealth and generalization, posing a significant threat to autonomous systems. Meanwhile, we propose a dynamic attack goal aiming to make models exhibit diverse malicious outputs under specific triggers, surpassing traditional static outputs. Experimental results show that our method significantly outperforms traditional methods, achieving a 20% to 80% performance improvement with a 5% poisoning rate and demonstrating stronger robustness under defense mechanisms, highlighting a critical vulnerability in the security of AI agents for IoT.

title: Soft labelling for deep ordinal classification: an experimental review_supp1-3681678.pdf
authors: David Guijo-Rubio

title: LightFire-Net: Efficient Cross-Modal Spectral–Spatial Information Fusion for Real-Time Wildfire Detection in Remote Sensing
authors: Muhammad John Abbas, Muhammad Attique Khan, Jiaxin Li, Irfan Haider, Latifah Almuqren, Mohammad Alhefdi, Jungpil Shin, Yunyoung Nam
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Wildfires are among the most devastating natural disasters, causing significant damage to the environment, the economy, wildlife, and human lives, and requiring an early detection system. This paper presents a novel LightFire-Net deep cross model fusion architecture for Wildfire classification and interpretation from remote sensing images. The proposed model is computationally efficient and employs Efficient Flame Pattern blocks with depthwise separable convolutional layers, a Lightweight spectral spatial module, and Efficient Feature Fusion for real-time wildfire classification and detection. The model's multi-stage architecture enables hierarchical feature extraction, resulting in more accurate and efficient classification. Bayesian Optimization (BO) is employed to select hyperparameters, thereby improving the training of the proposed model on selected remote sensing datasets. A Softmax classification layer is employed for the final prediction. The proposed model achieves exceptional performance with above 98% prediction accuracy while maintaining only 1.37 million parameters and an inference speed of 7.74 ms. A comprehensive evaluation across multiple dataset splits (90:05:05-40:30:30) demonstrates robust generalization, with accuracy ranging from 98.02% to 99.25%. The five-fold mean accuracy further confirms the model's stability, at 98.62%±0.15%. The proposed model outperforms existing CNN and transformer-based architectures while being significantly more efficient, making it highly suitable and reliable for real-time monitoring in resource-constrained environments.

title: Shielding Federated Learning Services From a Novel Stealthy Label-Flipping Attack Exposing Membership
authors: Geetanjli Sharma, M.A.P. Chamikara, Mohan Baruwal Chhetri, Yi-Ping Phoebe Chen
abstract: Label-flipping attacks pose a significant threat to the security and privacy of machine learning models, particularly in federated learning (FL). While existing defenses, such as FoolsGold and Krum, aim to mitigate these attacks, they often fail to account for more subtle adversarial behaviors, such as intermittent class-targeted flips. In this work, we introduce the Stealthy Label-Flipping Attack (SLFA), which exposes this overlooked vulnerability in FL services. SLFA is an adaptive attack that intermittently flips a fixed subset of class labels across multiple communication rounds before reverting them to their original state. We show that this transient manipulation (i) evades state-of-the-art poisoning and anomaly detectors, (ii) preserves overall model utility, and (iii) introduces gradient instability during the attacking rounds, thereby increasing the risk of privacy leakage. To counter this hybrid threat that not only evades state-of-the-art defenses but also amplifies privacy leakage, we propose FLStealthShield, a server-side defense mechanism that detects and neutralizes such adaptive attacks by analyzing temporal patterns in client updates. Extensive experiments under both IID and non-IID data distributions demonstrate that FLStealthShield achieves Byzantine robustness and maintains high model utility, outperforming existing defenses even when over 40% of clients are malicious.

title: SR-Net: A Heterogeneous Point Clouds Registration Method Based on Deep Learning
authors: Tongtong Lu, Xiaoyong Qiang, Jian Wang, Binfu Ge, Hao Guan, Chaoyang Bian, Fang Huang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The task of registering heterogeneous point clouds of urban scenes is particularly challenging due to differences in the imaging mechanisms of sensors and the limitations imposed by the conditions under which these images are acquired. The relevant challenges include a large number of outliers, differences in the densities of point clouds of several orders of magnitude, low rates of overlap between them, data scarcity, and large rotational angles that yield severe mismatches in the data distribution. These issues create significant performance-related bottlenecks for traditional methods of point cloud registration as well as those based on feature extraction through deep learning. In response to these issues, this study proposes a framework for registering heterogeneous point clouds called the super-point registration network (SR-Net). SR-Net consists of a pyramid feature extraction module based on KPConv, a super-point feature extraction and matching module, a local feature extraction module, and a local-to-global registration method. To enhance the capability of the model to extract features from heterogeneous point clouds with different resolutions, the authors propose an adaptive Top-K method for point cloud padding. The model incorporates self-attention and cross-attention mechanisms to capture the geometric structure within point clouds and the geometric consistency between them. The geometric information contained in the super-points is encoded through geometric structure embedding. Finally, to compensate for the scarcity of annotated multimodal data, we pre-trained the model on large-scale homologous benchmarks. This enabled it to learn the underlying invariant geometric feature priors across domains that guided it to stably converge on a dataset collected by the authors that involved major differences in the distribution of the features. We comprehensively validated the proposed model through a progressive experimental system (EXP-I∼VII) that covered basic evaluation, ablation, cross-domain generalization, and stress testing for efficiency. SR-Net achieved a relative rotational error (RRE) of 0.24 ° and a registration recall (RR) of 99.8% on the KITTI benchmark. Moreover, it achieved an RR of 96.97%, an RRE of 0.56 °, and a relative translational error (RTE) of 0.92 m on a challenging dataset of heterogeneous point clouds that was collected by the authors, with an average overlap rate of only 7.62% that reflected optimal stability. Our model also yielded an RR of 92.6% and a RTE of 0.31 m in experiments on the Cross3DReg heterologous datasets, significantly surpassing mainstream methods. The final evaluation confirmed that it achieved a high RR of 91.2% on 3DMatch, while optimizing the inferential delay to 85.96 ms. This verifies the high robustness of SR-Net when handling complex tasks of multimodal point cloud registration.

title: HemoSC-P: A Hemodynamic Semantic Channel Paradigm for Cardiovascular Parameter Estimation
authors: Xi Qiu, Zhifei Zhang, Hailin Cao, Li Yang, Hao Tang, Lianyang Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: As a leading cause of death worldwide, cardiovascular disease demands more precise monitoring and early warning systems, posing significant challenges to modern healthcare. However, cardiovascular early warning systems often face two major dilemmas: the “black box dilemma” leads to unreliable estimation results due to limited physiological interpretability, and limited robustness across subjects and blood pressure states arises from physiological heterogeneity. This study innovatively maps the methods of semantic extraction and channel modeling to the cardiovascular system, inspired by the 6G network concept of transmitting meaning rather than data under the semantic communication paradigm. It proposes a novel hemodynamic channel-guided cardiovascular parameter estimation paradigm (HemoSC-P). This paradigm adopts a dual-pillar modeling framework: the semantic pillar utilizes a multi-scale convolutional and phase-aware attention architecture to model the non-stationary dynamics of cardiovascular signals, elevating feature alignment from the temporal domain to the physiological domain. The cardiovascular channel, parameterized by the Windkessel circuit model, serves dual roles as a semantic pathway from cardiac contractions to observable physiological signals and as a repository of physiological knowledge. It guides channel-level physiologically deformable attention to achieve inverse estimation of cardiovascular parameters. To validate this paradigm, this study employs non-invasive blood pressure estimation as a representative case study. Validation was conducted on three representative public datasets (UCI-BP, MIMIC-III, PPG-BP). On MIMIC-III, the mean absolute error $\pm$ standard deviation for systolic and diastolic blood pressure reached 3.04 $\pm$ 3.24 mmHg and 2.57 $\pm$ 2.70 mmHg, respectively. Multi-dataset validation indicates that this paradigm surpasses benchmark methods in accuracy and stability while maintaining scalability.

title: Gait-Based Person Identification in the DAS System Using HOG-MAE
authors: Shang Shi, Kun Gui, Pengcheng Wang, Hongliang Ren, Jin Lu, Changqiu Yu, Quanjun Cao, Guomin Gu, Qi Xuan
abstract: This article proposes HOG-MAE, a novel self-supervised learning framework designed for efficient person identification using Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) spatiotemporal gait signals. To mitigate the high costs of manual annotation in DAS deployment, we develop a masked autoencoder that targets the reconstruction of Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) features. By reconstructing HOG features instead of raw pixels, the model prioritizes essential semantic attributes—specifically the spatial-structural geometry of strides and rhythmic frequency patterns—while maintaining inherent invariance to non-essential variations, such as background noise and signal intensity fluctuations caused by diverse footwear or walking speeds. Furthermore, leveraging the asymmetric distribution of spatial and temporal information in DAS signals, we further compress the gait semantic features, thereby achieving a lightweight model and reducing pre-training time. Experimental results demonstrate that HOG-MAE achieves a classification accuracy of 96.86% and reduces pretraining time by 22.08% compared to the standard MAE under the same epoch settings.

title: Forecasting Customer Attrition in Digital Banking with Real-Time Deep Learning
authors: Rajat Gera, Sandhya Antony M, Nisarga H A, Chirag V, Gangothri V, Chaturya Poornashree B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: In digital banking, the challenge of customer attrition is vital since the threat of service choice and resistance to switch increases due to an increase in service options and low switching costs. The nonlinear, dynamic trends in customer behavior are difficult to model using conventional statistical and rule-of-thumb models, leading to subsequent delayed or inaccurate forecasts. To propose one real-time deep learning model that predicts attrition based on behavioral telemetry, transactional sequence, frequency of interaction, and engagement. The system achieves a hybrid temporal structure, which is a combination of sequence modelling based on LSTM, feature weighting based on attention, and highdimensional learning with a multilayer predictive fusion block. Experimental analysis shows that the proposed framework has the highest prediction accuracy, 95.8%, F 1 -score, 94.6%, AUC 96.2%, and a decrease in false positives of 21.3%, whereas the time to detect early warnings increases by 31.7%. The model further performs better than the baseline machine learning systems and deep learners, with an overall performance improvement of 12 to 18% on core measures. The results support the fact that realtime deep learning-based churn forecasting has the potential to improve retention approaches, risk reduction, and data-driven decision-making in contemporary digital banking settings.

title: Development of a Digital Twin for Spacecraft Proximity Operations
authors: Martina Ferrauto, Matteo Melchiorre, Laura Salamina, Erica Scantamburlo, Davide Sorli, Stefano Mauro
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: Autonomous rendezvous and capture are key capabilities for future on-orbit servicing (OOS) missions. Two main strategies are typically employed: berthing, where a robotic manipulator seizes the target, and docking, often implemented through probe-and-drogue mechanisms. While both approaches are well established in spaceflight operations, their detailed modeling and systematic comparison remain open research challenges. This paper introduces a dedicated multibody framework developed within the MUSAPOEM project to analyze and contrast berthing and docking operations. The berthing model comprises a servicer spacecraft, a non-cooperative target, and a 7-DOF robotic manipulator equipped with a gripper-type end effector. In the proposed approach, detailed CAD-based geometries are decomposed into convex subcomponents, enabling the accurate simulation of probe insertion, compliant latching, and nonlinear contact force exchanges within Simscape Multibody. A comparative analysis highlights the complementary nature of the two approaches. Berthing offers adaptability and robustness when dealing with unprepared or uncooperative targets, whereas docking provides a compact, autonomous solution optimized for cooperative missions with standardized interfaces. Although the docking results are currently limited to model development, the framework establishes a solid basis for extending the analysis to hard docking and for integrating control strategies. In this way, the work contributes to advancing high-fidelity simulation tools that support the design and validation of next-generation OOS missions.

title: RIS-Assisted SWIPT Under Bounded CSI Uncertainty: A Worst-Case Max-Min Approach
authors: Dev Shah, Kunwar Pritiraj Rajput, Manish Kumar
abstract: This work studies a multiuser simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) network assisted by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). A realistic scenario with imperfect channel state information (CSI) is considered and modeled using a bounded uncertainty framework. With a user fairness objective, we maximize the minimum worst-case signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) by jointly optimizing the base station (BS) beamformers, RIS reflection coefficients, and user power-splitting ratios subject to BS transmit power and minimum harvested energy constraints. The resulting nonconvex problem is solved through an alternating optimization method that divides the design into three tractable parts. Robustness against CSI errors is achieved by reformulating the worst-case constraints into linear matrix inequalities via S-procedure and sign definiteness-based tools. Simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed robust design and demonstrate clear SINR gains over uncertainty-agnostic schemes, highlighting the benefit of the proposed robust design.

title: Hybrid Algorithm of Finite State Machine State Assignment for Power Minimization
authors: Tomasz Grzes
publish: 2025 27th International Symposium on Symbolic and Numeric Algorithms for Scientific Computing (SYNASC)
abstract: The presented paper discusses a hybrid algorithm for minimizing the power consumed by the finite state machine (FSM). The proposed algorithm is based on the modification of three algorithms: the sequential algorithm, where code assignment is based on the previous encoding, state splitting, where a state is divided into several states, and using a special model of an FSM, which lead to the reduction of the length of the internal state's code. Modifications to the algorithms include using the second or third best element in the sequential algorithm, splitting the state into more than two new states, and using different lengths for state code. Every modification is tested to see if it leads to a decrease in the power in the FSM. The proposed algorithm executes methods starting from the state splitting, followed by the implementation of the common architectural model, and finally, the sequential method of state encoding. The experimental results show that the proposed method reduces the power consumption compared to NOVA, JEDI, column-based based and original sequential algorithms by at least 16%.

title: Optimized DeepLabV3+ Architecture for Semantic Segmentation
authors: Cristina Cărunta, Călin-Adrian Popa
publish: 2025 27th International Symposium on Symbolic and Numeric Algorithms for Scientific Computing (SYNASC)
abstract: Designing highly accurate lightweight semantic segmentation models is an essential task in the computer vision field due to the necessity to deploy these segmentation models in real-world scenarios. Developing optimized architectures is a challenging research direction since it needs to maintain a right balance between segmentation accuracy and inference speed. Therefore, we consider to rethink an advanced semantic segmentation model, called DeepLabV3+, into a more lightweight approach, enhancing its real-time performance. Through our method, we achieve better optimization across three important performance metrics, i.e., inference speed, GFLOPs (Giga Floating Point Operations), and the number of learnable parameters, while maintaining the accuracy at the same level. Our method is validated through extensive experiments conducted on two urban street scene datasets, namely Cityscapes and CamVid. In particular, on the Cityscapes dataset, our optimized model demonstrates a significant speed-up improvement from 276 FPS to 289 FPS on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$512 \times 1024$</tex> input resolution, a 75% reduction in learnable parameters, and a lower computational load of the model, 10.8 GFLOPs compared to 18.8 GFLOPs, and all these results occur while preserving the 74% accuracy level.

title: Prototyping of a Customised Automated Water Sampling System for Quality Monitoring
authors: José Gonçalves, J. A. B. Coelho, Mariano Alvarez, Laiany Brancalião, Paulo Matos, João Paulo Coelho
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: This paper presents the development of an automated water sampling system designed to enhance quality control in water treatment facilities. The system is built around a mechanical structure that houses a watertight box containing all electronic components. A display inside the box allows users to program sampling schedules, including parameters such as the day, time, number of samples, sample volume, and intervals between samples. A balance integrated into the structure holds a bottle that collects the water samples, while a reservoir at the bottom accumulates water to ensure an adequate supply. A water pump connected to the structure enables controlled sample collection. The design ensures that all components are compactly integrated, while a non-invasive method is used to measure the volume of the sampled water, thereby avoiding direct contact between the sensors and the sample. A Project-Based Learning (PBL) approach, coupled with direct industry collaboration, has reinforced the effectiveness of active learning methodologies in engineering education.

title: Multi-Beholder: Biomarker Prediction for Low-Grade Glioma with Multiple Instance Learning and One-Class Classification
authors: Zijie Fang, Yihan Liu, Yifeng Wang, Xiangyang Zhang, Yang Chen, Changjing Cai, Yiyang Lin, Ying Han, Zhi Wang, Shan Zeng, Jun Tan, Yongbing Zhang, Hong Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Biomarker detection is an indispensable part of the diagnosis and treatment of low-grade glioma (LGG). However, current LGG biomarker detection methods rely on expensive and complex molecular genetic testing, for which professionals are required to analyze the results, and intra-rater variability is often reported. To overcome these challenges, we propose an interpretable deep learning pipeline, named Multi-Biomarker Histomorphology Discoverer (Multi-Beholder), to predict the status of five biomarkers in LGG using only hematoxylin and eosin-stained whole slide images. Specifically, Multi-Beholder incorporates one-class classification into the multiple instance learning framework to achieve accurate instance-level pseudo-labeling, thereby complementing slide-level labels and improving prediction performance. Multi-Beholder demonstrates high performance on two LGG cohorts with diverse races and scanning protocols, with area under the receiver operating characteristic curve up to 0.973 on the internal-validated TCGA-LGG dataset and 0.820 on the external-validated Xiangya cohort. Moreover, the interpretability of Multi-Beholder allows for discovering quantitative and qualitative correlations between biomarker status and histomorphology characteristics. Our pipeline not only provides a novel approach for biomarker prediction, enhancing the applicability of molecular treatments for LGG patients but also facilitates the discovery of new mechanisms in molecular functionality and LGG progression. Code can be accessed at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Vison307/Multi-Beholder</uri>.

title: Experimental Mitigation of Radiated Electromagnetic Interference in Variable-Frequency Drive Systems
authors: Hasen Zejćirović, Majda Tešanović, Sanela Užičanin, Lejla Mehanović
abstract: Variable-frequency drives (VFDs) have enabled the use of robust induction motors in industrial, regulated electric drive systems. Their application has led to increased reliability, energy efficiency, and flexibility of industrial processes. However, their operation, based on pulse-width modulation (PWM), results in the occurrence of electromagnetic interference (EMI) and degradation of power quality in the supply network. This paper presents an experimental analysis of electromagnetic interference in a low-power VFD system under laboratory conditions. The investigation was conducted on an Altivar 320 VFD supplying a 250 W induction motor operating in no-load mode. The influence of the switching frequency of the VFD on radiated electromagnetic interference was analyzed. In addition, the effectiveness of different EMI mitigation methods was experimentally examined. The obtained results indicate a correlation between the operating mode of the drive and the level of radiated electromagnetic interference.

title: Interpretable Churn Prediction in Motor Liability Insurance: A Comparative Analysis of Ensemble Methods and SHAP Explanations
authors: Ajla Ćerimagić Hasibović, Muhamed Hasić, Anel Tanović
abstract: Although motor third-party liability insurance is mandatory in most countries, it is very difficult to retain customers with a single insurance company. Retaining clients is crucial, as acquiring new ones is very expensive and doing business with new clients is always risky, whereas retaining existing clients allows insurance companies to focus their resources on other objectives. To retain customers, we use machine learning (ML) algorithms. Although advanced ML algorithms have demonstrated superior predictive performance compared to traditional actuarial methods, their lack of transparency (“black box”) hinders their use in regulatory environments that require explainability. This paper proposes a hybrid churn prediction framework that balances high accuracy with interpretability. We use ensemble learning techniques, specifically CatBoost, trained on a real dataset of automobile liability policies. To address the problem of class imbalance, typical in insurance data, we use the synthetic minority oversampling technique (SMOTE). The main contribution of this work is the integration of the SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) method for deconstructing model predictions. This approach provides global insight into key churn drivers – such as premium fluctuations and vehicle age – and local explanations at the individual policy level. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieves a higher AUC-ROC score than basic logistic regression models. Furthermore, the use of XAI (Explainable AI) enables insurers to develop targeted retention strategies while adhering to transparency standards.

title: AI-Augmented OBE Framework for French Listening-Speaking Instruction: A Data-Driven Adaptive Learning System with Real-Time Feedback
authors: Yunhan Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Individual and adaptive approaches to teaching a foreign language should be employed to develop effective listening and speaking skills in the foreign language. The suggested paper presents an outcome-oriented education (OBE) model of learning on how to teach French listening and speaking, complemented by the use of AI and a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based model of Speech Recognition and Natural Language Processing (NLP) as a real-time assessment and feedback mechanism. The system documents oral learner feedback, evaluates oral pronunciation, oral fluency, and oral comprehension, and provides corrective feedback to learners on an individual basis in the form of feedbacks that are aligned with the established predetermined OBE objectives. An adaptive learning engine is a reinforcement-based engine, which modifies the difficulty of the task, suggests particular exercises, and adjusts dynamically the learning paths according to the performance of the learner. The teachers will have the opportunity of viewing detailed visualizations of the learner progress and they have the ability to initiate appropriate interventions and make instructional decisions on the basis of the data.

title: An Experimental Methodology to Predict Cyber Attacks Using Learning-Assisted Generative AI Technology
authors: Deepa G, Geetha K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Cyber attacks have taken a new form in the dynamic digital environment and are extremely threatening to the critical infrastructure, state systems, and organizational networks. The current intrusion detection systems tend to be based on the traditional machine learning models that fail to deal with the class imbalance, sparse data, and limited ability to generalize towards unknown attacks or zero-day attacks. This paper is a new hybrid learning-supported model that combines a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) with Bi-LSTM and Transformer structure to improve predictive accuracy and resilience. The methodology starts by preprocessing NSL-KDD dataset and then extracting features and rebalancing the classes using SMOTE and synthetic samples generated by VAE. The main novelty is that it combines the generative augmentation approach with ensemble deep learning models, which provide the ability to be both temporally and contextually aware of more elaborate attack behavior. The proposed model did attain the total accuracy of 98.79, which exceeded the traditional classifiers such as Random Forest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(91.12 \%)$</tex>, SVM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(88.34 \%)$</tex>, and even the independent deep learners, such as Bi-LSTM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(94.88 \%)$</tex>. The level of detection in classes was also better and the minority attacks such as U2R attacks and R2L attacks were also well detected, unlike traditional systems. The discriminative power of the model was also ensured with the help of ROC-AUC analysis (0.994) and class-wise measures of precision. This experimental design proves that learning-assisted generative AI can become a promising approach to high-fidelity cyber threat detection, which allows one to intervene in the early stage. Adversarial training and sequence modeling is also presented in the framework to provide flexibility in adapting to dynamic cyber environments. Future directions will involve implementation in real-time settings and extrapolate the model to multimodal cybersecurity data to achieve higher levels of generalization and applicability to a real-life setting.

title: Design and Experimental Verification of a Four-Bar Separation Mechanism
authors: Licheng Liu, Shaohua Wang, Kang Gong, Tiejun Wang, Jinkui Gu, Haoran Jia, Gang Wang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Automation, Robotics and Applications (ICARA)
abstract: This paper addresses the requirement for precise payload deployment during deorbiting missions by designing a reusable four-bar linkage separation mechanism. It first outlines the system configuration, including core components such as the mounting structure, linkage mechanism, unlocking mechanism, and locking mechanism. Subsequently, it details the operational principles across three phases: initial state, payload loading, and payload deployment. Key parameters were determined through structural design. ADAMS kinematic analysis verifies that the mechanism can withstand maximum flight loads except for isolated components. Manual functional tests and payload release tests confirm normal locking/unlocking operations with execution times <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 10$</tex> milliseconds and no damage. This mechanism provides a reference design for similar separation devices.

title: FADIoT: Federated Anomaly Detection Framework for DDoS Attacks in IoT
authors: Tushar Rana, Sai Akshita Dimpu Pagoti, Neha Agrawal, Amulya Namburi
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks pose a severe threat to resource-constrained and heterogeneous Internet of Things (IoT) networks. This paper presents a three-layer IoT-Fog-Cloud architecture for collaborative, scalable, and lowlatency anomaly detection. The framework combines Continuous Ranked Probability Score with Exponential Smoothing (CRPS-ES) and Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for feature extraction, with a federated threshold aggregation mechanism to preserve privacy. Localized fog processing enables real-time analysis, while a CNN-BiLSTM model with federated learning enhances detection accuracy without sharing raw data. Experiments on heterogeneous IoT environments achieved up to 99% detection accuracy, outperforming existing CRPS-based methods, offering a robust, privacy-preserving defense against evolving DDoS threats.

title: AI-Enhanced Stock Screening System for Multi-Bagger Identification Using Financial and Market Indicators
authors: Muhamed Rafi, G T Sasetharan, Al Thasni C, Soumya. B, Althaf V T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: The paper will outline a new AI-enhanced stock screening tool to find possible multi-bagger stocks using the combination of financial and market-indicator-driven approach. The offered system utilizes an integrated machine-learning pipeline that combines a set of standard measures, including revenue growth, profitability ratios, cash-flow stability, and debt efficiency, and technical features, such as trend strength, volatility changes, and momentum signals. A feature-engineering layer is added that converts the raw financial statements and market data into predictive variables that are best suited to detect long-term values. The model also has an adaptive weighting mechanism that dynamically changes the weighting of indicators depending on the changing market conditions. The system predicts better and reduces false positives by training gradient-boosting algorithms and ensemble learning on multi-bagger forecasting. Multi-sector historical back-testing shows that the discovery of stocks at their early-stage is significantly enhanced using the machine learning approach as compared to the conventional screening heuristics. The findings confirm the fact that AI-based hybrid analysis has a significant positive impact on the reliability, speed, and accuracy of stocks with high long-term compounding potential.

title: Prediction of Diabetic Nephropathy Using Machine Learning Algorithms Based on Crucial Eight Clinical Features
authors: Seeta Devi, Manisha Mistry
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Objective It is still challenging to precisely differentiate diabetic nephropathy from non-diabetic renal disease. In order to promote the differential diagnosis between diabetic nephropathy and non-diabetic renal conditions, the current study aims to develop and validate a machine learning-based model that utilizes the utilizes clinical parameters. Methods: We included 1,077 patients from a Maharashtra hospital between January 2024 and April 2025. Clinical features including numbness, sensitivity to touch, nocturia, proteinuria, swelling, and more are present in the dataset. The presence or absence of diabetic nephropathy was determined by a binary target variable. Several machine learning algorithms, including NB, AB, SGD, Gradient Boosting, CN2 Rule Inducer, NN, SVM, and kNN, were used to pre-process and analyze the dataset. Ten-fold cross-validation was employed to assess the model's performance, and each model's accuracy and AUC were examined. Results: AdaBoost outperformed Gradient Boosting and CN2 Rule Inducer (AUC 0.805, accuracy 0.737), with the highest AUC of 0.807 and accuracy of 0.738. With an AUC of 0.500, the SGD model wasn't performing well. Strong correlations between clinical characteristics, including touch sensitivity and urine protein, and diabetic nephropathy were found by the feature analysis <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\chi^{2}= 62.32, \mathrm{p}=0.000)$</tex>. When it attempted predicting the risk of diabetic nephropathy, AdaBoost performed exceptionally well overall. Conclusion: AdaBoost and other ML algorithms are particularly effective at differentiating between nondiabetic renal disease and diabetic nephropathy. The creation of a decision-support system to help with the early detection and treatment of diabetic nephropathy is supported by these results.

title: Integrating Machine Learning AI with Green Supply Chain Practices for Enhanced Sustainable Performance
authors: S. Aneesha Bhanu, M. Krithika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Implementing artificial intelligence based on machine learning in the context of supply chain management has become one of the key factors in the development of sustainable performance. In this study, the authors investigate the connection between AIenabled practices in the supply chain and sustainability performance with a specific focus on the mediating role of green supply chain initiatives. Using a quantitative research approach, the authors collected the data on 251 professionals who represented manufacturing and service sectors. The main machine learning applications that have been discussed are predictive analytics, smart decisionmaking systems, demand forecasting, and process optimization. Findings of the rho correlation analysis of Spearman show that supply chain performance has a strong positive correlation with digital transformation and the coefficient of the correlation is 0.642 at statistically significant level. The results confirm that the artificial intelligence has a significant effect on sustainable performance during both direct and indirect influence, based on the environmentally conscious supply chain practices. On the whole, the investigation emphasizes the fact that the implementation of the machine learning technologies and the application of the eco-friendly supply chain strategies enables to improve the functioning of the organizations, reduce the number of waste, optimize the use of resources, and reinforce organizational sustainability.

title: Explainable Comparative Analysis of Tree-Based Ensemble Learning Models for MASLD Screening
authors: Kokhulash MS, Varalakshmi P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD) is a growing global health concern, yet its asymptomatic progression makes early, non-invasive screening critical for intervention. This study utilized a dataset of 9,023 participants from the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) 2009-2016. We systematically compared four tree-based ensemble methodsRandom Forest, XGBoost, CatBoost, and LightGBM-using model-specific feature selection and robust cross-validation. Performance was evaluated on a held-out test set using ROC-AUC, F1score, precision, recall, and specificity. All gradient boosting models demonstrated exceptional discriminatory power (ROC-AUC > 0.995). The optimized LightGBM model yielded the best and most balanced performance, achieving the highest F1-score of 0.8841 and precision of 0.8306, with a ROC-AUC of 0.9970 and 0.9450 recall. Model interpretability via SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) confirmed the LightGBM model's predictions were driven by clinically- relevant factors, identifying Ethnicity, GGT, and HOMA IR as the top predictors. This work demonstrates that an optimized LightGBM model, trained on a parsimonious set of non-invasive features, can serve as a highly accurate, robust, and interpretable tool for large-scale MASLD screening.

title: Artificial Intelligence—Driven Agricultural Intelligence Systems for Enhancing Crop Productivity and Sustainable Farm Management
authors: Sangeetha T, Mahendran R, M Soniya, Surendran R, Gedela Kalyani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: Fast population increase, climate change and depletion of resources are some reasons due to which there is an acceleration in demand for technology adoption for managing agricultural activities. AI (including IoT, ML, and robots) brings predictive analysis, autonomous decision-making and precision resource management to agriculture. The proposed AIAF (AI-driven Agricultural Intelligence Framework) provides a better framework to integrate multisource data (soil, climate, crop imagery, market data) with ML techniques for predicting the yield of crops, identifying the diseases and optimizing irrigation. Results from the field scale validation showed that the developed system increases water use efficiency by 27%, increases fertilizer use efficiency by 21%, and overall crop productivity is enhanced up to 18% in comparison to traditional practices. The platform also cuts the operational manpower needs by 34% with robotic automation. Results illustrate the importance of AI technologies in moving towards future sustainable agriculture and food security. Besides, this work discusses adoption challenges and provides a roadmap to large-scale deployment.

title: A Design of Cost-Effective Wireless Body Sensor Network with Electrocardiogram Acquisition System Using Hybrid Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Supriya C M, Ahmad Al-Qerem, Chrispin Jiji, Srinivasan Gayathri, Chinthakindi Babaiah, Rajini Parimala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: To create a cost-efficient Wireless Body Sensor Network (WBSN), essential components include low-energy, affordable wearable sensors that monitor basic physiological parameters such as blood pressure heart rate, and temperaturecontinuously. The CWBEHD model is a cost-saving health-monitoring system designed for continuous vital signs monitoring using WBSN. Heart rate, blood pressure is monitored by small wearable sensors linked to an ESP32 microcontroller, sending data through RF signals in a star network. Data after a local preprocessing step are transmitted in a secure way to the ThingsBoard IoT platform, which is the main place for classification, rule-based processing, and dynamic visualization. The model embraces deep learning architectures a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) for rapid classification and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for ECG feature extraction by both synthetic and in vivo data verified by professional ECG monitors. The parameter evaluation is accuracy, heart rate, blood pressure, body temperature, comparative accuracy calculation.

title: Viability Analysis of Clean-in-Place (CIP) System Implementation in Softgel Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Facilities
authors: Karlo Boy Carillo, Ardvin Kester Ong
abstract: Clean-in-Place (CIP) is an automated, recipedriven cleaning process that eliminates equipment disassembly, ensuring repeatability, traceability, and compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) standards. This research investigates the feasibility of implementing CIP systems in softgel pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities, addressing both operational efficiency and economic sustainability. A single-case study design was employed, focusing on the compounding process of a softgel plant. Discrete-event simulation (DES) modeled two scenarios-manual cleaning and CIP-under identical production schedules to assess operational performance and economic outcomes. Results indicate that CIP implementation reduces production time by 39.56 %, water consumption by 77.14 %, detergent use by 72.85 %, and electricity usage by 92.30 %, while eliminating solvent use entirely. Economic analysis yielded a return on investment (ROI) of 11.12 % and a payback period of 8.99 years, suggesting CIP as a long-term strategic investment. These findings underscore CIP's potential to improve throughput, sustainability, and compliance in regulated environments. The study contributes to operational optimization literature by integrating cleaning policies into DES modeling and offers practical insights for pharmaceutical manufacturers seeking efficiency and environmental stewardship. Future research should validate these results through real-world trials and expand scope to include downstream processes and dynamic scheduling.

title: A Comparative Study of Environmental Sensing for Ambient Intelligence with Distributed Sensor Networks and Mobile Robots
authors: Miloš Radović, Ivana Šenk
abstract: Ambient Intelligence (AmI) systems depend on reliable environmental sensing to support comfort, safety, and energy efficiency in indoor spaces. This paper reviews three sensing paradigms for monitoring indoor environmental conditions: distributed wireless sensor networks (WSN), mobile robots employed as sensing platforms, and hybrid architectures that integrate static and mobile sensing. Typical system architectures and operational characteristics are examined, with emphasis on the trade-offs between continuous temporal monitoring at fixed locations and adaptive spatial mapping enabled by mobility. Based on this analysis, a decision framework is introduced that defines practical selection criteria including spatial and temporal requirements, cost (CAPEX/OPEX), reliability, safety, privacy, scalability, and infrastructure constraints, and illustrates how these criteria guide the choice between WSN-based, robot-based, and hybrid solutions. Finally, directions for future research are outlined, focusing on quantitative validation and software-supported methods for automated sensing architecture selection in AmI systems.

title: Stage-Aware Event-Conditioned Transformers for Real-Time Odor Recognition with Electronic Noses
authors: Aayush Bhansali, Aditya Nigam
abstract: Electronic noses (e-noses) that infer odor identity from gas-sensor arrays could enable low-cost monitoring in food safety, healthcare, and environmental sensing. Yet most existing systems ignore the strongly phased Baseline-ExposurePurge (BEP) dynamics and the baseline drift that dominate real deployments, making simple magnitude-based features brittle and limiting the gains from off-the-shelf sequence models. In this work, we develop a compact 13-channel MEMS electronic nose and introduce MEMS-Odor-32, a 32-odor dataset collected over five days (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 6 0}$</tex> trials, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{2 5 M}$</tex> data points), capturing realistic inter-day variability in temperature, humidity, and sensor drift. Building on this hardware platform and open dataset, we present SmellFormer, a Stage-Aware Event-Conditioned Transformer that integrates dual-path baseline and delta encodings, event-relative temporal features, FiLM-style baseline conditioning, phasedependent gating, and kinetic summary tokens. On MEMS-Odor32, SmellFormer reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 0}$</tex> % Top-1 accuracy and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 6 9 8}$</tex> macroF1, outperforming MLP, CNN, LSTM, and vanilla transformer baselines. Ablation experiments show that phase-aware gating and kinetic tokens are particularly important for robustness. Beyond raw accuracy, SmellFormer yields chemically meaningful odor embeddings that cluster according to VOC families, suggesting a useful representation for downstream retrieval and few-shot learning.

title: Evaluating Cybersecurity Risk via Psychometric Analysis and Machine Learning
authors: Adrian Diac, Alin Stefanescu
publish: 2025 27th International Symposium on Symbolic and Numeric Algorithms for Scientific Computing (SYNASC)
abstract: In today's landscape, where cyber threats evolve on all fronts, the human factor remains a key enabler of their success. This paper presents a solution for psychological assessment and cybersecurity risk management by profiling individuals within an organization and estimating the likelihood that a phishing, ransomware, or social-engineering attack against them will succeed and propagate through the structure. The system's architecture is built as a set of microservices with distinct roles. The first component processes incoming text and extracts five relevant psychometric dimensions: awareness, conscientiousness, stress, neuroticism, and risk tolerance, using a BERT-based fine-tuned model and heuristic relabeling rules. The second component functions like an HR department, storing and connecting users and organizational entities in a relational database. The third one calculates each person's composite risk via various techniques, uncovers vulnerabilities in a simulated cyberattack scenario, and parses BPMN diagrams for interoperability. All services are exposed through a graphical interface designed to deliver high-performance support for organizational cybersecurity risk management, featuring real-time simulations, scenario customization, and an intuitive user experience. Experimental evaluations demonstrate superior performance in detecting attack susceptibility and provide a replicable framework for integrating the human factor into modern cybersecurity risk strategies.

title: The Influence of Speed on the Generation of Airborne Wear Particles During Braking
authors: Wojciech Tarasiuk, Michał Kalinowski, Mariusz Liszewski, Tomasz Węgrzyn, Bozena Szczucka-Lasota, Marek Ożarowski
abstract: This study investigated the effect of braking speed on the intensity of wear particle generation in the braking system. The study used a TRW GDB1621DTE brake pad on a 60-millimeter-diameter steel disc to simulate a vehicle brake disc. Measurements were taken with an Rtec MFT-5000 tribometer in a chamber that isolated the friction zone from external conditions. The chemical composition of the sample was analyzed, and the roughness of the counter-sample (steel disc) was measured. The study examined how three speed levels (5, 7, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>) affected the coefficient of friction, temperature near the contact point, and number of wear particles generated under a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ N}$</tex> load, equivalent to moderately intense passenger car breaking. The results showed an increasing trend in particle emission with increasing speed. These results have significant implications for evaluating the durability and environmental impact of friction materials used in automotive braking systems, as well as for developing methods to minimize wear particle emissions into the atmosphere.

title: Digital Transformation of Audit Practices Through AI and Information Technology: A Case Study of Automated Compliance Checking in Cloud-Based ERP Systems
authors: Zhang Jinyi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Innovative Computing, Intelligent Communication and Smart Electrical Systems (ICSES)
abstract: It has become a reality that a significant amount of cloud-based Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems is causing the digitization of the audit procedures. The traditional auditing processes are extremely reliant on a manual audit and set rules that fail to govern the massive and dynamic ERP data. The paper provides a very simple hybrid audit automation system, which entails Rule-Based Compliance Checking and Random Forest (RB-RF) in carrying out automated compliance audit in ERP systems in the cloud. The proposed strategy will employ preset audit guidelines founded on regulatory and organizational policy that will be utilized to filter blatant cases of meeting policy. Subsequently, the residual transactional and access log data is subsequently processed with the assistance of a Random Forest machine learning model that will be used to detect undisclosed non-compliant patterns and anomalies. The rule-based layer, which can be interpreted and regulated, and the object detecting accuracy of the Random Forest model are optimized to acquire complex relations between past audit data. Key aspects such as frequency of transaction, authorization and authorization mismatch and access irregularities are used to model training. To ensure continued audit and scalable compliance auditing, the hybrid framework will be implemented in a cloud ERP background. The results of the case study demonstrate that the proposed RB-RF approach helps to reduce false positives, improve the precision of the compliance detection, and reduce the number of efforts, which must be employed in the manual auditing by far compared to the application of standalone rules-based methods. The experiment confirms the fact that simple hybrid approach can be applied to make audits more efficient and reliable in cloud ERP systems in the recent times.

title: A Fusion-Based Random Forest-LSTM Model for Continuous Digital Health Monitoring via Smartwatch Wearing Prediction
authors: Emine Nur Nacar, Melda Kevser Akgün, Nurullah Güleç
